Bring the bar back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the front knee aligned with the toes.

ff_single_arm_barbell_overhead_bulgarian.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load overhead. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. In this barbell-overhead-bulgarian-split-squat version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_bodyweight_full_planche_push_up.description: Place the hands on the floor and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. In this bodyweight-full-planche-push-up version, use only the range you can control.

ff_bodyweight_straddle_planche_push_up.description: Place the hands on the floor and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs straddled to manage balance. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_ring_grip_full_planche_push_up.description: Set the rings low and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_bodyweight_two_finger_full_planche_pu.description: Place the hands on the floor and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. In this bodyweight-two-finger-full-planche-push-up version, use only the range you can control.

ff_ring_grip_straddle_planche_push_up.description: Set the rings low and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs straddled to manage balance. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_bodyweight_push_up_to_alternating_kic.description: Start in a strong plank with hands under or slightly wider than the shoulders. Do one push-up, then rotate and thread one leg under the body before returning to plank. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bodyweight_standing_walkout_push_up.description: Start in a strong plank with hands under or slightly wider than the shoulders. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep. In this bodyweight-standing-walkout-push-up version, use only the range you can control.

ff_ring_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with a comfortable overhand or neutral grip, arms long, and shoulders active. Pull the chest toward the rings by driving the elbows down, then lower until the arms are long again. Keep the body quiet and avoid kicking or shrugging through the top.

ff_single_arm_plate_windmill.description: Stand with feet wide and the loaded arm reaching overhead or ready to reach overhead. Push the hips back and reach the free hand toward the inside of the leg as the loaded shoulder stays stacked. Stand by driving the hips through. In this single-arm-plate-windmill version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_single_arm_plate_turkish_get_up.description: Lie on your back with the working-side knee bent and hold the plate locked over the shoulder. Roll to the elbow, post to the hand, sweep the leg under, and stand while watching the weight. Reverse each step slowly, keeping the arm vertical instead of letting the load drift behind you.

ff_bodyweight_pike_push_up.description: Start in a strong plank with hands under or slightly wider than the shoulders. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep. In this bodyweight-pike-push-up version, use only the range you can control.

ff_parallette_feet_elevated_pike_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep. In this parallette-feet-elevated-pike-push-up version, press evenly through both handles.

ff_barbell_sots_press.description: Set the bar or kettlebells in the front rack and descend into a stable squat. While staying in the bottom position, press the load overhead, then lower it back to the rack before standing or repeating. Keep the chest tall and use a lighter load if the press makes the heels lift.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_snatch.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch to a bottoms-up overhead catch only with a load you can balance. In this double-kettlebell-bottoms-up-snatch version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_bot.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch to a bottoms-up overhead catch only with a load you can balance. In this arm-kettlebell-bottoms-up-snatch version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_aro.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Guide the bell around the body and catch it high near the shoulder before passing or lowering it. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back. In this around-the-world-high-catch version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_tal.description: Kneel tall with both knees on the floor and glutes squeezed. Guide the bell around the body and catch it high near the shoulder before passing or lowering it. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_around_the_worl.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Pass the bell smoothly around the waist or head from hand to hand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back. In this single-arm-kettlebell-around-the-world version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_z_press.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended and the load at shoulder height. Brace tall, press the weight overhead or along the landmine path, then lower to the shoulder under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to turn it into an incline press. In this single-arm-dumbbell-z-press version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_parallette_feet_elevated_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep. In this parallette-feet-elevated-push-up version, press evenly through both handles.

ff_stability_ball_feet_elevated_diamond_.description: Place the feet on the stability ball and hands on the floor in a plank. Keep the hands close and bend the elbows back along the ribs as you lower. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bodyweight_tricep_extension.description: Start in a plank with hands on the floor or an elevated edge slightly in front of the shoulders. Bend only the elbows to lower the forehead or forearms toward the hands, then press the arms straight again. Keep the ribs tucked and elbows pointing forward instead of letting the hips sag.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_aro.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Pass the bell smoothly around the waist or head from hand to hand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back. In this arm-kettlebell-around-the-world version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_tall_kneeling_a.description: Kneel tall with both knees on the floor and glutes squeezed. Pass the bell smoothly around the waist or head from hand to hand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back. In this tall-kneeling-around-the-world version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_alternating_single_arm_tall_kneeling_.description: Kneel tall with both knees on the floor and glutes squeezed. Pass the bell smoothly around the waist or head from hand to hand. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back. In this tall-kneeling-around-the-world version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_waiter_clean.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter position near the shoulder. In this single-arm-kettlebell-waiter-clean version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_wai.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter position near the shoulder. In this alternating-single-arm-kettlebell-waiter-clean version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_double_kettlebell_waiter_clean.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter position near the shoulder. In this double-kettlebell-waiter-clean version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_waiter_clean_to_ove.description: Hinge to grip the kettlebell just in front of you, then pull it close as the hips drive. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter position near the shoulder. From there, press overhead and lower back to the rack before the next rep. In this double-kettlebell-waiter-clean-to-overhead-press version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_tactical_waiter.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter position near the shoulder. In this single-arm-kettlebell-tactical-waiter-clean version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_double_kettlebell_waiter_clean_to_alt.description: Hinge to grip the kettlebell just in front of you, then pull it close as the hips drive. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter position near the shoulder. From there, press overhead and lower back to the rack before the next rep. In this clean-to-alternating-overhead-press version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_waiter_snatch.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter catch overhead. In this single-arm-kettlebell-waiter-snatch version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_wai.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter catch overhead. In this alternating-single-arm-kettlebell-waiter-snatch version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_barbell_overhead_pistol_squat.description: Stand on one leg with the load held overhead and reach the free leg forward for a pistol or back for a pistol squat. Bend the standing knee and sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts or the torso collapses. In this barbell-overhead-pistol-squat version, keep the bar centered over the midfoot.

ff_barbell_seated_wrist_curl.description: Sit with forearms supported on the thighs or a bench and hold the bar with palms up. Let the wrists extend slightly, then curl the knuckles toward the forearms. Keep the forearms planted and avoid turning the rep into an elbow curl.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_hollow_body_chest.description: Lie on your back with one dumbbell at chest height and the opposite arm reaching out for balance. Lift the shoulders and legs into a hollow hold, then press the dumbbell toward the ceiling and lower it back to the chest. Keep the low back pressed into the floor and bend the knees if the ribs flare.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_tactical_waiter.description: Start with one kettlebell between the feet and prepare to switch hands at the backswing. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter catch overhead. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_single_arm_barbell_overhead_knee_over.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted. Hold the load overhead. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. In this knee-over-toe-split-squat version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_single_arm_barbell_knee_over_toe_spli.description: Set a split stance and hold one end or one side of the bar in the rack position. Let the front knee travel forward as far as you can control, then drive up and press overhead in one motion. Keep the torso tall and do not let the load pull you toward the working side.

ff_barbell_knee_over_toe_split_squat_thr.description: Set a split stance with the bar in the front rack and the front foot planted. Lower with the front knee traveling forward under control, then stand and press the bar overhead without pausing. Return the bar to the rack and avoid collapsing the front knee inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_waiter_snatch.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter catch overhead. In this double-kettlebell-waiter-snatch version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_waiter_clean_to_thr.description: Hinge to grip the kettlebell just in front of you, then pull it close as the hips drive. Guide the bell to a palm-up waiter position near the shoulder. From the rack, squat and drive straight into the overhead press. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_kettlebell_start_stop_swing.description: Stand with the kettlebell just in front of the feet and hinge to grip it. Hike the bell back with both hands, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. In this kettlebell-start-stop-swing version, keep the variation controlled through the finish.

ff_double_kettlebell_dead_clean.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor between or just inside the feet. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Catch the bell softly in the rack. In this double-kettlebell-dead-clean version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_single_arm_barbell_bulgarian_split_sq.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and hold the bar on the working side in a stable rack position. Lower into the Bulgarian split squat, then press the bar overhead as the front leg drives you back up. Keep the hips square and avoid leaning away from the bar during the press.

ff_single_arm_barbell_windmill.description: Stand with feet wide and the loaded arm reaching overhead or ready to reach overhead. Push the hips back and reach the free hand toward the inside of the leg as the loaded shoulder stays stacked. Stand by driving the hips through. In this single-arm-barbell-windmill version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_bodyweight_windmill.description: Stand with feet wide and the loaded arm reaching overhead or ready to reach overhead. Push the hips back and reach the free hand toward the inside of the leg as the loaded shoulder stays stacked. Stand by driving the hips through. In this bodyweight-windmill version, use only the range you can control.

ff_superband_isometric_split_squat_pallo.description: Anchor the superband at chest height and step sideways into a split squat stance. Hold the band at the sternum, lower into the split squat, and press the hands straight forward without rotating. Keep the front knee aligned with the toes and resist the band pulling the ribs open.

ff_single_arm_barbell_turkish_get_up.description: Lie on your back with the working-side knee bent and hold the load locked over the shoulder. Roll to the elbow, post to the hand, sweep the leg under, and stand while watching the weight. Reverse each step slowly, keeping the arm vertical instead of letting the load drift behind you.

ff_bodyweight_plate_plyometric_push_up.description: Start in a strong plank with hands under or slightly wider than the shoulders. Lower into a push-up, then press explosively so the hands leave the plate or floor briefly. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_single_leg_contra.description: Hinge onto one leg with the free leg reaching back and the weight hanging below the shoulder. Row toward the hip while keeping the hips square to the floor, then lower the weight without standing up. Use a light load if balance makes the torso twist. In this leg-contralateral-bent-over-row version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_double_dumbbell_single_leg_bent_over_.description: Hinge onto one leg with the free leg reaching back and the weight hanging below the shoulder. Row toward the hip while keeping the hips square to the floor, then lower the weight without standing up. Use a light load if balance makes the torso twist. In this double-dumbbell-single-leg-bent-over-row version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_thru.description: Stand with the load in the rack position and feet about shoulder width. Squat down, then drive up and continue directly into an overhead press. Lower the load back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the elbows from dropping during the dip. In this single-arm-kettlebell-bottoms-up-thruster version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Clean both kettlebells bottoms-up to the rack and stand with feet about shoulder width. Squat down, then drive up and press one bell overhead while the other stays racked. Lower under control and alternate the pressing side each rep without letting either bell tip.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_dead_clean.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor between or just inside the feet. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Catch the bell softly in the rack. In this single-arm-kettlebell-dead-clean version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_dea.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor between or just inside the feet. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Catch the bell softly in the rack. In this alternating-single-arm-kettlebell-dead-clean version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_bird_dog_row.description: Set one hand and the opposite knee on a bench, then extend the free leg behind you and hold a dumbbell in the free hand. Row the dumbbell toward the hip while keeping the leg and torso still. Lower slowly and avoid opening the hips as the weight comes up.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_half.description: Set up half kneeling with the kettlebell bottoms-up overhead and the loaded arm vertical. Hinge the hips back and reach the free hand toward the floor inside the front leg, then return by driving the hips under you. Crush the handle so the bell stays upright, and avoid rounding forward or letting the shoulder drift.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_hal.description: Set up in a half-kneeling stance with the front foot flat and back knee down. Guide the bell around the body and catch it high near the shoulder before passing or lowering it. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back.

ff_double_dumbbell_renegade_row.description: Set two dumbbells under the shoulders and hold a wide-foot plank. Row one dumbbell toward the hip, lower it quietly, then row the other side. Keep the hips level and avoid twisting the torso to lift the weight.

ff_single_arm_barbell_overhead_toe_balan.description: Hold a barbell overhead in one hand and rise onto the balls of the feet with the free arm out for balance. Squat with the knees forward while keeping the bar stacked over the shoulder, then stand without dropping the heels abruptly. Keep the wrist, elbow, and shoulder aligned instead of letting the bar drift sideways.

ff_barbell_overhead_toe_balance_squat.description: Press or snatch the barbell overhead, then rise onto the balls of the feet with the bar over the midfoot. Squat with the knees forward and arms locked, then stand while keeping the heels lifted and controlled. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the bar move behind the shoulders as depth increases.

ff_ring_kneeling_rollout.description: Kneel facing the rings with the handles hanging just in front of the shoulders. Hold both rings with straight arms. Brace the ribs down, slide the rings forward, and stop before the low back sags. Pull the rings back under the shoulders without bending at the hips first.

ff_single_arm_ring_kneeling_rollout.description: Kneel facing the rings with the handles hanging just in front of the shoulders. Use one ring and keep the free hand near the chest for balance. Brace the ribs down, slide the rings forward, and stop before the low back sags. Pull the rings back under the shoulders without bending at the hips first.

ff_ring_standing_rollout.description: Stand facing the rings with the handles set high enough to control the first reps. Hold both rings with straight arms. Brace the ribs down, slide the rings forward, and stop before the low back sags. Pull the rings back under the shoulders without bending at the hips first.

ff_single_arm_landmine_shoulder_press.description: Stand facing the landmine with the bar at one shoulder. Brace and press the bar forward and up until the arm is nearly straight, then lower back to the shoulder. Press one side for the planned reps before switching. Keep the torso from rotating or leaning away from the bar.

ff_alternating_single_arm_landmine_shoul.description: Stand facing the landmine and hold the end of the bar at one shoulder. Brace, press the bar forward and up until the arm is nearly straight, then lower and transfer to the other shoulder for the next rep. Keep the torso from rotating or leaning away as the pressing side changes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bent_press.description: Clean the kettlebell to the shoulder and set the feet wider than hip width with toes slightly turned. Hinge and rotate under the bell as you press it overhead, then stand tall with the arm stacked. Keep eyes on the bell and avoid pressing from a rounded or collapsed torso.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_aro.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Guide the bell around the body and catch it high near the shoulder before passing or lowering it. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back. In this around-the-world catch-to-squat version, switch sides only after the rep is stable.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_alternatin.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells locked overhead. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stand tall between sides. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_cossack_.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stand tall between sides. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_low_swit.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_bot.description: Hinge to grip the kettlebell just in front of you, then pull it close as the hips drive. Guide the bell into a bottoms-up rack with the handle vertical. From there, press overhead and lower back to the rack before the next rep. In this alternating bottoms-up clean-press version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clean_to.description: Hinge to grip the kettlebell just in front of you, then pull it close as the hips drive. Guide the bell into a bottoms-up rack with the handle vertical. From there, press overhead and lower back to the rack before the next rep. In this double bottoms-up clean-press version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted. Hold the load overhead. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. In this knee-over-toe-split-squat version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_single_arm_landmine_half_kneeling_con.description: Set up half kneeling with the working-side hand on the end of the bar. Brace and press the bar forward and up until the arm is nearly straight, then lower back to the shoulder. Press one side for the planned reps before switching. Keep the torso from rotating or leaning away from the bar.

ff_bodyweight_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees until the rear knee moves toward the floor, then stand by pushing through the front foot. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid bouncing off the back leg.

ff_plate_otis_up.description: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet planted, and a plate held against the chest. Sit up by curling the ribs toward the pelvis, then press the plate overhead once the torso is upright. Bring the plate back to the chest and roll down under control instead of throwing the weight forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_pistol_squ.description: Stand on one leg with the load held overhead and reach the free leg forward for a pistol or back for a pistol squat. Bend the standing knee and sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts or the torso collapses. In this double-kettlebell-overhead-pistol-squat version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Stand on one leg with the bells balanced bottoms-up and reach the free leg forward for a pistol or back for a pistol squat. Bend the standing knee and sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts or the torso collapses.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Stand on one leg with the load held overhead and reach the free leg forward for a pistol or back for a pistol squat. Bend the standing knee and sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts or the torso collapses. In this bottoms-up-overhead-pistol-squat version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_sots_press.description: Hinge to grip the kettlebell just in front of you, then pull it close as the hips drive. Catch the bell softly in the rack. From there, press overhead and lower back to the rack before the next rep. In this double-kettlebell-clean-to-sots-press version, keep both loads level throughout the rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clean_to.description: Hinge to grip the kettlebell just in front of you, then pull it close as the hips drive. Guide the bell into a bottoms-up rack with the handle vertical. From there, press overhead and lower back to the rack before the next rep. In this bottoms-up clean-to-Sots version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_single_arm_landmine_tall_kneeling_sho.description: Kneel tall facing the landmine with glutes squeezed. Brace and press the bar forward and up until the arm is nearly straight, then lower back to the shoulder. Press one side for the planned reps before switching. Keep the torso from rotating or leaning away from the bar.

ff_single_arm_landmine_z_press.description: Sit on the floor facing the landmine with legs extended or slightly bent. Brace tall, press the weight overhead or along the landmine path, then lower to the shoulder under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to turn it into an incline press.

ff_barbell_z_press.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended and the load at shoulder height. Brace tall, press the weight overhead or along the landmine path, then lower to the shoulder under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to turn it into an incline press. In this barbell-z-press version, keep the bar centered over the midfoot.

ff_single_arm_suspension_bicep_curl.description: Hold one suspension handle and lean back with the body in a straight line. Curl the handle toward the forehead by bending the elbow, then lower until the arm is straight. Keep the elbow high and avoid pulling the body up by shrugging the shoulder.

ff_suspension_atomic_push_up.description: Start in a strong plank with hands under or slightly wider than the shoulders. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep. In this suspension-atomic-push-up version, keep the variation controlled through the finish.

ff_parallette_feet_elevated_close_grip_p.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Keep the hands close and bend the elbows back along the ribs as you lower. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_thruster.description: Hinge to grip the kettlebell just in front of you, then pull it close as the hips drive. Catch the bell softly in the rack. From the rack, squat and drive straight into the overhead press. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_bodyweight_archer_push_up.description: Start in a strong plank with hands under or slightly wider than the shoulders. Lower toward one hand while the other arm reaches straighter to the side, then press back and switch sides. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_parallette_archer_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower toward one hand while the other arm reaches straighter to the side, then press back and switch sides. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_single_arm_suspension_push_up.description: Start in a strong plank with hands under or slightly wider than the shoulders. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep. In this single-arm-suspension-push-up version, keep the torso from twisting toward the loaded arm.

ff_ring_skin_the_cat.description: Hang from the rings with straight arms and a tight hollow body. Tuck the knees, roll the hips through the arms into a German hang, then reverse the path back to the start. Move slowly and keep the shoulders active instead of dropping into the straps.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load overhead. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. In this up-overhead-bulgarian-split-squat version, keep the bell vertical and stop before the grip collapses.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_contra.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down, then hold the load at the side. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the load at your side without leaning toward it.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_ipsila.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down, then hold the load at the side. Hold the load on the same side as the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the load at your side without leaning toward it.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load in the front rack. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the handle vertical and use a light load so the bell does not tip.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_bulgarian_.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load at the side. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the load at your side without leaning toward it.

ff_double_kettlebell_bulgarian_split_squ.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and clean both kettlebells to the front rack. Lower into the Bulgarian split squat, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press. Lower the bells back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the front foot rooted.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_bulgaria.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and balance both kettlebells bottoms-up in the rack. Lower into the Bulgarian split squat, then stand and press overhead only as far as the bells stay vertical. Use a light load and reset if the handles tip or the front knee caves inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Place the rear foot on a bench, step the front foot forward, and hold the load in the front rack. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the handle vertical and use a light load so the bell does not tip.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Place the rear foot on a bench, step the front foot forward, and hold the load in the front rack. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the elbows forward and the load close to the ribs.

ff_ring_feet_elevated_row.description: Place the feet on a box or bench and lean back under the rings. Brace the body in one line from shoulders to heels. Pull both rings toward the lower ribs. Keep the hips from sagging and lower with the same straight body line.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Place the rear foot on a bench, step the front foot forward, and hold the load in the front rack. Hold the load on the same side as the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the handle vertical and use a light load so the bell does not tip.

ff_ring_feet_elevated_bulgarian_row.description: Place the feet on a box or bench and lean back under the rings. Brace the body in one line from shoulders to heels. Let one arm assist less so the working side performs most of the pull. Keep the hips from sagging and lower with the same straight body line.

ff_ring_feet_elevated_archer_row.description: Place the feet on a box or bench and lean back under the rings. Brace the body in one line from shoulders to heels. Pull mostly toward one ring while the other arm reaches straighter, then switch sides. Keep the hips from sagging and lower with the same straight body line.

ff_ring_push_up.description: Hold the rings under the shoulders in a plank with straps vertical. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep. In this ring-push-up version, control ring wobble before the next rep.

ff_ring_chest_fly.description: Set the rings low and lean forward with arms straight but not locked. Open the arms only as far as the shoulders stay controlled, then squeeze the rings together in front of the chest. Keep a plank line and avoid letting the rings drift behind the shoulders.

ff_double_kettlebell_feet_elevated_push_.description: Place the feet on a bench or box and set the hands under the shoulders. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_half.description: Set up half kneeling with the front foot down and the bottoms-up kettlebell at the opposite shoulder. Brace the ribs down, press the bell overhead until the arm stacks over the shoulder, then lower to the rack. Keep the handle crushed and avoid leaning back or twisting toward the loaded side.

ff_parallette_alternating_shoulder_tap_p.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Do one push-up, then tap the opposite shoulder without letting the hips rotate. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_parallette_dive_bomber_push_up.description: Start in a pike with hips high, hands about shoulder width, and heels reaching back. Bend the elbows and sweep the chest forward close to the floor, then press into the front position before reversing to the pike. Keep the motion smooth and avoid snapping the low back as the hips move down. In this parallette-dive-bomber-push-up version, press evenly through both handles.

ff_barbell_front_rack_bulgarian_split_sq.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load in the front rack. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. In this barbell-front-rack-bulgarian-split-squat version, keep the bar centered over the midfoot.

ff_barbell_back_rack_bulgarian_split_squ.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set a barbell across the upper back before stepping the front foot forward. Bend both knees to lower straight down, then stand by driving through the front foot. Keep the torso braced and the front knee tracking over the toes instead of shifting the weight onto the bench.

ff_single_arm_suspension_contralateral_p.description: Stand on one leg and hold one suspension handle with the hand opposite the working leg. Reach the free leg forward as you sit into the pistol, then use just enough strap tension to stand through the planted foot. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pulling yourself up with the arm before the leg drives.

ff_barbell_front_rack_alternating_curtsy.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_bodyweight_dragon_flag.description: Lie on a bench or floor and grip a secure anchor behind the head. Roll onto the upper back, extend the body into one straight line, and lower slowly until just above the bench or floor. Keep the glutes tight and bend the knees if the low back starts to arch.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_ipsi.description: Place the rear foot on a bench, step the front foot forward, and hold the load in the front rack. Hold the load on the same side as the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the elbows forward and the load close to the ribs.

ff_bodyweight_dive_bomber_push_up.description: Start in a pike with hips high, hands about shoulder width, and heels reaching back. Bend the elbows and sweep the chest forward close to the floor, then press into the front position before reversing to the pike. Keep the motion smooth and avoid snapping the low back as the hips move down. In this bodyweight-dive-bomber-push-up version, use only the range you can control.

ff_barbell_staggered_stance_push_press.description: Set a staggered stance with the bar in the front rack and most of your weight balanced through both feet. Dip straight down, drive through the legs, and press the bar overhead as the knees and hips extend. Lower to the rack under control, keeping the torso vertical instead of lunging forward.

ff_parallette_full_planche.description: Set parallettes shoulder width and grip them with straight elbows. Lean the shoulders far past the hands, lift both straight legs together, and squeeze the body into one long line. Push the bars away and stop before the hips sag or the elbows bend.

ff_double_dumbbell_renegade_row_to_push_.description: Place two dumbbells under the shoulders and step into a wide-foot plank. Row one dumbbell to the hip, row the other, then perform a push-up between the handles. Keep the hips square and avoid twisting to lift the weights.

ff_ring_inverted_iron_cross.description: Set the rings low and start in an inverted hang with the body tight. Slowly open the arms out to a cross shape while keeping the shoulders active and legs controlled. Pull back to center before the elbows bend or the rings drift unevenly.

ff_barbell_reverse_grip_front_raise.description: Stand tall holding a light barbell with palms up and hands about shoulder width. Raise the straight arms to shoulder height, pause, then lower without swinging. Keep wrists neutral and ribs down instead of leaning back.

ff_ring_tuck_planche.description: Set low rings and press into support with straight elbows. Lean forward, tuck both knees toward the chest, and float the feet behind the hands. Keep the rings close and shoulder blades spread instead of letting the elbows soften.

ff_barbell_seated_reverse_grip_wrist_cur.description: Sit with forearms on the thighs and hold a barbell palms down with wrists just past the knees. Let the knuckles drop, then extend the wrists to lift the back of the hands. Keep forearms planted and avoid curling with the elbows.

ff_ring_full_planche.description: Set the rings low and begin in a straight-arm support. Lean the shoulders forward, extend both legs together behind you, and hold the body parallel to the floor. Press the rings down and avoid flaring them wide as fatigue builds.

ff_alternating_single_arm_parallette_pus.description: Set one hand on a parallette and the other hand on the floor in a plank. Lower into a push-up, press up, then switch which hand uses the parallette on the next rep. Keep the chest square and avoid dropping the hip on the raised-hand side.

ff_ring_scapular_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with arms straight and feet off the floor or lightly assisted. Pull the shoulder blades down to lift the body a few inches, then return to a long hang. Keep elbows locked and avoid turning it into a chin-up.

ff_ring_grip_full_planche.description: Grip low rings firmly and press into a straight-arm support. Lean until shoulders pass the hands, extend the legs together, and hold the full planche line. Keep the rings quiet and avoid bending the elbows to buy more time.

ff_bodyweight_full_planche.description: Set up for a bodyweight full planche with hands on the floor just outside shoulder width. Lean forward until the feet float, extend both legs together, and keep the body rigid. Push through the floor and exit before the hips drop or wrists collapse.

ff_cable_quadruped_hip_abduction.description: Attach a low cable to one ankle and set up on hands and knees. Keep the knee bent as you move the working thigh out to the side, then return slowly under tension. Keep the pelvis level and avoid rolling the trunk open.

ff_barbell_reverse_grip_bench_press.description: Lie on a bench with feet planted and grip the bar palms facing you. Lower the bar toward the lower chest with elbows tucked, then press to straight arms. Keep wrists stacked and use a grip you can control without the bar rolling.

ff_double_kettlebell_turkish_get_up.description: Lie on your back with two kettlebells pressed above the shoulders and one knee bent. Roll to the elbow, post to the hand, sweep the leg under, and stand with both bells vertical. Reverse the path slowly and avoid letting either bell drift behind you.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_rotational_over.description: Rack one kettlebell at the shoulder and stand with feet ready to pivot. Rotate through the hips and ribs as you press the bell overhead, then return to the rack. Keep the wrist straight and avoid twisting through the low back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_z_press.description: Sit on the floor with legs straight and one kettlebell racked at the shoulder. Brace tall, press the bell overhead, then lower to the rack with control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the press.

ff_parallette_straddle_planche.description: Grip parallettes and lean the shoulders forward with elbows locked. Lift the feet and spread the legs into a straddle while keeping the hips level. Push the bars away and avoid letting one leg drop lower than the other.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_sots_press.description: Sit into the bottom of a squat with one kettlebell racked at the shoulder. Stay tall and press the bell overhead, then lower before standing or switching sides. Keep the heel down and avoid tipping the chest forward.

ff_ring_straddle_planche.description: Press into low rings with straight elbows and shoulders protracted. Lean forward, lift the feet, and open the legs into a straddle planche. Keep the rings under the shoulders and avoid shaking them outward for balance.

ff_double_kettlebell_renegade_row.description: Set two kettlebells under the shoulders and take a wide plank stance. Row one bell toward the hip, lower it carefully, then row the other side. Keep the handles vertical and avoid rocking from foot to foot.

ff_barbell_turkish_sit_up.description: Lie on your back holding a barbell locked out above the chest with hands even. Sit up to a tall torso while keeping the bar stacked over the shoulders, then lower with control. Keep eyes forward to the bar and avoid letting one end dip.

ff_plate_turkish_sit_up.description: Lie on your back holding a plate above the chest with arms straight. Roll up to a tall seated position, keeping the plate stacked over the shoulders, then lower slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid throwing the plate forward for momentum.

ff_double_kettlebell_renegade_row_to_pus.description: Place two kettlebells under the shoulders and step into a wide plank. Row each kettlebell to the hip, then perform a push-up with hands on the handles. Brace before each pull and avoid letting the bells tip inward.

ff_single_arm_barbell_turkish_sit_up.description: Lie on your back holding one end of a barbell vertical above the shoulder. Sit up toward a tall torso while the working arm stays stacked, then lower slowly. Keep the bar balanced and avoid letting the sleeve drift behind the shoulder.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_romanian_deadlift.description: Stand holding one dumbbell between both palms at the front of the thighs. Hinge the hips back with soft knees until the hamstrings load, then stand by driving the hips forward. Keep the dumbbell close and avoid rounding to reach lower.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_c.description: Set a half-kneeling stance and rack the kettlebell on the side opposite the front leg. Press overhead until the arm stacks over the shoulder, then lower to the rack. Squeeze the back glute and avoid leaning toward the front leg.

ff_double_kettlebell_knee_over_toe_split.description: Hold two kettlebells in the front rack and set a split stance. Let the front knee travel over the toes as you lower, then drive up and press both bells overhead. Keep the front heel heavy and avoid pausing loose between squat and press.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_knee_ove.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the rack and set a split stance. Lower with the front knee tracking past the toes, then stand and press the bells overhead. Keep wrists vertical and shorten the range before the bells tip.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_z_pr.description: Sit tall with legs straight and one kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Press the handle upward until the arm is vertical, then lower slowly. Keep the forearm stacked and avoid leaning back as the bell wobbles.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_z_press.description: Sit on the floor with legs straight and two kettlebells bottoms-up in the rack. Press both bells overhead, then lower under control. Keep the handles vertical and avoid letting the ribs flare to save the rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_sots_press.description: Hold two kettlebells in the rack and sit into the bottom of a squat. Press both bells overhead while staying low, then return them to the rack. Keep the heels down and avoid collapsing the chest to create room.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_sots_pr.description: Sit in the bottom of a squat with two kettlebells racked. Press one bell overhead, lower it, then press the other while staying tall. Keep the nonpressing elbow tight and avoid shifting side to side.

ff_double_kettlebell_seesaw_sots_press.description: Settle into the bottom of a squat with two kettlebells at the shoulders. Press one bell up as the other lowers, alternating in a seesaw rhythm. Keep both heels grounded and avoid twisting the torso with each press.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_hollow_body_flutt.description: Lie on your back and squeeze one dumbbell between both palms over the chest. Lift shoulders and legs into a hollow hold, then make small flutter kicks. Press the low back down and raise the legs if the back arches.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seesaw_o.description: Stand with two kettlebells bottoms-up in the rack. Press one bell overhead while the other stays racked, then switch in a controlled seesaw. Keep wrists vertical and avoid side-bending under the moving bell.

ff_parallette_crow_pose.description: Set parallettes shoulder width and squat between them. Bend the elbows slightly, place the knees high on the upper arms, and shift forward until the feet float. Press evenly through both handles and avoid collapsing onto the wrists.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seesaw_z.description: Sit with legs straight and two kettlebells bottoms-up at the shoulders. Press one bell overhead as the other stays racked, then alternate smoothly. Keep the spine tall and avoid leaning back when the bell wobbles.

ff_suspension_single_leg_standing_bent_k.description: Hold suspension handles, stand on one softly bent leg, and lean back with arms straight. Open the arms into a reverse fly while keeping the lifted knee bent, then return slowly. Keep hips level and avoid shrugging the shoulders.

ff_kettlebell_bottoms_up_horn_grip_goble.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and hold a kettlebell bottoms-up by the horns at the chest. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the bell vertical and avoid letting the front knee cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_snatch_to_overhead_.description: Start with two kettlebells between the feet and hinge to grip them. Drive through the hips to snatch both bells overhead, stabilize, then squat with arms locked out. Keep the bells stacked and avoid rushing the squat before balance is set.

ff_suspension_single_leg_standing_bent_k.description: Hold suspension handles and balance on one bent leg while leaning back. Raise the arms into a Y shape until the body comes forward, then lower with control. Keep the lifted knee quiet and avoid arching the ribs.

Hold suspension handles and stand on one softly bent leg facing the anchor. Pull the handles toward the face with elbows high, then extend the arms slowly. Keep the stance knee steady and avoid turning it into a low row.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_d.description: Set half kneeling with one kettlebell on the floor beside the front foot. Hinge from the hips, clean the bell from dead stop to rack, then return it to the floor. Keep the elbow close and avoid banging the forearm.

ff_barbell_overhead_march.description: Press or snatch a barbell to a stable overhead lockout. March in place with slow knee lifts while the bar stays over the midfoot. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the bar drift forward as each knee rises.

ff_single_arm_barbell_overhead_march.description: Hold one end or offset barbell overhead with the working arm locked out. March slowly, lifting one knee at a time while keeping the bar stacked over the shoulder. Keep the free arm quiet and avoid leaning away from the bar.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_c.description: Set half kneeling with the kettlebell just outside the front foot. Hike or pull the bell close and clean it to the rack without standing up. Keep the torso tall and avoid casting the bell in a wide arc.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_s.description: Start half kneeling with one kettlebell on the floor near the front foot. Drive from the hips and guide the bell close into an overhead lockout. Punch through softly and avoid letting the bell flop onto the wrist.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_alternat.description: Clean two kettlebells to the front rack and stand tall. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, return to center, then alternate sides. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the front knee dive inward.

ff_slider_lateral_lunge.description: Place one foot on a slider and stand tall with the other foot planted. Slide the moving leg out to the side as the planted knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep the planted heel down and avoid twisting toward the slider.

ff_kettlebell_bottoms_up_horn_grip_toe_b.description: Hold a kettlebell bottoms-up by the horns at chest height and rise onto the balls of the feet. Squat as low as balance allows, then stand while keeping the heels lifted. Keep the bell vertical and avoid letting the knees wobble inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_overh.description: Start with two kettlebells on the floor between the feet. Clean them from dead stop to the rack, brace, then press both bells overhead. Reset the rack before pressing and avoid pulling the clean with only the arms.

ff_double_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_thrus.description: Start with two kettlebells on the floor and clean them to the front rack. Squat down, drive up hard, and continue into an overhead press. Keep elbows connected in the rack and avoid letting the bells crash onto the forearms.

ff_suspension_single_leg_standing_bent_k.description: Balance on one bent leg holding suspension handles and lean back. Pull the handles to the face, rotate the hands upward, then press into a Y overhead shape. Keep the stance knee steady and avoid shrugging during the press.

Hold suspension handles and balance on one softly bent leg. Pull elbows high, rotate the hands upward, then press to a controlled overhead angle. Keep the torso tall and avoid yanking through the shoulder turn.

ff_double_kettlebell_single_leg_standing.description: Stand on one softly bent leg with two kettlebells racked at the shoulders. Press both bells overhead, then lower without setting the free foot down. Keep hips level and avoid leaning back to finish the press.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_dead_snatch.description: Place one kettlebell on the floor between the feet and hinge to grip it. Drive through the legs and guide the bell close into an overhead lockout. Punch through at the top and avoid swinging the bell far away from the body.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_dea.description: Start with one kettlebell on the floor between the feet. Snatch it overhead from dead stop, lower and switch hands or sides for the next rep. Keep the back long and avoid twisting as the bell leaves the floor.

ff_double_kettlebell_dead_half_snatch.description: Start with two kettlebells on the floor and hinge to grip both handles. Snatch them overhead, then lower to the rack before returning to the floor. Keep the bells close and avoid letting them crash into the shoulders.

ff_slider_barbell_overhead_reverse_lunge.description: Lock a barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep the bar stacked over the shoulders and shorten the slide if the ribs flare.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_single_leg_stan.description: Stand on one bent leg and rack a kettlebell in the opposite hand. Press the bell overhead while the free knee stays lifted or bent, then lower with control. Keep the pelvis level and avoid leaning toward the load.

ff_suspension_single_leg_standing_bent_k.description: Face the suspension anchor, hold the handles, and balance on one softly bent leg. Curl the handles toward the forehead by bending the elbows, then extend slowly. Keep upper arms high and avoid dropping the lifted foot for balance.

ff_double_kettlebell_otis_up.description: Lie on your back with two kettlebells locked out over the shoulders. Sit up to a tall torso while keeping both bells vertical, then lower with control. Keep the arms stacked and avoid using a fast leg kick.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_otis_up.description: Lie on your back with one kettlebell pressed above the shoulder. Sit up toward a tall torso while the bell stays stacked, then lower slowly. Keep eyes near the bell and avoid letting it drift behind you.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_otis_up.description: Lie on your back holding a kettlebell by the horns above the chest. Sit up to a tall position, keeping the bell close to the midline, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid throwing the bell forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_otis_up.description: Lie on your back with two kettlebells bottoms-up over the shoulders. Sit up smoothly while keeping the handles vertical, then lower to the floor. Use a light load and avoid chasing speed when the bells wobble.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_otis.description: Lie on your back with one kettlebell bottoms-up above the shoulder. Sit up while keeping the handle vertical, then return slowly to the floor. Keep the wrist stacked and avoid letting the bell tip inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_staggered_stance_roma.description: Stand with two dumbbells and stagger one foot slightly behind the other. Hinge over the front leg as the dumbbells track close to the thighs, then stand tall. Keep hips square and avoid pushing off hard from the rear foot.

ff_barbell_staggered_stance_romanian_dea.description: Hold a barbell in front of the thighs and stagger one foot back as a kickstand. Hinge the hips behind you while the bar stays close, then drive the front foot down to stand. Keep the bar over the front leg and avoid rounding for depth.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_turk.description: Lie on your back with one kettlebell bottoms-up over the shoulder and same-side knee bent. Roll up to the elbow and hand, then sit tall before returning. Keep the handle vertical and avoid letting the bell fall toward the thumb side.

ff_ring_hanging_knees_to_elbows.description: Hang from rings with arms long and shoulders active. Curl the pelvis and lift both knees toward the elbows, then lower without swinging. Keep the rings quiet and avoid kicking the legs for momentum.

ff_single_arm_plate_teacup.description: Stand tall holding a light plate flat in one palm. Circle the plate around the hand in a smooth teacup path without gripping the edge, then reverse if prescribed. Keep the shoulder relaxed and avoid rushing the wrist turnover.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_turkish_.description: Lie on your back with two kettlebells bottoms-up over the shoulders. Sit up to a tall torso while keeping both handles vertical, then lower slowly. Keep the bells stacked and avoid letting one side race ahead.

ff_superband_assisted_nordic_hamstring_c.description: Anchor a superband in front of you and kneel with heels secured. Hold the band for assistance as you lower the body forward from the knees, then pull back up. Keep hips extended and avoid folding at the waist.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_turkish_.description: Lie with two bottoms-up kettlebells locked out, then sit up into a tall Z-sit with legs straight. Lower the bells to the rack and press them overhead before returning. Keep handles vertical and avoid leaning back during the press.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_shin_box_hip_e.description: Sit in a 90/90 shin-box position with hands free or lightly posted. Lift the hips by driving both knees down, lower, then switch shin-box sides. Keep the chest tall and avoid twisting through the low back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_turkish_sit_up_.description: Lie with one kettlebell locked out over the shoulder and sit up into a Z-sit. Lower the bell to the rack, press it overhead, then reverse the sit-up. Keep the legs quiet and avoid leaning away from the pressing arm.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_turk.description: Lie with one kettlebell bottoms-up above the shoulder and sit up into a Z-sit. Bring the bell to the rack, press it overhead, then reverse the path. Keep the handle vertical and avoid rushing the transition.

ff_ring_chin_up.description: Hang from rings with palms turned toward you and arms straight. Pull the chest toward the rings by driving elbows down, then lower to full reach. Keep legs still and avoid craning the chin to finish.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_rotational_thru.description: Rack one kettlebell at the shoulder with feet set to pivot. Squat, drive up, and rotate through the hips as you press the bell overhead. Keep the knee tracking with the toes and avoid twisting from the low back.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and take a wide stance. Sink into one side, stay low, and shift across to the other side. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the working heel pop up.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_dead_cle.description: Set two kettlebells on the floor with handles angled for a clean. Clean them from dead stop to a bottoms-up rack, then reset or repeat. Keep elbows close and avoid catching with bent wrists or loose bells.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_carry.description: Hold one dumbbell at your side with the shoulder pulled gently down. Walk with slow, even steps and a tall spine. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_glute_bridge_isometri.description: Lie on your back with dumbbells at the chest and feet planted. Lift into a glute bridge and hold the hips level while pressing the dumbbells. Keep the ribs down and avoid dropping the hips as the arms press.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_dead_cle.description: Clean two kettlebells from the floor into a bottoms-up rack. Stabilize the handles, then press both bells overhead and lower back to the rack. Keep wrists vertical and reset if the bells tip during the clean.
Clean two kettlebells from the floor into a bottoms-up front rack. Squat, drive up, and press overhead in one controlled thruster. Keep the handles vertical and avoid speeding through the rack catch.

ff_bodyweight_foot_elevated_cossack_squa.description: Stand wide with one foot slightly elevated on a low plate or block. Shift toward the other leg, bend that knee, and keep the elevated leg long. Press back to center and avoid collapsing the chest for extra depth.

ff_barbell_overhead_foot_elevated_cossac.description: Lock a barbell overhead and stand wide with one foot on a low elevation. Shift into the working side while the elevated leg stays long, then return to center. Keep the bar stacked and avoid chasing depth if the heel lifts.

ff_landmine_isometric_split_squat_obliqu.description: Anchor a barbell in a landmine and hold the sleeve near the chest in a split squat. Hold the lower position while rotating the bar across the body, then return to center. Keep the front knee steady and avoid twisting the hips with the bar.

ff_barbell_back_rack_foot_elevated_cossa.description: Set a barbell across the upper back and stand wide with one foot slightly elevated. Shift into the grounded side, keeping the other leg long, then press back to center. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the bar roll forward.

ff_barbell_front_rack_foot_elevated_coss.description: Hold the bar in a front rack and stand wide with one foot on a low elevation. Sit into the grounded side while the elevated leg stays long, then return to center. Keep elbows high and avoid rounding to reach lower.

ff_barbell_feet_elevated_inverted_row.description: Set a barbell around waist height and place both heels on a bench or box for a feet-elevated inverted row. Hang under the bar with the body straight, then pull the chest to the bar. Lower until arms are long and avoid letting the hips sag.

ff_barbell_overhead_low_switch_cossack_s.description: Lock a barbell overhead and take a wide stance. Shift into one side, stay low through the middle, and switch to the other side. Keep the bar over the midfoot and avoid bending the elbows as you move.

ff_double_kettlebell_windmill.description: Hold one kettlebell overhead and the second at the lower hand or rack as prescribed. Turn the feet slightly away, push the hips back, and hinge into the windmill. Keep eyes on the top bell and avoid rounding the spine to reach lower.

ff_double_kettlebell_dragon_flag.description: Lie on a bench with two kettlebells held securely near the chest or as anchors if set up that way. Brace, roll onto the upper back, and lower the body as one long line. Bend the knees before the low back arches or hips fold.

ff_barbell_staggered_stance_overhead_pre.description: Set a staggered stance with the barbell in the front rack. Brace and press the bar overhead while both feet stay planted, then lower to the shoulders. Keep the ribs down and avoid turning the stance into a lunge.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_9090_hip.description: Sit in a 90/90 hip position with two kettlebells in the front rack. Shift the knees from side to side under control, then press one bell overhead at a time. Keep the torso tall and avoid leaning back during the press.

ff_kettlebell_dead_bug.description: Lie on your back holding a kettlebell above the chest. Brace the low back to the floor, then move opposite arm or leg in a dead-bug pattern. Keep the bell stacked and avoid letting the ribs pop up.

ff_battle_rope_isometric_split_squat_alt.description: Set a split stance and hold the bottom of the lunge while gripping both battle rope ends. Slam one rope to the side, then the other, keeping the legs still. Keep the front knee over the toes and avoid rising out of the hold.

ff_superband_standing_hip_adduction.description: Anchor a superband to the side and loop it around the working ankle. Stand tall and draw the leg across the body against the band, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis square and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_superband_lateral_step_hip_adduction.description: Loop a superband around the working ankle and anchor it to the side. Step laterally so the band pulls outward, then pull the leg back under the hip into adduction. Keep toes forward and avoid leaning the torso to finish.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack. Step forward into a walking lunge, drive through the front foot, and bring the rear leg through. Keep handles vertical and avoid letting the bells pull the elbows wide.

ff_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_horn_grip_sq.description: Start with a kettlebell on the floor between the feet. Clean it from dead stop, catch it by the horns at the chest, then perform a goblet squat. Keep the bell close and avoid letting it bang the forearms during the hand change.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack with elbows close. March in place, lifting one knee at a time without losing rack height. Keep wrists vertical and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Press two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and lock the arms out. March slowly, lifting one knee at a time while the bells stay stacked. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the handles tip forward.

ff_suspension_alternating_cossack_squat.description: Hold suspension handles and take a wide stance. Shift into one side for a Cossack squat, use the straps for balance, then move to the other side. Keep the working heel planted and avoid pulling yourself up with the arms.

ff_battle_rope_single_leg_standing_alter.description: Hold the battle rope ends and balance on one softly bent leg. Make alternating waves from the shoulders while the free leg stays off the floor. Keep the hips level and avoid hopping to keep balance.

ff_superband_assisted_dips.description: Loop a superband over dip bars and place knees or feet into the band. Lower between the bars with elbows tracking back, then press to straight arms. Keep shoulders down and avoid bouncing out of the band.

ff_battle_rope_isometric_split_squat_alt.description: Hold a split squat while gripping the battle rope ends. Make alternating waves without changing leg position. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee collapse as the ropes move.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_bal.description: Hinge with a kettlebell on the floor between the feet. Row it explosively with one hand, switch hands as it lowers, and repeat on the other side. Keep the back long and avoid jerking the torso upright.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up at the shoulders. Press both handles overhead until arms are vertical, then lower with control. Keep wrists stacked and avoid chasing reps after the bells start tipping.

ff_suspension_split_squat.description: Hold suspension handles and step into a split stance. Lower mostly straight down, using the straps lightly for balance, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid pulling yourself up with the arms.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_tactical_clean.description: Start with one kettlebell between the feet and hinge to grip it. Clean the bell, switch hands near the rack or between the legs as practiced, and catch smoothly on the other side. Keep the bell close and avoid a wide looping path.

ff_suspension_cyclist_squat.description: Hold suspension handles and stand with heels elevated and feet narrow. Sit the knees forward into a cyclist squat, then press through the forefoot and heel to stand. Use the straps for balance and avoid pulling the chest forward.

ff_macebell_360.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the handle end and the head offset. Cast it over one shoulder, guide it behind the head, and return to the front. Keep elbows close and avoid arching the low back as the mace circles.

ff_single_arm_suspension_contralateral_s.description: Hold one suspension handle with the hand opposite the standing leg. Reach the free leg back into a skater squat and lower under control, then drive through the standing foot. Keep the knee tracking over toes and avoid hanging from the strap.

ff_single_arm_macebell_360.description: Grip the mace in one hand near the handle end with the head offset. Cast it around the shoulder and behind the head, then guide it back to the front. Keep the wrist firm and avoid letting the mace pull you into a backbend.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_pivot_sp.description: Hold two kettlebells in the front rack and set a split stance. Pivot the feet and hips slightly as you lower into the split squat, then return to the start. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the front knee twist inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and set a split stance. Pivot with control as you lower, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep handles vertical and avoid rotating faster than the knees can track.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Press two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and set a split stance. Pivot the feet slightly as you lower into the split squat, then stand with the bells stacked. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the bells drift outside the shoulders.

ff_bodyweight_cyclist_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close together. Bend the knees forward and squat with an upright torso, then push the floor away to stand. Keep knees tracking over toes and avoid rocking off the heel wedge.

ff_kettlebell_half_kneeling_rotational_s.description: Set half kneeling with a kettlebell beside the front hip. Hike the bell across the body and swing it with a controlled torso rotation, then guide it back. Keep the spine tall and avoid yanking with the arm.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_push_jerk.description: Clean two kettlebells to the rack and stand tall. Dip, drive, and drop slightly under the bells to catch them overhead in a push jerk. Stand to finish and avoid pressing out a soft catch.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_cle.description: Clean one kettlebell to the rack, then dip and drive it overhead into a push jerk. Lower, switch sides, and repeat with the same path. Keep the bell close and avoid overextending the low back in the catch.

ff_kettlebell_isometric_split_squat_low_.description: Hold a split squat and grip a kettlebell by the horns near the outside hip. Sweep it diagonally up toward the opposite shoulder, then return under control. Keep the legs still and avoid turning the front knee with the chop.

ff_kettlebell_isometric_split_squat_alte.description: Hold the bottom of a split squat with a kettlebell by the horns at chest height. Circle the bell around the head one way, then alternate directions. Keep the ribs down and avoid rising out of the split squat.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_march.description: Hold two kettlebells in the front rack with elbows close. March in place, lifting one knee at a time while staying tall. Keep the bells quiet and avoid leaning back as the knee rises.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_march.description: Hold one kettlebell in each hand at the sides. March slowly with tall posture, lifting each knee without the bells swinging. Keep shoulders level and avoid shrugging under the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_rotational_half.description: Start with one kettlebell in a hinge and let the feet pivot slightly. Drive the hips, rotate through the torso, and guide the bell to overhead before lowering to the rack. Keep the bell close and avoid twisting through the low back.

ff_double_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_front.description: Start with two kettlebells on the floor. Clean them from dead stop to the front rack, then squat and stand with elbows forward. Keep the rack tight and avoid letting the bells pull the chest down.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_alternat.description: Sit in a shin-box position with two kettlebells in the front rack. Lift the hips into extension, lower, then switch shin-box sides and repeat. Keep elbows close and avoid twisting the low back during the switch.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_alternatin.description: Press two kettlebells overhead from a shin-box seat. Lift the hips, lower, then alternate the shin-box position while the bells stay stacked. Keep arms vertical and avoid leaning back to hold the weights.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_turkish_.description: Lie on your back with two kettlebells bottoms-up above the shoulders. Move through the get-up to standing while both handles stay vertical, then reverse each step. Use small transitions and avoid letting one bell drift behind you.

ff_slider_single_leg_hamstring_curl.description: Lie on your back with one heel on a slider and hips lifted. Slide that heel away until the leg is nearly straight, then curl it back under the knee. Keep hips level and avoid dropping to the floor between reps.

ff_kettlebell_staggered_stance_rotationa.description: Stand in a staggered stance with one kettlebell slightly in front. Hike it back, rotate through the hips, and swing to chest height. Keep the back long and avoid letting the rear foot twist the knee inward.

ff_single_arm_suspension_cyclist_squat.description: Hold one suspension handle and stand with heels elevated and feet narrow. Squat with knees traveling forward, then stand using the strap only for balance. Keep the torso upright and avoid pulling the working shoulder forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_altern.description: Hold one kettlebell at your side and step back across into a curtsy lunge. Pass the bell under or beside the front thigh as you return, then alternate sides. Keep hips square and avoid letting the front knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_low_bar_pause_back_squat.description: Set the bar low across the rear shoulders and brace before descending. Sit back and down, pause still at the bottom, then drive up over the midfoot. Keep the chest from folding and avoid bouncing out of the pause.
