ff_barbell_front_rack_pause_squat.description: Hold the bar in a front rack with elbows high. Squat between the heels, pause at the bottom, then stand while keeping the bar over midfoot. Keep elbows lifted and avoid letting the bar roll forward.

ff_barbell_high_bar_pause_back_squat.description: Set the bar high on the traps and stand with feet about shoulder width. Squat down with a tall chest, pause, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep knees tracking with toes and avoid relaxing in the bottom.

ff_barbell_overhead_pause_squat.description: Lock the bar overhead with hands wide and feet set for a squat. Descend, pause while the bar stays stacked over the midfoot, then stand. Keep elbows locked and avoid letting the bar drift forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_walkin.description: Hold one kettlebell suitcase-style and step forward into a lunge. Pass the bell under or beside the front thigh as you come through to the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid the load pulling you sideways.

ff_ring_hanging_knees_to_wrists.description: Hang from rings with arms long and shoulders active. Curl the pelvis and lift both knees toward the wrists, then lower slowly. Keep the rings steady and avoid swinging into the next rep.

ff_slider_suspension_lateral_lunge.description: Hold suspension handles with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the other knee bends, then pull back to center using the planted leg. Keep the straps light and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_macebell_10_to_2.description: Hold the mace with both hands and the head behind one shoulder. Swing it behind the head from the 10 oclock side to the 2 oclock side, then return. Keep elbows close and avoid arching the ribs as the mace passes behind you.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_cyclist_squat.description: Hold one dumbbell vertically at the chest and elevate the heels. Squat with knees traveling forward and torso upright, then press through the feet to stand. Keep the dumbbell close and avoid rocking off the wedge.

ff_slider_barbell_front_rack_lateral_lun.description: Hold a barbell in the front rack with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot to the side as the planted knee bends, then pull back to standing. Keep elbows high and avoid letting the planted knee collapse inward.

ff_slider_barbell_back_rack_lateral_lung.description: Set a barbell across the upper back and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out while sitting into the planted leg, then return to center. Keep the chest tall and avoid twisting the bar toward the slider.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_cossack_.description: Lock a barbell overhead and take a wide stance. Shift into a Cossack squat on one side, return through center, then alternate. Keep the bar over the midfoot and avoid letting the working heel lift.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_reverse_.description: Lock a barbell overhead with arms straight. Step one foot back into a reverse lunge, return to standing, then alternate sides. Keep the bar stacked and avoid arching the back as the knee lowers.

ff_macebell_single_leg_standing_bent_kne.description: Stand on one softly bent leg and hold the mace with the head offset below the hand. Curl it briskly toward the shoulder, then control the drop without setting the free foot down. Keep the wrist firm and avoid swinging the torso.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_carry.description: Hold one kettlebell in each hand at the sides. Walk with tall posture, quiet shoulders, and even steps. Keep the bells from swinging and avoid shrugging them toward the ears.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_carry.description: Hold one kettlebell at your side like a suitcase. Walk slowly while keeping shoulders and hips level. Brace the torso and avoid leaning away from the bell.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one side for a Cossack squat, then drive back to center. Keep handles vertical and avoid letting the working knee cave inward.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_kne.description: Set up on hands and toes with knees hovering just above the floor and a kettlebell outside one hand. Reach the opposite hand under the body to pull the bell across, then alternate. Keep hips low and avoid rocking side to side.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_high_pull.description: Start with one kettlebell in a hinge between the feet. Drive the hips, pull the elbow high and back, then let the bell float down into the next hinge. Keep the bell close and avoid muscling it up with a shrug.

ff_double_indian_club_outer_heart_shaped.description: Stand tall with one Indian club in each hand at the sides. Swing both clubs outward and up through a heart-shaped path, then guide them back down. Keep wrists relaxed and avoid speeding up before both clubs match.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_thr.description: Rack one kettlebell at the shoulder and stand with feet squat width. Squat, drive up into a press, then lower and switch sides on the next rep. Keep the elbow connected in the rack and avoid turning the press into a backbend.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_knee_over_.description: Hold one kettlebell in each hand and set a split stance. Let the front knee travel over the toes as you lower, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the heel planted and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_kettlebell_sumo_deadlift_to_pu.description: Stand over two kettlebells in a wide sumo stance and deadlift them to standing. Set the bells down, step or jump back to a plank, and perform a push-up on or between the handles. Keep the back long and avoid rushing the transition.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_cle.description: Clean one kettlebell to the rack, squat, and drive into an overhead press. Lower, switch sides, and repeat the clean-to-thruster sequence. Keep the bell close and avoid letting the rack collapse before the squat.

ff_suspension_alternating_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold suspension handles and stand tall. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, return to center, then alternate sides. Use the straps for balance and avoid pulling the front knee inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_single_leg_bala.description: Stand on one softly bent leg with a kettlebell in one hand. Pass the bell around the body or from hand to hand while staying balanced. Keep the stance hip level and avoid rushing the transfer.

ff_barbell_front_rack_alternating_forwar.description: Hold the barbell in a front rack with elbows high. Step forward into a lunge, push back to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the bar roll down the shoulders.

ff_barbell_front_rack_alternating_revers.description: Hold the bar in a front rack and stand tall. Step one leg back into a reverse lunge, drive through the front foot to stand, then alternate. Keep elbows high and avoid tipping forward as the back knee lowers.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_forward_.description: Lock the barbell overhead with arms straight. Step forward into a lunge, push back to standing, then alternate sides. Keep the bar over the shoulders and avoid letting the front knee cave inward.

ff_macebell_alternating_360.description: Hold the mace with both hands and cast it around one shoulder into a 360. Return to the front, then send the next rep around the other shoulder. Keep the path smooth and avoid twisting the knees with the mace.

ff_clubbell_shield_cast.description: Hold the clubbell in order position near the chest. Cast it over the shoulder and around behind the head, then pull it back to order. Keep shoulders packed and avoid flaring the ribs as the club travels.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_shield_cast.description: Hold one clubbell in order position at the shoulder. Cast it around the head and back to the start with a smooth elbow circle. Keep the wrist firm and avoid letting the club pull the torso backward.

ff_macebell_barbarian_squat.description: Hold the mace with both hands and the head behind the shoulders or upper back. Squat down with feet rooted, then stand while keeping the mace controlled. Keep elbows from flaring wide and avoid rounding the chest.

ff_single_arm_macebell_barbarian_squat.description: Hold the mace in one hand with the head behind the shoulder in a barbarian position. Squat under control and stand while the free arm balances. Keep the mace close and avoid side-bending away from the load.

ff_alternating_double_clubbell_shield_ca.description: Hold two clubbells in order position. Cast one club around the shoulder and back, then repeat on the other side. Keep the resting club steady and avoid rotating the torso faster than the club path.

ff_double_clubbell_side_shoulder_cast.description: Hold two clubbells at the shoulders with the heavy ends controlled. Cast them to the side shoulder path and return to order. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the clubs pull the wrists open.

ff_macebell_isometric_split_squat_low_to.description: Hold a split squat with the mace near the outside hip. Chop diagonally upward toward the opposite shoulder, then return slowly. Keep the front knee steady and avoid rotating the pelvis with the mace.

ff_double_clubbell_side_shoulder_cast_to.description: Hold two clubbells in order position and perform a side shoulder cast. As they return, press into a side flag position before bringing them back. Keep wrists firm and avoid leaning back to move the clubs.

ff_clubbell_mill.description: Start with the clubbell in a guard position. Swing through the 180 arc, cast around the shoulder, and return to guard in one smooth mill. Lead with the elbow and avoid muscling the path with only the hand.

ff_clubbell_half_kneeling_shield_cast.description: Set a half-kneeling stance and hold the clubbell in order position. Cast it around the shoulder and behind the head, then return to order. Squeeze the back glute and avoid leaning away from the club.

ff_double_clubbell_iron_cross.description: Stand tall with one clubbell in each hand at the sides. Raise both arms out to an iron-cross position and hold or lower under control. Keep shoulders down and avoid arching the back to lift the clubs.

ff_double_clubbell_half_kneeling_iron_cr.description: Set a half-kneeling stance with one clubbell in each hand. Raise both arms out to an iron-cross position, pause, then lower with control. Keep ribs stacked and avoid shifting onto the front leg.

ff_clubbell_gamma_cast.description: Hold the clubbell in order position near the chest. Rotate the shoulders and thoracic spine as you cast the club through a gamma path, then decelerate back to order. Keep the hips quiet and avoid losing the handle angle.

ff_clubbell_half_kneeling_gamma_cast.description: Set half kneeling and hold the clubbell in order position. Cast through the gamma path with controlled torso rotation, then return to order. Keep the back glute tight and avoid swaying through the low back.

ff_double_clubbell_side_flag_press.description: Hold two clubbells at the chest with heavy ends forward. Press both clubs out into a side flag angle, then pull them back under control. Keep wrists straight and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_foot_e.description: Hold one kettlebell suitcase-style opposite the front leg and elevate the front foot slightly. Lower with the front knee traveling over the toes, then drive up through that foot. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the load pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_contra.description: Hold one kettlebell at the side opposite the front leg and set a split stance. Let the front knee travel over the toes as you lower, then stand through the front foot. Keep hips square and avoid leaning toward the kettlebell.

ff_double_indian_club_inner_heart_shaped.description: Stand tall with one Indian club in each hand. Swing both clubs inward through a heart-shaped path and guide them back to the sides. Keep elbows soft and avoid forcing the clubs with stiff wrists.

ff_alternating_double_indian_club_inner_.description: Hold one Indian club in each hand and start tall. Swing one club inward through the heart-shaped path, then alternate to the other side. Keep the rhythm even and avoid crossing the clubs too close to the face.

ff_alternating_double_clubbell_side_flag.description: Hold two clubbells at the chest with heavy ends forward. Press one club into a side flag while the other stays parked, then alternate. Keep the torso square and avoid letting the resting club drift.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_side_flag_press.description: Hold one clubbell at the chest with the heavy end forward. Press it out to a side flag angle, pause, then pull it back under control. Keep the shoulder packed and avoid bending the wrist backward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_circle.description: Hold one clubbell near the shoulder in order position. Guide the elbow through an inside circle so the club arcs around and returns to the start. Keep the ribs down and avoid letting the club yank the wrist open.

ff_alternating_double_clubbell_inside_ci.description: Hold two clubbells in order position. Make an inside circle with one club, return it, then alternate to the other side. Keep the nonmoving club stable and avoid rushing the hand switch.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_side_shoulder_cas.description: Hold one clubbell at the shoulder in order position. Cast it through the side shoulder path and return it to the start. Lead with the elbow and avoid leaning back as the club passes behind you.

ff_barbell_reverse_grip_bicep_curl.description: Stand holding a barbell with palms down and hands about shoulder width. Curl the bar toward the chest, then lower to straight arms. Keep elbows close and avoid bending the wrists back to finish.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_reverse_mill.description: Hold one clubbell in a guard position. Move through the reverse mill path with an outside elbow circle, then return to guard. Keep the shoulder packed and avoid letting the hand drag the club behind the elbow.

ff_clubbell_clean_to_barbarian_squat.description: Start with the clubbell near the floor or low guard. Clean it to the barbarian position behind the shoulders, then squat and stand under control. Keep the club close and avoid catching it with a loose wrist.

ff_clubbell_tall_kneeling_gamma_cast.description: Set both knees on the floor and hold the clubbell in order position. Cast through the gamma path and return to order without shifting the hips. Keep glutes tight and avoid leaning back as the club rotates.

ff_clubbell_inside_circle.description: Hold the clubbell in order position with both hands or the prescribed grip. Guide it through an inside circle and return to the start smoothly. Keep elbows soft and avoid letting the club pull the ribs open.

ff_clubbell_tall_kneeling_shield_cast.description: Kneel tall with the clubbell in order position. Cast it around the shoulder and behind the head, then return to order. Keep glutes squeezed and avoid arching the low back during the cast.

ff_clubbell_isometric_split_squat_shield.description: Hold the bottom of a split squat with a clubbell in order position. Perform a shield cast around the shoulder while the legs stay still. Keep the front knee tracking over toes and avoid rising out of the hold.

ff_clubbell_single_leg_standing_bent_kne.description: Stand on one softly bent leg with the clubbell in order position. Cast it around the shoulder and return to the start while maintaining balance. Keep hips level and avoid hopping as the club moves.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_tall_kneeling_shi.description: Kneel tall and hold one clubbell in order position at the shoulder. Cast it around the head and return to order with a smooth elbow circle. Keep the wrist firm and avoid leaning away from the club.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_mill.description: Hold one clubbell in a guard position with feet planted. Swing through the mill arc, cast around the shoulder, and return to guard. Lead with the elbow and avoid muscling the club only with the hand.

ff_double_clubbell_isometric_split_squat.description: Lean the shoulders forward past the hands with elbows locked. Press the floor or rings away and hold the listed planche or lever shape with tight ribs. Keep the shoulder blades spread and avoid jumping into a bent-elbow hold.

ff_double_clubbell_single_leg_standing_b.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_deadlift.description: Hold the load in one hand at your side like a suitcase. Set the bar over midfoot, brace, and push the floor away while keeping the bar close to the legs through the pull. Keep the lats tight and avoid letting the hips shoot up before the bar leaves the floor.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clean.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_double_clubbell_mill.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_alternating_single_arm_clubbell_mill.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_clubbell_isometric_split_squat_gamma_.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_clubbell_single_leg_standing_bent_kne.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_macebell_alternating_360_to_ballistic.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Curl the weight through the range named by the exercise, then lower with control. Keep the upper arm still and avoid swinging the load.

ff_bar_chin_up.description: Hang from the bar with arms long and shoulders set. Pull the chest toward the bar by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_bar_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar with arms long and shoulders set. Pull the chest toward the bar by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_side_swing.description: Stand tall with the kettlebell to one side and feet wide enough to let the hips hinge. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_pendulum.description: Stand tall holding the clubbell in one hand near the shoulder. Guide it in a controlled inside arc around the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare or the wrist collapse.

ff_double_clubbell_swipe.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_swipe.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_cross_body_swipe.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_bar_scapular_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar with arms long and shoulders set. Pull the chest toward the bar by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_circle_to_.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_clubbell_barbarian_squat.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_circle_to_.description: Stand tall and guide the clubbell in a controlled circle around the head or body as named by the drill. Keep the grip secure and the elbows soft through the arc. Move slowly and avoid letting the bell pull the ribs open.

ff_clubbell_inside_circle_to_outside_cir.description: Stand tall and guide the clubbell in a controlled circle around the head or body as named by the drill. Keep the grip secure and the elbows soft through the arc. Move slowly and avoid letting the bell pull the ribs open.

ff_double_clubbell_barbarian_squat.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_side_to_side_swin.description: Stand tall with the kettlebell to one side and feet wide enough to let the hips hinge. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_alternating_single_arm_clubbell_side_.description: Stand tall with the kettlebell to one side and feet wide enough to let the hips hinge. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_clubbell_alternating_inside_pendulum_.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_single_leg_standi.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_bar_skin_the_cat.description: Hang from the bar and tuck the knees, then thread the feet through the arms into a controlled invert. Open the shoulders gradually and return the same way. Use a spot or low rings until the shoulder range feels secure.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_tall.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_circle_to_.description: Hold one clubbell at the chest with the heavy end forward. Press it forward and slightly up until the arm is long, then pull it back under control. Keep the shoulder packed and avoid letting the club twist the wrist.

ff_double_clubbell_order_bulgarian_split.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean.description: Hold the bar at mid-thigh and hinge slightly to start. Clean the bar to the front rack, receive in a squat, then stand directly into an overhead press. Keep the elbows high in the squat and avoid pressing before the legs finish driving.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_fee.description: Anchor the band low behind you and stand facing away with the band between the legs. Hinge back until the hips reach behind you, then drive the hips forward to stand. Keep the arms as hooks and avoid turning it into a squat.

ff_miniband_supine_alternating_hip_flexi.description: Place the band above the knees or ankles as listed and set a quarter-squat or hip-hinge stance. Step laterally or forward while keeping tension on the band through each step. Avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_tall_kne.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_half_kne.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_barbarian_squat.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_single_leg_standi.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_clubbell_order_cyclist_squat.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Stand with heels elevated and a narrow stance, sit the knees forward and hips down, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the torso upright and avoid losing balance off the elevated surface.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_contralater.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load on the side opposite the front leg. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_alternating_single_arm_macebell_10_to.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Guide the bell around the body and catch it high near the shoulder before passing or lowering it. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_tall_kne.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_clubbell_inside_pendulum_to_inside_pu.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Guide the load in a controlled arc around or over the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare.

ff_clubbell_order_squat.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsilateral.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load on the same side as the front leg. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_squat.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_double_clubbell_order_squat.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_clubbell_alternating_inside_pendulum_.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Guide the load in a controlled arc around or over the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_arm_.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_double_kettlebell_push_up.description: Place the feet on a bench or box and set the hands under the shoulders. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bodyweight_pseudo_planche_lean.description: Set up in a push-up position with hands turned out and shoulders leaning forward of the wrists. Protract the shoulder blades and hold or move through the lean as the drill describes. Keep the core tight and avoid sagging at the hips.

ff_macebell_360_to_single_arm_360.description: Hold the mace near the head with both hands. Swing it in a controlled 360 around the body, passing behind the back with a smooth hand change. Keep the ribs braced and use a lighter mace until the path stays even on both sides.

ff_tire_feet_elevated_decline_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bodyweight_bench_feet_elevated_declin.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bodyweight_box_feet_elevated_decline_.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bodyweight_feet_elevated_pike_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_tire_feet_elevated_pike_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_push_jer.description: Stand with one kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Dip and drive the weight upward, then quickly bend the knees to catch it overhead. Stand tall to finish and avoid pressing it out slowly after the catch.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Stand with one kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Dip and drive the weight upward, then quickly bend the knees to catch it overhead. Stand tall to finish and avoid pressing it out slowly after the catch.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Brace tall and press the load overhead until the arms are vertical, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_push.description: Stand with one kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Dip and drive the weight upward, then quickly bend the knees to catch it overhead. Stand tall to finish and avoid pressing it out slowly after the catch.

ff_bulgarian_bag_side_to_side_swing.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_half.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_single_ring_rotational_row.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Pull the load toward the torso by driving the elbows back, then return until the arms are long. Keep the chest up and avoid rocking the torso to move the weight.

ff_trap_bar_bottoms_up_tall_kneeling_ove.description: Kneel tall under or inside the trap bar with the handles at shoulder height. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_clubbell_clean.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_double_clubbell_clean.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_clean.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_trap_bar_z_press.description: Sit on the floor with legs straight and the bell in the rack. Brace tall and press the load overhead until the arms are vertical, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_trap_bar_bottoms_up_floor_press.description: Kneel tall under or inside the trap bar with the handles at shoulder height. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_trap_bar_bottoms_up_bench_press.description: Kneel tall under or inside the trap bar with the handles at shoulder height. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_bulgarian_bag_alternating_spin_to_sid.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_swipe.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_snatch.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_alternating_rotational_.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_alternating_side_swing_.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_dead_clean.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_swing.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_front_rack_squat.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load in the front rack. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the elbows forward and the load close to the ribs.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_squat.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load as listed. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_swing_squat.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_alternating_forward_lun.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_battle_rope_single_leg_standing_doubl.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with knees softly bent. Create the wave, slam, or spiral named by the drill while keeping the torso braced. Drive the motion from the shoulders and core instead of swaying the whole body.

ff_battle_rope_single_leg_standing_power.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Work one leg at a time with the other foot lifted or behind you. Load through the hips and core, then release or throw the ball along the path named by the drill. Use the legs first and avoid throwing only with the arms.

ff_landmine_v_grip_tall_kneeling_chest_p.description: Kneel tall facing the landmine with glutes squeezed. Brace and press the bar forward and up until the arm is nearly straight, then lower back to the shoulder. Press one side for the planned reps before switching. Keep the torso from rotating or leaning away from the bar.

ff_double_clubbell_swipe_to_alternating_.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_double_clubbell_order_cyclist_squat.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_cyclist_squ.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Lie on the floor with the kettlebell or kettlebells held bottoms-up over the elbows. Press to straight arms, then lower until the upper arms touch the floor. Keep wrists vertical and avoid letting the bells tip.

ff_battle_rope_single_leg_standing_clap.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with knees softly bent. Create the wave, slam, or spiral named by the drill while keeping the torso braced. Drive the motion from the shoulders and core instead of swaying the whole body.

ff_single_arm_macebell_mill.description: Hold the mace near the head and guide it through the mill or whip path behind the body with soft elbows. Switch hand timing smoothly as the mace circles. Keep the ribs braced and reduce weight if the bell pulls you off balance.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_low_switch_cossack_sq.description: Stand in a wide stance holding a dumbbell at the chest. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_alternatin.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clean_to.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_split_jerk.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_clean_to.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_cossack_sq.description: Stand in a wide stance with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_alternating_.description: Stand in a wide stance with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_cossack_squa.description: Stand in a wide stance with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_double_dumbbell_low_hold_alternating_.description: Stand in a wide stance with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_double_dumbbell_low_hold_cossack_squa.description: Stand in a wide stance with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_split_.description: Stand at the landmine with one hand on the sleeve. Clean the sleeve to the shoulder, then dip and split the feet as you press overhead. Land with both knees soft and avoid pressing before the leg drive finishes.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_cle.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side, alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_suspension_low_switch_cossack_squat.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_suspension_cossack_squat.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Stand in a wide stance and shift to one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_single_arm_suspension_alternating_cos.description: Set the dumbbell between or just in front of the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive through the legs and hips, then guide the dumbbell close to the body into an overhead lockout as you receive it in a squat. Keep the arm path close and avoid swinging the load far away from you.

ff_single_arm_suspension_contralateral_c.description: Stand on one leg with the load held as listed and reach the free leg forward for a pistol or back for a pistol squat. Bend the standing knee and sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts or the torso collapses.

ff_single_arm_suspension_low_switch_coss.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_suspension_foot_elevated_cossack_squa.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the named position. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_suspension_foot_elevated_c.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_barbell_overhead_cossack_squat.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the named position. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_barbell_front_rack_low_switch_cossack.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_back_rack_low_switch_cossack_.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_back_rack_cossack_squat.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar across the upper back. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_barbell_front_rack_cossack_squat.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar on the front shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_landmine_goblet_cossack_squat.description: Stand in a wide stance holding the landmine end at the chest. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_landmine_goblet_low_switch_cossack_sq.description: Stand in a wide stance holding a dumbbell at the chest. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_contralater.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_carry.description: Hold the load in the listed carry position and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_dumbbell_hip_thrust.description: Hold the dumbbells in a stable start position. Set the upper back on a bench or floor, tuck the ribs, and drive through the heels to lift the hips. Pause at the top and avoid arching the low back for extra height.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_carry.description: Hold the load overhead with the arm stacked and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_carry.description: Hold the load in the front rack and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_carry.description: Hold the load in the front rack and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_barbell_overhead_carry.description: Hold the load overhead with the arm stacked and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_single_arm_barbell_overhead_carry.description: Hold the load overhead with the arm stacked and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_barbell_front_rack_carry.description: Hold the load in the front rack and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_alternatin.description: Hold kettlebells at your sides and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep the front heel grounded and avoid leaning toward one weight.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sum.description: Place one kettlebell between wide-set feet with toes turned slightly out. Hinge and bend the knees to grip the handle with one hand, then stand by driving through the whole foot. Keep the chest lifted and do not jerk the bell off the floor.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_march.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_double_kettlebell_half_snatch.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_hal.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_snatch.description: Set up half kneeling with the bell in front of the working hip. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_double_kettlebell_half_snatch_to_thru.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_cossack_sq.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then drive back through center and repeat to the other side. Keep the heel down on the working side and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_squat.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_squat.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_heavy_sandbag_turkish_get_up.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Start on your back with the load pressed up, roll to the elbow, post to the hand. Reverse the steps to return.. Move in clear segments and avoid rushing past a wobbly overhead position.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_alternating_fo.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_alternating_re.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.
