ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_alternating_re.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_walking_lunge.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_clean.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_heavy_sandbag_over_the_shoulder.description: Set the feet close to the tire or sandbag and hinge to grip it securely. Drive through the legs and hips to lift, then push or heave the load over as the drill describes. Keep the back braced and avoid rounding during the pull.

ff_heavy_sandbag_alternating_shoulder_cl.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_alternating_over_the_sh.description: Set the feet close to the tire or sandbag and hinge to grip it securely. Drive through the legs and hips to lift, then push or heave the load over as the drill describes. Keep the back braced and avoid rounding during the pull.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_walking_lunge.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_turkish_get_up.description: Lie on your back with one arm locked out holding a dumbbell above the shoulder and the same-side knee bent. Roll to the elbow, come to the hand, bridge the hips, sweep the leg under, and stand while watching the weight. Reverse the steps slowly and keep the loaded shoulder stacked over the hand.

ff_single_arm_macebell_reverse_mill.description: Hold the mace near the head and guide it through the mill or whip path behind the body with soft elbows. Switch hand timing smoothly as the mace circles. Keep the ribs braced and reduce weight if the bell pulls you off balance.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_sissy_squat.description: Stand in a split stance near a support you can hold lightly. Bend both knees to lower straight down, then press through the front foot to stand. Use the hand for balance only and keep the front knee tracking over toes.

ff_bodyweight_sissy_squat.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_knee_over_to.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the named position. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_superband_sissy_squat.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_walking_lu.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_walking_.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_walking_.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_walkin.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_barbell_front_rack_walking_lunge.description: Stand tall with the bar across the upper back. Step forward into a lunge, drive through the front foot, and continue into the next step. Keep the torso stacked over the hips and avoid pushing off the back foot too hard.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_horse_stance_squat.description: Hold one dumbbell or kettlebell vertically at the chest. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_alternating_forward.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_alternating_single_arm_clubbell_rever.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_macebell_mill.description: Hold the mace near the head and guide it through the mill or whip path behind the body with soft elbows. Switch hand timing smoothly as the mace circles. Keep the ribs braced and reduce weight if the bell pulls you off balance.

ff_double_clubbell_order_walking_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_walking_lun.description: Hold the kettlebell in the front rack. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_clubbell_order_walking_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_bodyweight_box_jump.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_bodyweight_lateral_box_jump.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_lateral_box_ju.description: Stand beside a low bench or box with knees slightly bent. Jump laterally over or onto the bench, land softly, then repeat to the other side. Regain balance before the next jump and avoid stiff knees.

ff_bodyweight_straddle_planche.description: Lean the shoulders forward past the hands with elbows locked. Press the floor or rings away and hold the listed planche or lever shape with tight ribs. Keep the shoulder blades spread and avoid jumping into a bent-elbow hold.

ff_macebell_single_leg_offset_bent_over_.description: Hinge at the hips with a flat back and hold the weight so the arms hang below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the lower ribs, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the torso quiet and avoid standing up to finish the row.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bird_dog_row.description: Set one hand and the opposite knee on a bench, then extend the free leg behind you and hold a dumbbell in the free hand. Row the dumbbell toward the hip while keeping the leg and torso still. Lower slowly and avoid opening the hips as the weight comes up.

ff_double_kettlebell_half_snatch_to_fron.description: Hold the load in the front rack with elbows forward. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_single_arm_suspension_ipsilateral_pis.description: Stand on one leg with the load held as listed and reach the free leg forward for a pistol or back for a pistol squat. Bend the standing knee and sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts or the torso collapses.

ff_kettlebell_single_leg_standing_hip_fl.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then drive back through center and repeat to the other side. Keep the heel down on the working side and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

ff_alternating_single_arm_macebell_360.description: Hold the mace near the head with both hands. Swing it in a controlled 360 around the body, passing behind the back with a smooth hand change. Keep the ribs braced and use a lighter mace until the path stays even on both sides.

ff_double_kettlebell_low_hold_cossack_sq.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_low_hold_alternatin.description: Stand in a wide stance with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_cossack.description: Stand in a wide stance holding a dumbbell at the chest. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_barbell_zercher_carry.description: Hold the bar in the crooks of the elbows with the chest tall. Sit the hips down between the elbows and stand by driving through the whole foot. Keep the elbows tight to the torso and avoid letting the bar slip down the arms.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seated_o.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Brace tall and press the load overhead until the arms are vertical, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seat.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Brace tall and press the load overhead until the arms are vertical, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_macebell_offset_overhead_press.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Brace tall and press the load overhead until the arms are vertical, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_sots_press.description: Kneel with one foot forward and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite the front leg. Brace the glutes and press the dumbbell overhead, then lower to shoulder height. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_sots_press.description: Sit in the bottom of a squat with the load in the rack. Brace tall and press the load overhead until the arms are vertical, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_alte.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width with one dumbbell held securely. Squat between the heels, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_ipsilateral_cos.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down, then hold the load at the side. Hold the load on the same side as the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the load at your side without leaning toward it.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_foot_elevated_cossa.description: Stand in a wide stance holding the landmine end at the chest. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_alternating_.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_contrala.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_barbell_overhead_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_contrala.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_ipsilate.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_ipsilate.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up overhead. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_low_switch_cossack_.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_kettlebell_low_hold_cossack_squat.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_kettlebell_low_hold_alternating_cossa.description: Stand in a wide stance with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_ipsi.description: Hold the load in the front rack with elbows forward. Hold the load on the same side as the working leg. Stand in a wide stance and shift to one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_carry.description: Hold the load in the front rack and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_carry.description: Hold the load overhead with the arm stacked and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold the load overhead with the arm stacked and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_carry.description: Hold the load overhead with the arm stacked and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Hold the load overhead with the arm stacked and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_b.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_double_clubbell_order_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_double_clubbell_order_alternating_ste.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_contralater.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsilateral.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_bar_advanced_tuck_back_lever.description: Hang or start inverted on the rings with a firm grip. Lower into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full back lever shape named. Push the rings down and avoid shrugging into the ears.

ff_bar_advanced_tuck_front_lever.description: Hang from the rings with arms straight and shoulders pulled down. Lift into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full front lever shape named. Keep ribs tucked and avoid letting the hips sag.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_sots_pres.description: Stand with two dumbbells racked at the shoulders. Dip and drive to press one dumbbell overhead, lower it, then alternate sides. Keep the dip vertical and avoid leaning toward the pressing arm.

ff_double_dumbbell_seesaw_sots_press.description: Kneel tall with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Press one weight overhead as the other stays racked, then lower and switch sides. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning away from the pressing arm.

ff_suspension_single_leg_standing_bent_k.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_bar_circle_front_lever.description: Hang or support on the bar or rings with straight arms. Lift or hold the body in the lever position named by the exercise while keeping shoulders engaged. Move slowly into position and avoid bending the elbows to cheat the hold.

ff_suspension_single_leg_standing_bent_k.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_single_arm_suspension_rotational_row.description: Hold one suspension handle and lean back with feet planted. Pull the chest toward the handle, then lower until the arm is long. Keep shoulders and hips square instead of twisting to shorten the rep.

ff_alternating_double_indian_club_outer_.description: Stand tall holding one light Indian club at your side. Swing it outward in a heart-shaped path and return to the start with a smooth wrist turnover. Keep the shoulder relaxed and avoid forcing speed before the pattern is clear.

ff_double_indian_club_parallel_swing.description: Stand tall holding one light Indian club at your side. Swing it outward in a heart-shaped path and return to the start with a smooth wrist turnover. Keep the shoulder relaxed and avoid forcing speed before the pattern is clear.

ff_suspension_feet_elevated_decline_push.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bar_front_lever_pull_to_inverted_hang.description: Start in a front lever shape on the rings with arms straight. Pull the body upward as one piece until you reach an inverted hang, then lower with control. Keep the hips lifted and avoid bending the elbows early.

ff_bar_full_back_lever.description: Hang or support on the bar or rings with straight arms. Lift or hold the body in the lever position named by the exercise while keeping shoulders engaged. Move slowly into position and avoid bending the elbows to cheat the hold.

ff_bar_full_front_lever.description: Hang from the bar with shoulders active and legs held in a tight lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull.

ff_slider_atomic_push_up.description: Place the slider under the working foot or limb on a smooth surface. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_slider_reach_push_up.description: Start in a kneeling push-up with one hand on a slider. As you bend the elbows, slide the slider hand forward into a reach, then pull it back as you press up. Keep the ribs braced and avoid letting the low back sag.

ff_bar_half_lay_back_lever.description: Hang or start inverted on the rings with a firm grip. Lower into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full back lever shape named. Push the rings down and avoid shrugging into the ears.

ff_bar_half_lay_front_lever.description: Hang from the rings with arms straight and shoulders pulled down. Lift into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full front lever shape named. Keep ribs tucked and avoid letting the hips sag.

ff_bar_hanging_knee_raise.description: Hang from the bar with active shoulders and a braced core. Lift the toes, legs, or knees toward the bar as the variation requires, then lower without swinging. Avoid using a huge kip unless the drill calls for it.

ff_bar_hanging_knees_to_elbows.description: Hang from the rings or bar with arms long and shoulders engaged. Exhale as you lift the knees toward the elbows or wrists, then lower with control. Avoid swinging the legs or shrugging into the ears.

ff_bar_hanging_knees_to_wrists.description: Hang from the rings or bar with arms long and shoulders engaged. Exhale as you lift the knees toward the elbows or wrists, then lower with control. Avoid swinging the legs or shrugging into the ears.

ff_bar_hanging_leg_raise.description: Hang from the bar with active shoulders and a braced core. Lift the toes, legs, or knees toward the bar as the variation requires, then lower without swinging. Avoid using a huge kip unless the drill calls for it.

ff_bar_hanging_toes_to_bar.description: Hang from the bar with active shoulders and a braced core. Lift the toes, legs, or knees toward the bar as the variation requires, then lower without swinging. Avoid using a huge kip unless the drill calls for it.

ff_bar_hanging_oblique_knee_raise.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and legs together. Tuck the knees toward one side of the chest, lower under control, then alternate sides. Start each rep from stillness and avoid twisting with momentum.

ff_bar_inverted_hang.description: Hang from the bar or rings with arms long and shoulders relaxed but engaged. Hold the listed hang position while breathing steadily. Avoid shrugging into the ears or swinging the body.

ff_bar_hanging_l_sit_flutter_kicks.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and raise both legs to an L-sit height you can control. Alternate small flutter kicks while keeping the torso still. Lower the legs if your grip slips or the body starts swinging.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_i.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_suspension_rotational_row.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Pull the load toward the torso by driving the elbows back, then return until the arms are long. Keep the chest up and avoid rocking the torso to move the weight.

ff_slider_alternating_reach_push_up.description: Place the slider under the working foot or limb on a smooth surface. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_alternating_single_arm_landmine_z_pre.description: Stand facing the landmine with the bar at one shoulder. Brace and press the bar forward and up until the arm is nearly straight, then lower back to the shoulder. Switch shoulders each rep or after each side as listed. Keep the torso from rotating or leaning away from the bar.

ff_single_arm_macebell_bull_whip.description: Hold the mace near the head and guide it through the mill or whip path behind the body with soft elbows. Switch hand timing smoothly as the mace circles. Keep the ribs braced and reduce weight if the bell pulls you off balance.

ff_bar_single_leg_out_back_lever.description: Hang or start inverted on the rings with a firm grip. Lower into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full back lever shape named. Push the rings down and avoid shrugging into the ears.

ff_bar_single_leg_out_front_lever.description: Hang from the rings with arms straight and shoulders pulled down. Lift into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full front lever shape named. Keep ribs tucked and avoid letting the hips sag.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_push_pre.description: Lie on the floor with the kettlebell or kettlebells held bottoms-up over the elbows. Press to straight arms, then lower until the upper arms touch the floor. Keep wrists vertical and avoid letting the bells tip.

ff_bar_straddle_back_lever.description: Hang or support on the bar or rings with straight arms. Lift or hold the body in the lever position named by the exercise while keeping shoulders engaged. Move slowly into position and avoid bending the elbows to cheat the hold.

ff_bar_straddle_front_lever.description: Hang or support on the bar or rings with straight arms. Lift or hold the body in the lever position named by the exercise while keeping shoulders engaged. Move slowly into position and avoid bending the elbows to cheat the hold.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Lie on the floor with the kettlebell or kettlebells held bottoms-up over the elbows. Press to straight arms, then lower until the upper arms touch the floor. Keep wrists vertical and avoid letting the bells tip.

Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_bar_tuck_back_lever.description: Hang or start inverted on the rings with a firm grip. Lower into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full back lever shape named. Push the rings down and avoid shrugging into the ears.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seesaw_o.description: Lie on a flat bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_bar_tuck_front_lever.description: Hang from the rings with arms straight and shoulders pulled down. Lift into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full front lever shape named. Keep ribs tucked and avoid letting the hips sag.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_half_kne.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_ring_full_planche_push_up.description: Set the rings low and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_ring_straddle_planche_push_up.description: Set the rings low and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs straddled to manage balance. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_bodyweight_fingertip_full_planche_pus.description: Place the hands on the floor and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_parallette_wall_supported_handstand_p.description: Kick or walk into a wall-supported handstand with hands under shoulders. Lower the head toward the floor or pad, then press back up. Brace the ribs and keep elbows tracking back instead of flaring wide.

ff_ring_grip_push_up.description: Hold the rings under the shoulders in a plank with straps vertical. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_single_arm_barbell_overhead_press.description: Kneel with one foot forward and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite the front leg. Brace the glutes and press the dumbbell overhead, then lower to shoulder height. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_ring_shoulder_stand.description: Support on the shoulders with hands on the rings or floor as listed and stack the hips over the shoulders. Hold the inverted position with controlled balance and steady breathing. Keep the neck unloaded and exit slowly if balance shifts.

ff_ring_strap_handstand.description: Place the hands shoulder width and kick or press into a stacked handstand against the wall or freestanding. Keep ribs tucked, arms straight, and weight centered over the hands. Avoid arching the low back or letting the shoulders collapse.

ff_ring_handstand.description: Place the hands shoulder width and kick or press into a stacked handstand against the wall or freestanding. Keep ribs tucked, arms straight, and weight centered over the hands. Avoid arching the low back or letting the shoulders collapse.

ff_ring_strap_handstand_push_up.description: Set the rings low enough to control them and kick or press into your handstand with the straps vertical. Bend the elbows to lower the head between the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep ribs tucked and elbows tracking under the hands instead of flaring or arching the low back.

ff_bodyweight_straddle_press_handstand.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Press the load away in the plane named by the exercise, then return under control. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid using momentum from the lower back.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_bodyweight_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_macebell_order_cossack_squat.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Stand in a wide stance and shift to one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_cossack_sq.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Stand in a wide stance and shift to one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_macebell_order_low_switch_cossack_squ.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_macebell_order_foot_elevated_cossack_.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the named position. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_bodyweight_pike_press_handstand.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Press the load away in the plane named by the exercise, then return under control. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid using momentum from the lower back.

ff_parallette_l_sit_to_straddle_press_ha.description: Start in a strong L-sit on the parallettes or rings with shoulders pushed down and legs lifted. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press.

ff_parallette_l_sit_to_pike_press_handst.description: Start in a strong L-sit on the parallettes or rings with shoulders pushed down and legs lifted. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press.

ff_heavy_sandbag_clean_to_push_press.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_ring_tuck_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with shoulders active and legs held in a tight lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull.

ff_ring_advanced_tuck_front_lever_pull_u.description: Hang from the rings with shoulders active and legs held in a tight lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_walking_lu.description: Hold kettlebells at your sides and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep the front heel grounded and avoid leaning toward one weight.

ff_double_clubbell_order_march.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_march.description: Hold the clubbell in the order position at one shoulder and march with high, controlled knee lifts. Keep the torso tall and the bell close to the body. Avoid leaning back as each knee rises.

ff_ring_straddle_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with shoulders active and legs held in a tight lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull.

ff_single_arm_ring_push_up.description: Hold the rings under the shoulders in a plank with straps vertical. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_ring_l_sit_muscle_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_ring_strict_muscle_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_walkin.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_walk.description: Hold the kettlebell in the front rack. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_walkin.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_landmine_isometric_split_s.description: Start tall holding the landmine sleeve. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_inside_pendulum_t.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_clubbell_inside_pendulum.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_clubbell_inside_pendulum_to_outside_p.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_outside_circle.description: Stand tall and guide the clubbell in a controlled circle around the head or body as named by the drill. Keep the grip secure and the elbows soft through the arc. Move slowly and avoid letting the bell pull the ribs open.

ff_alternating_double_clubbell_outside_c.description: Stand tall and guide the clubbell in a controlled circle around the head or body as named by the drill. Keep the grip secure and the elbows soft through the arc. Move slowly and avoid letting the bell pull the ribs open.

ff_double_clubbell_inside_circle_to_outs.description: Stand tall and guide the clubbell in a controlled circle around the head or body as named by the drill. Keep the grip secure and the elbows soft through the arc. Move slowly and avoid letting the bell pull the ribs open.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_kettlebell_romanian_deadlift.description: Hold the kettlebell in the rack or at arm's length as listed. Hinge at the hips with a soft knee bend and lower the load along the legs until hamstrings load, then stand by driving the hips forward. Keep the back long and avoid rounding to chase extra range.

ff_barbell_snatch_balance.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_parallette_straddle_press_to_handstan.description: Start in a strong L-sit on the parallettes or rings with shoulders pushed down and legs lifted. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_march.description: Hold the load in one hand at your side like a suitcase. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_march.description: Stand with a dumbbell beside one foot like a suitcase. Hinge and bend the knees to grip it, then stand tall by pushing the floor away. Keep shoulders square and avoid leaning toward the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_march.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_march.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width with one dumbbell held securely. Squat between the heels, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_march.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_march.description: Kneel with one foot forward and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite the front leg. Brace the glutes and press the dumbbell overhead, then lower to shoulder height. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_barbell_front_rack_march.description: Hold the load in the front rack with elbows forward. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_barbell_back_rack_march.description: Set the bar across the upper back. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_barbell_back_rack_alternating_forward.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_barbell_back_rack_alternating_reverse.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_lateral_.description: Press a light barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as you bend the standing knee, then pull back to stand tall. Keep the bar stacked over the ribs and avoid letting the chest fall forward.

ff_barbell_front_rack_alternating_latera.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_barbell_back_rack_alternating_lateral.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_kettlebell_single_leg_romanian_deadli.description: Stand on one leg holding the kettlebells with a soft knee. Hinge by sending the free leg back and lowering the load toward the floor. Keep the hips square and avoid opening the pelvis to chase more depth.

ff_single_arm_ring_eccentric_chin_up.description: Hang from the rings with palms facing you, arms long, and shoulders active. Pull the chest toward the rings by driving the elbows down, then lower until the arms are long again. Keep the body quiet and avoid kicking or shrugging through the top.

ff_parallette_freestanding_handstand.description: Place the hands shoulder width and kick or press into a stacked handstand against the wall or freestanding. Keep ribs tucked, arms straight, and weight centered over the hands. Avoid arching the low back or letting the shoulders collapse.

ff_ring_gironda_sternum_chin_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_ipsi.description: Hold the load in the front rack with elbows forward. Hold the load on the same side as the working leg. Stand on one leg with the free leg reaching forward, sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Hold the load on the side opposite the working leg. Stand on one leg with the free leg reaching forward, sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts.

Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Hold the load on the same side as the working leg. Stand on one leg with the free leg reaching forward, sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_pistol_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close holding a kettlebell by the horns. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_contra.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_ipsila.description: Hold the load in the front rack with elbows forward. Hold the load on the same side as the working leg. Stand on one leg with the free leg reaching forward, sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Hold the load on the same side as the working leg. Stand on one leg with the free leg reaching forward, sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts.

ff_double_kettlebell_l_sit.description: Press down through the hands on the floor, parallettes, or bells and lift the hips. Extend or tuck the legs in front of you to hold the L-sit shape. Keep the shoulders depressed and avoid rounding the upper back.

ff_double_kettlebell_l_sit_lift_over.description: Press down through the hands on the floor, parallettes, or bells and lift the hips. Extend or tuck the legs in front of you to hold the L-sit shape. Keep the shoulders depressed and avoid rounding the upper back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_wind.description: Set up half kneeling with the load in the hand opposite or above the front leg as listed. Push the hips back and reach the free hand toward the inside of the leg as the loaded shoulder stays stacked. Stand by driving the hips through. Keep the weight over the shoulder and avoid folding forward with a rounded back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_bent.description: Lie on the floor with the kettlebell or kettlebells held bottoms-up over the elbows. Press to straight arms, then lower until the upper arms touch the floor. Keep wrists vertical and avoid letting the bells tip.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_half.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_ring_l_sit_archer_pull_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_march.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_march.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_alternatin.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_alternat.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_alternatin.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_alternating_.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.
Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_alternatin.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_alternating_lateral_l.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_alternating_forward_l.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_alternating_reverse_l.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step one foot back into a reverse lunge. Drive through the front foot to stand, then alternate legs. Keep shoulders level and avoid swinging the weights.

ff_clubbell_order_alternating_lateral_lu.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_clubbell_order_alternating_forward_lu.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_clubbell_order_alternating_reverse_lu.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_clubbell_order_alternating_lat.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_clubbell_order_alternating_for.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_clubbell_order_alternating_rev.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_alternating.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step into the lunge pattern named by the drill and push through the front foot to return. Keep the torso tall and the mace stacked over the shoulder.
Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step into the lunge pattern named by the drill and push through the front foot to return. Keep the torso tall and the mace stacked over the shoulder.
Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step into the lunge pattern named by the drill and push through the front foot to return. Keep the torso tall and the mace stacked over the shoulder.

ff_landmine_low_hold_alternating_lateral.description: Hold the landmine low at the chest or inside shoulder and stand tall. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, then push the floor away to return. Keep the opposite leg long and avoid letting the working knee dive inward.

ff_landmine_goblet_alternating_lateral_l.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_landmine_goblet_alternating_reverse_l.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step one foot back into a reverse lunge. Drive through the front foot to stand, then alternate legs. Keep shoulders level and avoid swinging the weights.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_lateral_lu.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_forward_lu.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_reverse_lu.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_altern.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_cyclist_sq.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Squat between the heels, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_cyclist_squa.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close with two kettlebells overhead. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_cyclis.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width with one dumbbell held securely. Squat between the heels, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_cyclist_.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell overhead. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_lateral_lunge.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_forward_lunge.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_reverse_lunge.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_macebell_order_cyclist_squat.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Stand with heels elevated and a narrow stance, sit the knees forward and hips down, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the torso upright and avoid losing balance off the elevated surface.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_squat.description: Stand in a wide stance with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_squat.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_macebell_order_squat.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_bulgarian_.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load in the front rack. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the elbows forward and the load close to the ribs.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_bulgarian_sp.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_ipsilate.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load on the same side as the front leg. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_contrala.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load on the side opposite the front leg. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_ipsilate.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down, then hold the load at the side. Hold the load on the same side as the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the load at your side without leaning toward it.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_contra.description: Hold the load in the front rack with elbows forward. Hold the load on the side opposite the working leg. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_ipsila.description: Hold the load in the front rack with elbows forward. Hold the load on the same side as the working leg. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_clubbell_order_march.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_macebell_order_walking_lunge.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_suspension_alternating_lateral_lunge.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_alternating_l.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_walking_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_alternating_f.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_alternating_la.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_alternating_la.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_alternating_latera.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_walking_lunge.description: Hold the kettlebell in the front rack. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_alternating_forwar.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_alternating_revers.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_plate_overhead_walking_lunge.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_plate_overhead_alternating_lateral_lu.description: Press a light barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as you bend the standing knee, then pull back to stand tall. Keep the bar stacked over the ribs and avoid letting the chest fall forward.

ff_plate_overhead_alternating_forward_lu.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_plate_overhead_alternating_reverse_lu.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_barbell_single_leg_standing_bent_knee.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_single_arm_barbell_single_leg_standin.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_bodyweight_tuck_planche.description: Lean the shoulders forward past the hands with elbows locked. Press the floor or rings away and hold the listed planche or lever shape with tight ribs. Keep the shoulder blades spread and avoid jumping into a bent-elbow hold.

ff_single_arm_barbell_single_leg_standin.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_double_kettlebell_push_up_to_l_sit.description: Sit tall with legs straight and two kettlebells held in the front rack. Roll down only as far as you can control, sit back up, then press both bells overhead from the Z-sit. Keep the ribs down during the press and avoid using leg momentum.

ff_bar_hanging_l_sit.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and raise both legs to an L-sit height you can control. Alternate small flutter kicks while keeping the torso still. Lower the legs if your grip slips or the body starts swinging.

ff_ring_hanging_l_sit.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and raise both legs to an L-sit height you can control. Alternate small flutter kicks while keeping the torso still. Lower the legs if your grip slips or the body starts swinging.

ff_single_arm_bar_hanging_l_sit.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and raise both legs to an L-sit height you can control. Alternate small flutter kicks while keeping the torso still. Lower the legs if your grip slips or the body starts swinging.

ff_single_arm_ring_hanging_l_sit.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and raise both legs to an L-sit height you can control. Alternate small flutter kicks while keeping the torso still. Lower the legs if your grip slips or the body starts swinging.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_altern.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_parallette_l_sit_flutter_kicks.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and raise both legs to an L-sit height you can control. Alternate small flutter kicks while keeping the torso still. Lower the legs if your grip slips or the body starts swinging.

ff_bodyweight_single_arm_contralateral_s.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_parallette_tuck_planche_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_ring_maltese_push_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_ring_tuck_planche_push_up.description: Set the rings low and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_parallette_l_sit_to_tuck_planche_push.description: Start in an L-sit on parallettes with arms straight and shoulders pressed down. Pull the knees in, lean the shoulders forward, and shift into a tuck planche before returning with control. Keep the elbows locked and avoid dropping the feet between positions.

ff_ring_l_sit_to_tuck_planche_push_up.description: Start in an L-sit on parallettes with arms straight and shoulders pressed down. Pull the knees in, lean the shoulders forward, and shift into a tuck planche before returning with control. Keep the elbows locked and avoid dropping the feet between positions.

ff_bar_advanced_tuck_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with shoulders active and legs held in a tight lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull.

ff_bar_gironda_sternum_chin_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.
