ff_bar_l_sit_archer_pull_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_bar_straddle_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the bar with shoulders active and legs held in a tight lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull.

ff_bar_strict_muscle_up.description: Start hanging below the bar or rings with a strong false grip if needed. Pull the chest above the hands, transition over the bar, and press to support. Keep the rings close and avoid a big kip if strict form is the goal.

ff_bar_tuck_front_lever_pull_up.description: Hang from the rings with shoulders active and legs held in a tight lever position. Pull the chest toward the hands while keeping the hips lifted, then lower back to a straight-arm hang. Use a tuck if needed, and avoid swinging the legs to start the pull.

ff_bodyweight_tuck_planche_push_up.description: Place the hands on the floor and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_double_clubbell_asynchronous_mill.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_clubbell_order_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_clubbell_order_alternating_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_bodyweight_l_sit.description: Press down through the hands on the floor, parallettes, or bells and lift the hips. Extend or tuck the legs in front of you to hold the L-sit shape. Keep the shoulders depressed and avoid rounding the upper back.

ff_bodyweight_single_arm_l_sit.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and raise both legs to an L-sit height you can control. Alternate small flutter kicks while keeping the torso still. Lower the legs if your grip slips or the body starts swinging.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_low.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_alte.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

Hold a kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid rotating the torso.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_parallette_v_sit_to_pike_press_handst.description: Start in a strong L-sit on the parallettes or rings with shoulders pushed down and legs lifted. Shift weight into the hands and press the hips upward until the legs can stack over the shoulders. Finish in a tight handstand, avoiding a loose arch or a jump that bypasses the press.

ff_ring_pseudo_planche_push_up.description: Set the rings low and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_contralater.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_low_switch_.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_alternating.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_foot_elevat.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_stability_ball_feet_elevated_glute_br.description: Lie on your back with one heel on a stability ball and the other leg lifted. Press the heel into the ball to raise the hips, then lower without letting the ball roll away. Keep the pelvis square and the ribs down.

ff_bodyweight_mountain_climber.description: Start in a high plank with shoulders over wrists. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep a steady plank as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high.

ff_suspension_feet_elevated_mountain_cli.description: Start in a high plank with shoulders over wrists. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep a steady plank as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high.

ff_bodyweight_straddle_l_sit.description: Press down through the hands on the floor, parallettes, or bells and lift the hips. Extend or tuck the legs in front of you to hold the L-sit shape. Keep the shoulders depressed and avoid rounding the upper back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_bodyweight_cossack_squat.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Stand in a wide stance and shift to one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_bodyweight_wall_facing_handstand_push.description: Kick or walk into a wall-supported handstand with hands under shoulders. Lower the head toward the floor or pad, then press back up. Brace the ribs and keep elbows tracking back instead of flaring wide.

ff_ring_pelican_push_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_barbell_back_rack_alternating_step_up.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the back rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_barbell_front_rack_alternating_step_u.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_ring_feet_elevated_pelican_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_suspension_pelican_push_up.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_ring_tuck_l_sit.description: Start in an L-sit on parallettes with arms straight and shoulders pressed down. Pull the knees in, lean the shoulders forward, and shift into a tuck planche before returning with control. Keep the elbows locked and avoid dropping the feet between positions.

ff_ring_hanging_tuck_l_sit.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and raise both legs to an L-sit height you can control. Alternate small flutter kicks while keeping the torso still. Lower the legs if your grip slips or the body starts swinging.

ff_bar_hanging_tuck_l_sit.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and raise both legs to an L-sit height you can control. Alternate small flutter kicks while keeping the torso still. Lower the legs if your grip slips or the body starts swinging.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_altern.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.
Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_ring_mountain_climber.description: Start in a high plank with shoulders over wrists. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep a steady plank as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high.

ff_ring_feet_elevated_mountain_climber.description: Start in a high plank with shoulders over wrists. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep a steady plank as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_altern.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

ff_ring_dead_hang.description: Hang from the bar or rings with arms long and shoulders relaxed but engaged. Hold the listed hang position while breathing steadily. Avoid shrugging into the ears or swinging the body.

ff_parallette_kneeling_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders with arms straight. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_clubbell_outside_circle.description: Stand tall and guide the clubbell in a controlled circle around the head or body as named by the drill. Keep the grip secure and the elbows soft through the arc. Move slowly and avoid letting the bell pull the ribs open.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_carry.description: Hold the load in the listed carry position and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_clubbell_order_carry.description: Hold the load in the listed carry position and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_macebell_order_carry.description: Hold the load in the listed carry position and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_macebell_order_march.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_march.description: Use the setup implied by Single Arm Macebell Order March: stable base, controlled tempo, and full-body bracing. Move through each phase of the exercise without rushing the transition. Keep form strict and reduce load or range if balance or control breaks down.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_stability_ball_ts_ys_is.description: Position the stability ball under the listed contact point. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_resistance_band_reverse_fly.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Pull or raise the arms back with elbows bent or slightly soft as the setup requires, squeezing the upper back. Use a light enough load to keep the shoulders down and avoid shrugging.

ff_resistance_band_lateral_raise.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Raise the arms in the plane named by the exercise to shoulder height or slightly above, then lower with control. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight with momentum.

ff_double_dumbbell_lateral_raise.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Raise the arms in the plane named by the exercise to shoulder height or slightly above, then lower with control. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight with momentum.

ff_single_arm_resistance_band_standing_s.description: Sit tall with one elbow supported at shoulder height and bent to 90 degrees. Rotate the dumbbell upward and backward, then lower slowly to the start. Keep the elbow fixed and use a light weight instead of twisting the torso.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_lateral_raise.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Raise the arms in the plane named by the exercise to shoulder height or slightly above, then lower with control. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight with momentum.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_side_lying_should.description: Sit tall with one elbow supported at shoulder height and bent to 90 degrees. Rotate the dumbbell upward and backward, then lower slowly to the start. Keep the elbow fixed and use a light weight instead of twisting the torso.

ff_miniband_standing_shoulder_external_r.description: Sit tall with one elbow supported at shoulder height and bent to 90 degrees. Rotate the dumbbell upward and backward, then lower slowly to the start. Keep the elbow fixed and use a light weight instead of twisting the torso.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_side_lying_should.description: Sit tall with one elbow supported at shoulder height and bent to 90 degrees. Rotate the dumbbell upward and backward, then lower slowly to the start. Keep the elbow fixed and use a light weight instead of twisting the torso.

ff_stability_ball_double_dumbbell_seated.description: Lie with upper back on a stability ball and feet planted wide. Hold one dumbbell at chest height and press it over the shoulder, then lower until the elbow is near the ball. Keep hips lifted and avoid rolling toward the weighted side.

ff_single_arm_cable_lateral_raise.description: Stand facing away from a low cable with one handle in hand. Raise the arm forward to shoulder height, then lower without letting the cable pull you back. Keep the shoulder down and avoid leaning.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_overhead_press.description: Set a half-kneeling stance with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_press.description: Set a half-kneeling stance with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_double_dumbbell_shrug.description: Stand tall holding the load at the sides or in the rack. Elevate the shoulders straight up toward the ears, pause briefly, then lower slowly. Keep the arms straight and avoid rolling the shoulders forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_standing_scaption.description: Hold light dumbbells at the sides with thumbs pointing up. Raise the arms in a wide V to about shoulder height, then lower with control. Keep the ribs down and avoid shrugging as the arms lift.

ff_ez_bar_kneeling_rollout.description: Kneel or stand with the rollout tool under the shoulders and brace the core. Roll out as far as you can while keeping the hips from sagging, then pull back with the abs and lats. Shorten the range if the low back arches.

ff_ez_bar_standing_rollout.description: Kneel or stand with the rollout tool under the shoulders and brace the core. Roll out as far as you can while keeping the hips from sagging, then pull back with the abs and lats. Shorten the range if the low back arches.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_scaption.description: Hold light dumbbells at the sides with thumbs pointing up. Raise the arms in a wide V to about shoulder height, then lower with control. Keep the ribs down and avoid shrugging as the arms lift.

ff_stability_ball_double_dumbbell_seated.description: Lie with upper back on a stability ball and feet planted wide. Hold one dumbbell at chest height and press it over the shoulder, then lower until the elbow is near the ball. Keep hips lifted and avoid rolling toward the weighted side.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_bicep_cur.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_hammer_cu.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_cross_bod.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_incline_bench_pre.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_bicep_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_zottman_curl.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_dumbbell_waiter_curl.description: Hold the dumbbells in a stable start position. Curl the weight through the range named by the exercise, then lower with control. Keep the upper arm still and avoid swinging the load.

ff_cable_straight_bar_drag_curl.description: Set a straight bar on a high cable and stand facing the stack. Keep elbows high as you curl the bar toward the forehead, then straighten the arms. Do not let the elbows drift down as fatigue builds.

ff_cable_rope_hammer_curl.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_lateral.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_suspension_bicep_curl.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_double_dumbbell_tricep_kickback.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Set the elbows in a fixed position and extend the arms to straighten, then bend back under control. Keep the upper arms still and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.

ff_tire_jump.description: Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep. Use a height or distance you can land quietly.

ff_tire_jump_through.description: Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep. Use a height or distance you can land quietly.

ff_tire_broad_jump.description: Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep. Use a height or distance you can land quietly.

ff_tire_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_tire_alternating_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_tire_alternating_toe_taps_around_the_.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Guide the bell around the body and catch it high near the shoulder before passing or lowering it. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back.

ff_dumbbell_seated_overhead_tricep_exten.description: Sit tall holding an EZ bar overhead with elbows pointing forward. Bend the elbows to lower the bar behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_tire_lateral_jump_through.description: Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep. Use a height or distance you can land quietly.

ff_tire_carry.description: Hold the load in the listed carry position and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_dumbbell_overhead_tricep_extension.description: Sit tall holding an EZ bar overhead with elbows pointing forward. Bend the elbows to lower the bar behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_double_dumbbell_close_grip_floor_pres.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

ff_barbell_back_rack_carry.description: Hold the load in the listed carry position and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_single_arm_barbell_suitcase_carry.description: Hold the load at your side like a suitcase and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_suspension_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_suspension_bulgarian_split_squat_jump.description: Set the rear foot on a bench or ball using bodyweight only. Bend the front knee and lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep hips square and avoid letting the front heel lift.

ff_bodyweight_lateral_bear_crawl.description: Set up on hands and feet with knees bent under the hips. Move forward or laterally by taking short, controlled steps while keeping the back flat. Keep the knees low and avoid rocking side to side.

ff_bodyweight_step_up_jump.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_tire_step_up_jump.description: Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep. Use a height or distance you can land quietly.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_step_up_jump.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_tire_alternating_step_up_jump.description: Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep. Use a height or distance you can land quietly.

ff_barbell_power_clean_to_push_press.description: Start with the bar over the midfoot and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the shoulders, stand tall, then press it overhead. Keep the bar close and reset your brace before the press.

ff_double_dumbbell_close_grip_bench_pres.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

ff_double_clubbell_inside_pendulum.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_outside_pendulum.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_double_clubbell_outside_pendulum.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_double_clubbell_inside_pendulum_to_ou.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_clubbell_outside_pendulum.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_double_clubbell_inside_circle.description: Stand tall and guide the clubbell in a controlled circle around the head or body as named by the drill. Keep the grip secure and the elbows soft through the arc. Move slowly and avoid letting the bell pull the ribs open.

ff_double_clubbell_outside_circle.description: Stand tall and guide the clubbell in a controlled circle around the head or body as named by the drill. Keep the grip secure and the elbows soft through the arc. Move slowly and avoid letting the bell pull the ribs open.

ff_clubbell_side_to_side_swing.description: Stand tall with the kettlebell to one side and feet wide enough to let the hips hinge. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_double_dumbbell_close_grip_incline_be.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

ff_cable_rope_tricep_pushdown.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Set the elbows in a fixed position and extend the arms to straighten, then bend back under control. Keep the upper arms still and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.

ff_barbell_incline_push_up.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bodyweight_kneeling_push_up.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_suspension_push_up.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_suspension_chest_fly.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_stability_ball_double_dumbbell_chest_.description: Lie with upper back on a stability ball and feet planted wide. Hold one dumbbell at chest height and press it over the shoulder, then lower until the elbow is near the ball. Keep hips lifted and avoid rolling toward the weighted side.

ff_stability_ball_dumbbell_pullover.description: Position the stability ball under the listed contact point. Guide the load in a controlled arc around or over the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare.

ff_tire_feet_elevated_glute_bridge.description: Lie on your back with both heels in the suspension straps and knees bent. Press the heels down to lift the hips, then lower slowly while the straps stay quiet. Keep ribs tucked and avoid letting the knees drift apart or collapse inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_chest_f.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Row the weights toward the ribs, pause, and lower until arms are long. Keep the chest glued to the bench and avoid shrugging at the top.

ff_suspension_low_row.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Pull the load toward the torso by driving the elbows back, then return until the arms are long. Keep the chest up and avoid rocking the torso to move the weight.

ff_tire_flip.description: Set the feet close to the tire or sandbag and hinge to grip it securely. Drive through the legs and hips to lift, then push or heave the load over as the drill describes. Keep the back braced and avoid rounding during the pull.

ff_tire_in_and_out_squat_jump.description: Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep. Use a height or distance you can land quietly.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_forward.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_feet_elevate.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_suspension_mid_row.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Pull the load toward the torso by driving the elbows back, then return until the arms are long. Keep the chest up and avoid rocking the torso to move the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_calf_raise.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Raise the arms in the plane named by the exercise to shoulder height or slightly above, then lower with control. Keep the ribs down and avoid swinging the weight with momentum.

ff_barbell_seated_calf_raise.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Raise the arms in the pattern named by the exercise, then lower slowly. Use a load you can control without shrugging the shoulders.

ff_double_clubbell_order_feet_elevated_c.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_clubbell_order_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_clubbell_order_feet_elevated_calf_rai.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_clubbell_order_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_feet_elevat.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_feet_elevate.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_bodyweight_calf_raise.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Raise the arms in the pattern named by the exercise, then lower slowly. Use a load you can control without shrugging the shoulders.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_foot_elevate.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_single_leg_c.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_bodyweight_single_leg_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_barbell_front_rack_feet_elevated_calf.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_reverse.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_l_sit_flutter_kicks.description: Hang from rings with shoulders active and raise both legs to an L-sit height you can control. Alternate small flutter kicks while keeping the torso still. Lower the legs if your grip slips or the body starts swinging.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_feet_eleva.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_barbell_back_rack_feet_elevated_calf_.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_tire_mountain_climber.description: Start in a high plank with shoulders over wrists. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep a steady plank as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high.

ff_bodyweight_wall_supported_tibialis_ra.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Raise the arms in the pattern named by the exercise, then lower slowly. Use a load you can control without shrugging the shoulders.

ff_cable_seated_tibialis_raise.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Raise the arms in the pattern named by the exercise, then lower slowly. Use a load you can control without shrugging the shoulders.

ff_cable_seated_single_leg_tibialis_rais.description: Anchor the band in front of you and loop it over the forefoot while standing on one leg or sitting tall. Pull the toes toward the shin against the band, then lower slowly. Keep the knee still and avoid curling the toes to fake the motion.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_walking_lunge.description: Hold one dumbbell or kettlebell vertically at the chest. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_dumbbell_seated_tibialis_raise.description: Hold the dumbbells in a stable start position. Raise the arms in the pattern named by the exercise, then lower slowly. Use a load you can control without shrugging the shoulders.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_late.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_kettlebell_low_hold_alternating_later.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_feet_elevated_.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_barbell_seated_feet_elevated_calf_rai.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_forw.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_tire_leg_press.description: Set the load in the start position named by the exercise and brace the torso. Press through the listed path until the arms finish overhead or at extension, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back.

ff_cable_isometric_split_squat_pallof_pr.description: Anchor the superband at chest height and step sideways into a split squat stance. Hold the band at the sternum, lower into the split squat, and press the hands straight forward without rotating. Keep the front knee aligned with the toes and resist the band pulling the ribs open.

ff_bodyweight_isometric_split_squat_calf.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_bodyweight_isometric_split_squat_foot.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_isometric_sp.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_reve.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_walking_lunge.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_barbell_back_rack_isometric_split_squ.description: Place a barbell across the upper back and stand in a split stance. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over toes and avoid tipping forward.

Place a barbell across the upper back and stand in a split stance. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over toes and avoid tipping forward.

ff_barbell_front_rack_isometric_split_sq.description: Place a barbell across the upper back and stand in a split stance. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over toes and avoid tipping forward.

Place a barbell across the upper back and stand in a split stance. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over toes and avoid tipping forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_hand_ass.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_kettlebell_straddle_l_sit.description: Press down through the hands on the floor, parallettes, or bells and lift the hips. Extend or tuck the legs in front of you to hold the L-sit shape. Keep the shoulders depressed and avoid rounding the upper back.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_isometric_.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_low_.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width with one dumbbell held securely. Squat between the heels, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_foot.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_bodyweight_squat_jump.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_alternating_.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Keep the working knee over the toes and avoid pushing off the floor leg.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_cable_v_grip_seated_low_row.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Pull the load toward the torso by driving the elbows back, then return until the arms are long. Keep the chest up and avoid rocking the torso to move the weight.

ff_resistance_band_half_kneeling_face_pu.description: Kneel with one foot forward facing a cable set near face height. Pull the rope toward the face with elbows high, then reach forward. Keep hips square and avoid leaning back as the rope approaches.

ff_bodyweight_prone_cobra.description: Lie face-down with hands under the shoulders or at the sides. Lift the chest or arms through the range named by the exercise while keeping the pelvis grounded. Move slowly and avoid cranking the low back.

ff_barbell_hang_power_snatch.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_snatch.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_reverse_fly.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull or raise the arms back with elbows bent or slightly soft as the setup requires, squeezing the upper back. Use a light enough load to keep the shoulders down and avoid shrugging.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_seated_concentrat.description: Stand hinged slightly forward with one dumbbell hanging between the legs. Brace the free hand on the thigh and curl the weight toward the opposite shoulder. Keep the upper arm quiet and avoid twisting the torso for momentum.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_seated_bi.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_front_rai.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_cable_wide_grip_lat_pulldown.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_cable_reverse_grip_lat_pulldown.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_cable_v_grip_lat_pulldown.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_bodyweight_bicycle_crunch.description: Lie on your back and brace the core before moving the arms and legs through the pattern named by the exercise. Move with control and exhale on the hardest phase. Keep the low back from arching off the floor.

ff_bodyweight_broad_jump.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_bodyweight_forearm_plank.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Brace the ribs down and hold the listed core shape while moving arms or legs as the variation requires. Keep the low back from arching off the floor and move slowly enough to stay in control.

ff_parallette_plank.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders with arms straight. Brace the ribs down and hold the listed core shape while moving arms or legs as the variation requires. Keep the low back from arching off the floor and move slowly enough to stay in control.

ff_double_clubbell_clean_to_shoulder_par.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_clubbell_shoulder_park_squat.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_shoulder_park_squ.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_clubbell_swing_to_alternating_shoulde.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_bench_press.description: Kneel with one foot forward and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite the front leg. Brace the glutes and press the dumbbell overhead, then lower to shoulder height. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_bench_press.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

ff_barbell_split_jerk.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_barbell_power_clean_to_split_jerk.description: Start with the bar over midfoot and grip just outside the legs. Pull the bar close, receive it in a front squat, stand, then dip and drive into a split jerk overhead. Keep the bar close and recover the feet before lowering or resetting.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_press.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

ff_barbell_power_jerk.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_barbell_squat_jerk.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_double_kettlebell_bent_over_row.description: Hold a kettlebell in one hand and hinge with the other hand braced if needed. Row the bell toward the hip, then lower until the arm is long. Keep the torso square and avoid yanking with the shoulder.

ff_bodyweight_seated_wall_angels.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_parallette_kneeling_close_grip_push_u.description: Grip parallettes close together under the chest and set a straight plank. Lower between the handles, then press back up. Keep elbows near the ribs and avoid letting the shoulders roll forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_shrug.description: Stand tall holding the load at the sides or in the rack. Elevate the shoulders straight up toward the ears, pause briefly, then lower slowly. Keep the arms straight and avoid rolling the shoulders forward.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_split_squat_ju.description: Set the rear foot on a bench or ball using bodyweight only. Bend the front knee and lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep hips square and avoid letting the front heel lift.

ff_suspension_y_raise.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Pull or raise the arms back with elbows bent or slightly soft as the setup requires, squeezing the upper back. Use a light enough load to keep the shoulders down and avoid shrugging.

ff_bodyweight_standing_wall_angels.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_mountain_climber.description: Start in a high plank with shoulders over wrists. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep a steady plank as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high.

ff_bodyweight_split_squat_jump.description: Set the rear foot on a bench or ball using bodyweight only. Bend the front knee and lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep hips square and avoid letting the front heel lift.

ff_bodyweight_duck_walk.description: Set up on hands and feet with knees bent under the hips. Move forward or laterally by taking short, controlled steps while keeping the back flat. Keep the knees low and avoid rocking side to side.

ff_barbell_front_rack_split_squat.description: Set a split stance with the bar on the front shoulders. Let the front knee travel forward over the toes as you lower, then press through the front foot to rise. Keep the heel planted and move slowly instead of bouncing.

ff_barbell_overhead_split_squat.description: Set a split stance with the bar locked out overhead. Let the front knee travel forward over the toes as you lower, then press through the front foot to rise. Keep the heel planted and move slowly instead of bouncing.

ff_double_dumbbell_split_jerk.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_suspension_reverse_fly.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Pull or raise the arms back with elbows bent or slightly soft as the setup requires, squeezing the upper back. Use a light enough load to keep the shoulders down and avoid shrugging.

ff_double_dumbbell_decline_bench_press.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_split_jerk.description: Stand at the landmine with one hand on the sleeve. Clean the sleeve to the shoulder, then dip and split the feet as you press overhead. Land with both knees soft and avoid pressing before the leg drive finishes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_snat.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch to a bottoms-up overhead catch only with a load you can balance. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_bodyweight_copenhagen_plank_side_rais.description: Place the top knee on a bench and support yourself on the lower forearm in a side plank. Lift the hips and bring the lower leg up toward the bench, then lower and raise the hips with control. Keep the body long and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_bodyweight_copenhagen_plank_with_leg_.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Set up in a side plank with the top leg on a bench as listed, lift the hips, and hold or adduct the lower leg under control. Keep the body long and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_double_dumbbell_chest_fly.description: Lie with upper back on a stability ball and feet planted wide. Hold one dumbbell at chest height and press it over the shoulder, then lower until the elbow is near the ball. Keep hips lifted and avoid rolling toward the weighted side.

ff_double_dumbbell_decline_bench_chest_f.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Row the weights toward the ribs, pause, and lower until arms are long. Keep the chest glued to the bench and avoid shrugging at the top.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_feet_ele.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_bent_over_row.description: Hinge at the hips with a flat back and hold the barbell so the arms hang below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the lower ribs, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the torso quiet and avoid standing up to finish the row.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_calf_rai.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_bicep_curl_to_ove.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_overhead_press.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_floor_press.description: Sit on the floor with legs straight and press two dumbbells together at the chest. Keep the dumbbells squeezed as you press overhead, then lower to the chest. Sit tall and avoid leaning back.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_overhead_tricep_e.description: Sit tall holding an EZ bar overhead with elbows pointing forward. Bend the elbows to lower the bar behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_isometric_.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_bodyweight_single_leg_hip_thrust.description: Set the upper back on a bench with a barbell across the hips and one foot planted. Lift the free leg, drive through the planted heel, and raise the hips until the pelvis is level. Lower under control and avoid rotating toward the working side.

ff_stability_ball_single_leg_hip_thrust.description: Start in a high plank with one shin or foot on a stability ball and the other leg lifted. Pull the supported knee toward the chest, then extend back to plank. Keep hips level and avoid letting the ball drift sideways.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_isometric_.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_hand_a.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_isometri.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_cable_v_grip_seated_floor_lat_pulldow.description: Sit on the floor facing a high cable with one handle overhead. Pull the elbow down toward the ribs, then reach tall again. Sit upright and avoid leaning back to finish the pull.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_isometri.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_wave.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with knees softly bent. Create the wave, slam, or spiral named by the drill while keeping the torso braced. Drive the motion from the shoulders and core instead of swaying the whole body.

ff_cable_rope_tall_kneeling_tricep_pushd.description: Kneel tall facing away from a high cable with the rope behind your head. Extend the elbows until the arms are long, then bend them to return. Keep glutes squeezed and avoid flaring the ribs.

ff_bodyweight_feet_elevated_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_stability_ball_reverse_hyperextension.description: Lie face down over the ball or bench with hips supported and knees bent. Lift the thighs until they line up with the torso, then lower slowly. Squeeze the glutes and avoid kicking into a back arch.

ff_bodyweight_frog_reverse_hyperextensio.description: Lie face-down on the bench or floor with hips at the edge. Lift the legs behind you by squeezing the glutes and hamstrings, then lower slowly. Keep the pelvis stable and avoid swinging the legs for momentum.

ff_bodyweight_reverse_hyperextension.description: Lie face-down on the bench or floor with hips at the edge. Lift the legs behind you by squeezing the glutes and hamstrings, then lower slowly. Keep the pelvis stable and avoid swinging the legs for momentum.

ff_cable_rope_tall_kneeling_low_to_high_.description: Stand tall and hold the kettlebell near one hip. Brace and guide the weight diagonally across the body toward the opposite shoulder. Let the torso rotate only as far as you can control, avoiding a loose lower-back twist.

ff_bodyweight_v_up.description: Lie on your back and brace the core before moving the arms and legs through the pattern named by the exercise. Move with control and exhale on the hardest phase. Keep the low back from arching off the floor.

ff_slider_barbell_front_rack_reverse_lun.description: Hold two kettlebells in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot straight back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep elbows tucked and the front knee aligned over the toes.

ff_slider_barbell_back_rack_reverse_lung.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_slider_reverse_lunge.description: Place the slider under the working foot or limb on a smooth surface. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_barbell_push_jerk.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_barbell_power_clean_to_push_jerk.description: Start with the bar over the midfoot and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the shoulders, stand tall, then press it overhead. Keep the bar close and reset your brace before the press.

ff_barbell_squat_clean_to_push_jerk.description: Start with the bar over midfoot and grip just outside the legs. Pull the bar close, receive it in a front squat, stand, then dip and drive into a split jerk overhead. Keep the bar close and recover the feet before lowering or resetting.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_outside_swing.description: Stand with one kettlebell just in front of you and grip it with one hand. Hike the load back, snap the hips forward, and let it float to about chest height. Keep the arm relaxed and avoid squatting or lifting with the shoulder.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_altern.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_contralateral_f.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Hold the load on the side opposite the working leg. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_alternating_for.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_press.description: Kneel on both knees with two kettlebells in the rack. Squeeze the glutes and press both bells overhead, then lower to the rack. Keep ribs down and avoid sitting back on the heels.

ff_cable_straight_bar_bicep_curl.description: Set a straight bar on a high cable and stand facing the stack. Keep elbows high as you curl the bar toward the forehead, then straighten the arms. Do not let the elbows drift down as fatigue builds.

ff_double_kettlebell_glute_bridge_isomet.description: Lie on your back with dumbbells at chest height and feet planted. Lift into a glute bridge and hold it while pressing the dumbbells over the chest. Keep hips level and avoid flaring the ribs as you press.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack.description: Hold a kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the rack tight and avoid collapsing toward the kettlebell.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_c.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_i.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held on the same side as the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the front foot rooted and avoid tipping toward the weight.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_c.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_i.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the weight drift forward.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_front_rack_rev.description: Hold two kettlebells in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot straight back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep elbows tucked and the front knee aligned over the toes.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_suitcase_rever.description: Hold kettlebells at your sides and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep the front heel grounded and avoid leaning toward one weight.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_overhead_rever.description: Hold two kettlebells overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep wrists stacked over shoulders and use a shorter slide if the ribs flare.

ff_double_dumbbell_hammer_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_bench_pre.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

ff_slider_single_arm_clubbell_order_cont.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_slider_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsi.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_slider_double_clubbell_order_reverse_.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_slider_clubbell_order_reverse_lunge.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_slider_macebell_order_reverse_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_slider_single_arm_macebell_order_cont.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_slider_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsi.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_slider_landmine_goblet_reverse_lunge.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_slider_landmine_shoulder_contralatera.description: Start tall holding the landmine sleeve. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_slider_single_arm_landmine_front_rack.description: Hold a kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid rotating the torso.

Hold a kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the rack tight and avoid collapsing toward the kettlebell.

ff_slider_single_arm_landmine_suitcase_c.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_slider_single_arm_landmine_suitcase_i.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held on the same side as the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the front foot rooted and avoid tipping toward the weight.

ff_slider_single_arm_landmine_overhead_c.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.
