ff_slider_single_arm_landmine_overhead_i.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the weight drift forward.

ff_suspension_squat_jump.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_decline_b.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_miniband_glute_bridge.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Set the upper back on a bench or floor, tuck the ribs, and drive through the heels to lift the hips. Pause at the top and avoid arching the low back for extra height.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_top_down_.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

ff_bar_dead_hang.description: Hang from the bar or rings with arms long and shoulders relaxed but engaged. Hold the listed hang position while breathing steadily. Avoid shrugging into the ears or swinging the body.

ff_trap_bar_deadlift.description: Stand inside the trap bar and grip the handles at your sides. Set the bar over midfoot, brace, and push the floor away while keeping the bar close to the legs through the pull. Keep the lats tight and avoid letting the hips shoot up before the bar leaves the floor.

ff_trap_bar_staggered_stance_deadlift.description: Stand inside the trap bar and grip the handles at your sides. Set the bar over midfoot, brace, and push the floor away while keeping the bar close to the legs through the pull. Keep the lats tight and avoid letting the hips shoot up before the bar leaves the floor.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_bicep_cur.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_landmine_goblet_alternating_curtsy_lu.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_landmine_goblet_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_barbell_overhead_alternating_curtsy_l.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_barbell_back_rack_alternating_curtsy_.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_curtsy_lu.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_trap_bar_suitcase_carry.description: Hold the load at your side like a suitcase and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_trap_bar_romanian_deadlift.description: Stand inside the trap bar and grip the handles at your sides. Hinge at the hips with a soft knee bend and lower the load along the legs until hamstrings load, then stand by driving the hips forward. Keep the back long and avoid rounding to chase extra range.

ff_resistance_band_bicep_curl.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_resistance_band_chest_press.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Press the load away in the plane named by the exercise, then return under control. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid using momentum from the lower back.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_chest_pre.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Press the load away in the plane named by the exercise, then return under control. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid using momentum from the lower back.

ff_trap_bar_deficit_deadlift.description: Stand inside the trap bar and grip the handles at your sides. Set the bar over midfoot, brace, and push the floor away while keeping the bar close to the legs through the pull. Keep the lats tight and avoid letting the hips shoot up before the bar leaves the floor.

ff_trap_bar_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Stand inside the trap bar and grip the handles at your sides. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_trap_bar_squat_jump.description: Stand inside the trap bar and grip the handles at your sides. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_resistance_band_chest_fly.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_battle_rope_power_slam_to_burpee.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and one rope end in each hand. Lift both arms high, then slam the ropes down hard and reset. Brace the trunk and avoid rounding the back as the ropes hit.

ff_resistance_band_low_row.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Pull the load toward the torso by driving the elbows back, then return until the arms are long. Keep the chest up and avoid rocking the torso to move the weight.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_reverse_lunge.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_bodyweight_isometric_split_squat_hold.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_bodyweight_split_squat.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_barbell_frog_pump.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Set the upper back on a bench or floor, tuck the ribs, and drive through the heels to lift the hips. Pause at the top and avoid arching the low back for extra height.

ff_barbell_front_rack_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_barbell_back_rack_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_trap_bar_calf_raise.description: Stand inside the trap bar and grip the handles at your sides. Raise the arms in the pattern named by the exercise, then lower slowly. Use a load you can control without shrugging the shoulders.

ff_barbell_zercher_cyclist_squat.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Stand with heels elevated and a narrow stance, sit the knees forward and hips down, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the torso upright and avoid losing balance off the elevated surface.

ff_trap_bar_rack_pull.description: Set the bar in the rack just below or at knee height and grip it with a braced torso. Drive through the floor to stand tall with hips and knees extended. Keep the bar close and avoid jerking the shoulders back at lockout.

ff_resistance_band_mid_row.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Pull the load toward the torso by driving the elbows back, then return until the arms are long. Keep the chest up and avoid rocking the torso to move the weight.

ff_resistance_band_face_pull.description: Kneel with one foot forward facing a cable set near face height. Pull the rope toward the face with elbows high, then reach forward. Keep hips square and avoid leaning back as the rope approaches.

ff_resistance_band_face_pull_to_overhead.description: Hold suspension handles and lean back with arms straight. Pull the handles toward the face, then rotate and press them overhead before returning. Keep the body rigid and avoid letting hips sag.

ff_single_arm_resistance_band_low_row.description: Kneel facing a low cable with one foot forward and hold the handle in the opposite hand. Row the elbow toward the hip, pause, and return until the shoulder reaches forward. Keep hips square and avoid rotating with the pull.

ff_single_arm_resistance_band_mid_row.description: Kneel facing a low cable with one foot forward and hold the handle in the opposite hand. Row the elbow toward the hip, pause, and return until the shoulder reaches forward. Keep hips square and avoid rotating with the pull.

ff_single_arm_resistance_band_chest_pres.description: Anchor a band in front of you and kneel with one foot forward. With the upper arm lifted, rotate the forearm up and press overhead, then reverse. Keep the band light and avoid arching the back.

ff_resistance_band_reverse_grip_bicep_cu.description: Stand holding an EZ bar with palms facing down. Curl the bar while keeping elbows close to the ribs, then lower to straight arms. Use a lighter load and avoid bending the wrists back.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_reverse_g.description: Stand holding an EZ bar with palms facing down. Curl the bar while keeping elbows close to the ribs, then lower to straight arms. Use a lighter load and avoid bending the wrists back.

ff_resistance_band_hammer_curl.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_battle_rope_jumping_jack.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_battle_rope_squat_jump_power_slam.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and one rope end in each hand. Lift both arms high, then slam the ropes down hard and reset. Brace the trunk and avoid rounding the back as the ropes hit.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_side_slam.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_battle_rope_squat_jump_alternating_wa.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_battle_rope_in_and_out_squat_jump_pow.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and one rope end in each hand. Lift both arms high, then slam the ropes down hard and reset. Brace the trunk and avoid rounding the back as the ropes hit.

ff_battle_rope_in_and_out_squat_jump_alt.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and one rope end in each hand. Lift both arms high, then slam the ropes down hard and reset. Brace the trunk and avoid rounding the back as the ropes hit.

ff_bodyweight_in_and_out_squat_jump.description: Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep. Use a height or distance you can land quietly.

ff_miniband_thigh_in_and_out_squat_jump.description: Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep. Use a height or distance you can land quietly.

ff_miniband_ankle_in_and_out_squat_jump.description: Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep. Use a height or distance you can land quietly.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_hammer_cu.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_resistance_band_y_raise.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Pull or raise the arms back with elbows bent or slightly soft as the setup requires, squeezing the upper back. Use a light enough load to keep the shoulders down and avoid shrugging.

ff_battle_rope_isometric_split_squat_pow.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_split_squat_j.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_resistance_band_cuban_press.description: Anchor a band in front of you and kneel with one foot forward. With the upper arm lifted, rotate the forearm up and press overhead, then reverse. Keep the band light and avoid arching the back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_contra.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_resistance_band_tall_kneeling_cuban_p.description: Anchor a band in front of you and kneel with one foot forward. With the upper arm lifted, rotate the forearm up and press overhead, then reverse. Keep the band light and avoid arching the back.

ff_superband_pull_apart.description: Hold a band at chest height with arms extended. Pull the band apart by squeezing the shoulder blades, then return with control. Keep a slight elbow bend and avoid shrugging the shoulders up.

ff_resistance_band_tall_kneeling_face_pu.description: Kneel with one foot forward facing a cable set near face height. Pull the rope toward the face with elbows high, then reach forward. Keep hips square and avoid leaning back as the rope approaches.

ff_resistance_band_overhead_press.description: Hold suspension handles and lean back with arms straight. Pull the handles toward the face, then rotate and press them overhead before returning. Keep the body rigid and avoid letting hips sag.

ff_resistance_band_alternating_overhead_.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Press the load away in the plane named by the exercise, then return under control. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid using momentum from the lower back.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_overhead_.description: Kneel tall with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Press one dumbbell overhead, lower it, then alternate sides. Squeeze the glutes and avoid shifting the hips.

ff_suspension_skull_crusher.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Set the elbows in a fixed position and extend the arms to straighten, then bend back under control. Keep the upper arms still and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_front_rack_lat.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_suitcase_later.description: Stand tall with dumbbells at your sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_overhead_later.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

ff_double_dumbbell_skull_crusher.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Set the elbows in a fixed position and extend the arms to straighten, then bend back under control. Keep the upper arms still and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_skull_c.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Row the weights toward the ribs, pause, and lower until arms are long. Keep the chest glued to the bench and avoid shrugging at the top.

ff_double_dumbbell_decline_bench_skull_c.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Row the weights toward the ribs, pause, and lower until arms are long. Keep the chest glued to the bench and avoid shrugging at the top.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_skull_crusher.description: Pinch the weight plates together at your sides or in front of the thighs. Set the elbows in a fixed position and extend the arms to straighten, then bend back under control. Keep the upper arms still and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_incline_bench_sku.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Row the weights toward the ribs, pause, and lower until arms are long. Keep the chest glued to the bench and avoid shrugging at the top.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_decline_bench_sku.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Row the weights toward the ribs, pause, and lower until arms are long. Keep the chest glued to the bench and avoid shrugging at the top.

ff_double_dumbbell_bent_over_row.description: Hinge onto one leg with the free leg reaching back and the weight hanging below the shoulder. Row toward the hip while keeping the hips square to the floor, then lower the weight without standing up. Use a light load if balance makes the torso twist.

ff_ez_bar_reverse_grip_bent_over_row.description: Hinge at the hips with a flat back and hold the barbell so the arms hang below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the lower ribs, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the torso quiet and avoid standing up to finish the row.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_ipsila.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up overhead. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Hold the load on the side opposite the working leg. Stand in a wide stance and shift to one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Hold the load on the same side as the working leg. Stand in a wide stance and shift to one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_foot.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_foot_e.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_bent_over_row.description: Hinge onto one leg with the free leg reaching back and the weight hanging below the shoulder. Row toward the hip while keeping the hips square to the floor, then lower the weight without standing up. Use a light load if balance makes the torso twist.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_knee_supported_ro.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then drive back through center and repeat to the other side. Keep the heel down on the working side and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_foot_ele.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_low_switch.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then drive back through center and repeat to the other side. Keep the heel down on the working side and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_foot_eleva.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_dumbbell_pullover.description: Hold the dumbbells in a stable start position. Guide the load in a controlled arc around or over the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_dumbbell_incline_bench_pullover.description: Lie on the bench with shoulder blades set and feet planted. Guide the load in a controlled arc around or over the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then drive back through center and repeat to the other side. Keep the heel down on the working side and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_altern.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up overhead. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_low_sw.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width with one dumbbell held securely. Squat between the heels, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_tire_incline_push_up.description: Place the hands on the tire in an incline push-up position with the body in one line. Lower the chest toward the tire, then press back to straight arms. Keep the core tight and avoid sagging at the hips.

ff_double_dumbbell_close_grip_decline_be.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

ff_bodyweight_eccentric_push_up.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_double_dumbbell_bicep_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_cable_knee_hover_quadruped_fire_hydra.description: Start on hands and knees with the knees hovering an inch above the floor. Keeping the knee bent, lift one thigh out to the side, then return with control. Keep the pelvis square and avoid leaning away from the working hip.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_bicep_c.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_hammer_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_seated_ha.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_hammer_.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_half_kneeling_win.description: Press the kettlebell overhead and angle the feet slightly away from the load. Push the hips back and slide the free hand down the lead leg while keeping eyes on the bell. Return by driving the hips forward and stacking the shoulders.

ff_double_dumbbell_windmill.description: Press the kettlebell overhead and angle the feet slightly away from the load. Push the hips back and slide the free hand down the lead leg while keeping eyes on the bell. Return by driving the hips forward and stacking the shoulders.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_double_dumbbell_cyclist_thruster.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then drive back through center and repeat to the other side. Keep the heel down on the working side and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_cyclist_t.description: Stand with heels elevated and the kettlebell or kettlebells racked at the shoulders. Squat down, stand hard, and use that leg drive to press the weight overhead. Finish tall with ribs down and avoid turning the press into a backbend.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_pistol_.description: Stand in a wide stance holding a dumbbell at the chest. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_pistol_squat.description: Stand in a wide stance holding a dumbbell at the chest. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_pist.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Circle the bell, catch it at chest height, then sit into the squat before standing. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_bent_press.description: Kneel with one foot forward and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite the front leg. Brace the glutes and press the dumbbell overhead, then lower to shoulder height. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_half_kneeling_ben.description: Kneel with one foot forward and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite the front leg. Brace the glutes and press the dumbbell overhead, then lower to shoulder height. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_zottman_curl.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_cable_straight_bar_tricep_pushdown.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Set the elbows in a fixed position and extend the arms to straighten, then bend back under control. Keep the upper arms still and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.

ff_cable_straight_bar_reverse_grip_trice.description: Stand facing a high cable and hold one handle with palm up. Keep the elbow pinned near the side as you press the handle down, then return to about 90 degrees. Keep the wrist straight and avoid leaning over the handle.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_zottman_c.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_seated_zo.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_tall_kneeling_ove.description: Sit tall holding an EZ bar overhead with elbows pointing forward. Bend the elbows to lower the bar behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_press.description: Kneel with one foot forward and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite the front leg. Brace the glutes and press the dumbbell overhead, then lower to shoulder height. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_seated_overhead_p.description: Kneel with one foot forward and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite the front leg. Brace the glutes and press the dumbbell overhead, then lower to shoulder height. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_bicep_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_zottman.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_seated_ov.description: Kneel tall with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Press one dumbbell overhead, lower it, then alternate sides. Squeeze the glutes and avoid shifting the hips.

ff_double_kettlebell_floor_press.description: Lie on the floor with the kettlebell or kettlebells held bottoms-up over the elbows. Press to straight arms, then lower until the upper arms touch the floor. Keep wrists vertical and avoid letting the bells tip.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_floor_press.description: Lie on the floor with the kettlebell or kettlebells held bottoms-up over the elbows. Press to straight arms, then lower until the upper arms touch the floor. Keep wrists vertical and avoid letting the bells tip.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_floor_p.description: Lie on the floor with the kettlebell or kettlebells held bottoms-up over the elbows. Press to straight arms, then lower until the upper arms touch the floor. Keep wrists vertical and avoid letting the bells tip.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_ipsila.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width with one dumbbell held securely. Squat between the heels, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_alternat.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width with one dumbbell held securely. Squat between the heels, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_seated_overhead_t.description: Sit tall holding an EZ bar overhead with elbows pointing forward. Bend the elbows to lower the bar behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bent_ov.description: Hold a kettlebell in one hand and hinge with the other hand braced if needed. Row the bell toward the hip, then lower until the arm is long. Keep the torso square and avoid yanking with the shoulder.

ff_battle_rope_double_wave.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with knees softly bent. Create the wave, slam, or spiral named by the drill while keeping the torso braced. Drive the motion from the shoulders and core instead of swaying the whole body.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_prone_l.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with light dumbbells hanging. Raise the arms diagonally into a Y, thumbs up, then lower slowly. Keep the neck long and avoid shrugging toward the ears.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_seated_bicep_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_prone_h.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_low_swit.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width with one dumbbell held securely. Squat between the heels, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_foot_elevated_cossack.description: Stand in a wide stance holding the landmine end at the chest. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsilateral.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_foot_elevat.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsilateral.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_foot_elevat.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_single_arm_dummbell_front_rack_foot_e.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_foot_e.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_incline_b.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_incline_bench_pre.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_decline_bench_pre.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebell at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_cope.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_double_dumbbell_floor_press.description: Lie on your back with dumbbells at chest height and feet planted. Lift into a glute bridge and hold it while pressing the dumbbells over the chest. Keep hips level and avoid flaring the ribs as you press.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_glute_br.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_floor_pre.description: Lie on your back with dumbbells at chest height and feet planted. Lift into a glute bridge and hold it while pressing the dumbbells over the chest. Keep hips level and avoid flaring the ribs as you press.

ff_kettlebell_seated_single_leg_tibialis.description: Anchor the band in front of you and loop it over the forefoot while standing on one leg or sitting tall. Pull the toes toward the shin against the band, then lower slowly. Keep the knee still and avoid curling the toes to fake the motion.

ff_double_kettlebell_seated_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_double_kettlebell_seated_feet_elevate.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_thruster.description: Stand with heels elevated and the kettlebell or kettlebells racked at the shoulders. Squat down, stand hard, and use that leg drive to press the weight overhead. Finish tall with ribs down and avoid turning the press into a backbend.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_floor_press.description: Kneel with one foot forward and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite the front leg. Brace the glutes and press the dumbbell overhead, then lower to shoulder height. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_single_arm_macebell_inside_circle.description: Stand tall holding the clubbell in one hand near the shoulder. Guide it in a controlled inside arc around the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare or the wrist collapse.

ff_single_arm_macebell_outside_circle.description: Hold the mace near the head and trace a controlled circle around the head or body. Keep the elbows soft and the core braced as the mace moves. Use a lighter mace until both directions feel even.

ff_single_arm_macebell_inside_circle_to_.description: Hold the mace near the head and trace a controlled circle around the head or body. Keep the elbows soft and the core braced as the mace moves. Use a lighter mace until both directions feel even.

ff_macebell_inside_circle.description: Hold the mace near the head and trace a controlled circle around the head or body. Keep the elbows soft and the core braced as the mace moves. Use a lighter mace until both directions feel even.

ff_macebell_outside_circle.description: Hold the mace near the head and trace a controlled circle around the head or body. Keep the elbows soft and the core braced as the mace moves. Use a lighter mace until both directions feel even.

ff_macebell_inside_circle_to_outside_cir.description: Hold the mace near the head and trace a controlled circle around the head or body. Keep the elbows soft and the core braced as the mace moves. Use a lighter mace until both directions feel even.

ff_battle_rope_clap.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with knees softly bent. Create the wave, slam, or spiral named by the drill while keeping the torso braced. Drive the motion from the shoulders and core instead of swaying the whole body.

ff_battle_rope_inward_circle.description: Stand facing the rope anchor with feet about shoulder width and a rope end in each hand. Brace the trunk and make alternating inward circles, keeping the circles smooth and continuous. Keep the elbows slightly bent and avoid twisting the torso to create the motion.

ff_battle_rope_outward_circle.description: Stand facing the rope anchor with feet about shoulder width and one rope end in each hand. Brace the trunk and make alternating outward circles, letting one arm circle out as the other returns. Keep the knees soft and shoulders down instead of shrugging as the ropes move.

ff_double_kettlebell_tall_kneeling_shrug.description: Kneel on both knees with two kettlebells in the rack. Squeeze the glutes and press both bells overhead, then lower to the rack. Keep ribs down and avoid sitting back on the heels.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_bent_over.description: Hinge onto one leg with the free leg reaching back and the weight hanging below the shoulder. Row toward the hip while keeping the hips square to the floor, then lower the weight without standing up. Use a light load if balance makes the torso twist.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_cossack_squat.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_bodyweight_low_switch_cossack_squat.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_cossack_s.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar across the upper back. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_barbell_zercher_cossack_squat.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar across the upper back. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_barbell_zercher_low_switch_cossack_sq.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_zercher_foot_elevated_cossack.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the named position. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_barbell_zombie_squat.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_barbell_zombie_split_squat.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_cossack_squat.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_alternating_co.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_contralateral_.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_alternating_co.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_ipsilateral_co.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_barbell_zombie_cyclist_squat.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Stand with heels elevated and a narrow stance, sit the knees forward and hips down, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the torso upright and avoid losing balance off the elevated surface.

ff_single_arm_suspension_foot_elevated_i.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_single_arm_suspension_ipsilateral_cos.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_battle_rope_low_switch_cossack_squat_.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_plate_overhead_cossack_squat.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Stand in a wide stance and shift to one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_plate_overhead_alternating_cossack_sq.description: Hold the kettlebells in the front rack and take a wide stance. Shift into one hip, bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then drive back through center and repeat to the other side. Keep the heel down on the working side and avoid collapsing the knee inward.

ff_plate_overhead_low_switch_cossack_squ.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_plate_overhead_foot_elevated_cossack_.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the named position. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_cossack_squat.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load as listed. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_alternating_c.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load as listed. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_low_switch_co.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_foot_elevated.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar across the upper back. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_cossack_squat.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar on the front shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_alternating_cossac.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar on the front shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_low_switch_cossack.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_foot_elevated_coss.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar across the upper back. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_bulgarian_spl.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load as listed. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_plate_dead_bug.description: Lie on your back and brace the core before moving the arms and legs through the pattern named by the exercise. Move with control and exhale on the hardest phase. Keep the low back from arching off the floor.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_bulgarian_split_sq.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load in the front rack. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the elbows forward and the load close to the ribs.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_bulgarian_split_squ.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load as listed. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_plate_russian_twist.description: Lie on your back and brace the core before moving the arms and legs through the pattern named by the exercise. Move with control and exhale on the hardest phase. Keep the low back from arching off the floor.

ff_plate_feet_elevated_russian_twist.description: Set up kneeling or seated holding the landmine sleeve. Rotate the handle from side to side while the hips stay steady. Move from the ribs and avoid yanking with only the arms.

ff_bodyweight_feet_elevated_russian_twis.description: Set up kneeling or seated holding the landmine sleeve. Rotate the handle from side to side while the hips stay steady. Move from the ribs and avoid yanking with only the arms.

ff_bodyweight_russian_twist.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Rotate through the thoracic spine or hips as the setup requires while keeping the base stable. Turn from the mid-back and hips instead of cranking the low back.

ff_landmine_russian_twist.description: Anchor the bar in a landmine and hold the free end at chest or shoulder height. Rotate through the thoracic spine or hips as the setup requires while keeping the base stable. Turn from the mid-back and hips instead of cranking the low back.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_hawaiian_squat.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_bodyweight_hawaiian_squat.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_suspension_hawaiian_squat.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_hawaiian_squ.description: Stand in a split stance near a support you can hold lightly. Bend both knees to lower straight down, then press through the front foot to stand. Use the hand for balance only and keep the front knee tracking over toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_incline_bench_press.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_incline_bench_p.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebell at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_incline.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_seated_overhead.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Brace tall and press the load overhead until the arms are vertical, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_knee_supported_.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_macebell_offset_thruster.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_single_arm_dummbell_overhead_foot_ele.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_seated_overhead_pre.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Brace tall and press the load overhead until the arms are vertical, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_cyclist_squa.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_cyclist_.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close, holding the kettlebell at your sides. Sit the knees forward and down while keeping the torso tall, then stand through the midfoot. Keep the knees tracking over the toes and avoid rocking onto the toes.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_seated_.description: Stand or kneel in the stance named by the exercise with the load at shoulder height. Press from the shoulder or rack position until the arm is stacked overhead. Lower under control and keep the ribs tucked instead of leaning back.

ff_double_kettlebell_bench_press.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_cyclist_squ.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_plate_overhead_cyclist_squat.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Stand with heels elevated and a narrow stance, sit the knees forward and hips down, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the torso upright and avoid losing balance off the elevated surface.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_cyclist_squat.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load as listed. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_cyclist_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell in the front rack. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_cyclist_squat.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_cyclist_squat.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_landmine_goblet_cyclist_squat.description: Hold one dumbbell or kettlebell vertically at the chest. Stand with heels elevated and a narrow stance, sit the knees forward and hips down, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the torso upright and avoid losing balance off the elevated surface.

ff_double_dumbbell_tall_kneeling_shrug.description: Kneel tall with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bench_press.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebell at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_pistol_squat.description: Hold one dumbbell or kettlebell vertically at the chest. Stand on one leg with the free leg reaching forward, sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_alternating_pistol_sq.description: Stand in a wide stance holding a dumbbell at the chest. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bench_p.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_pistol_squat.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Stand on one leg with the free leg reaching forward, sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts.

ff_barbell_zombie_pistol_squat.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Stand on one leg with the free leg reaching forward, sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts.

ff_barbell_front_rack_pistol_squat.description: Hold the bar in the front rack with elbows high and feet about shoulder width. Squat between the heels, then drive up while keeping the elbows lifted. Avoid letting the bar roll forward.

ff_barbell_back_rack_pistol_squat.description: Place a barbell across the upper back and stand in a split stance. Lower straight down until both knees bend, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over toes and avoid tipping forward.

ff_barbell_zercher_pistol_squat.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Stand on one leg with the free leg reaching forward, sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_front_rack_squat.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width with one dumbbell held securely. Squat between the heels, then drive through the floor to stand. Keep the chest tall and avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_decline_bench_press.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_decline_bench_p.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebell at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_squat.description: Set the feet in the stance named by the exercise and brace before descending. Sit the hips down with the chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_squat.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar on the front shoulders. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_squat.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load as listed. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_alternating_cossack.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar across the upper back. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_cossack_squat.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar across the upper back. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_low_switch_cossack_.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_foot_elevated_cossa.description: Stand in a wide stance with the bar across the upper back. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_alternating_c.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_decline.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_suspension_ipsilateral_ska.description: Stand on one leg with the load held as listed and reach the free leg forward for a pistol or back for a pistol squat. Bend the standing knee and sit down under control, then drive through the whole foot to stand. Keep the knee in line with the toes and reduce depth before the heel lifts or the torso collapses.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_walking_lunge.description: Stand tall with the bar across the upper back. Step forward into a lunge, drive through the front foot, and continue into the next step. Keep the torso stacked over the hips and avoid pushing off the back foot too hard.

ff_resistance_band_pull_apart.description: Hold a band at chest height with arms extended. Pull the band apart by squeezing the shoulder blades, then return with control. Keep a slight elbow bend and avoid shrugging the shoulders up.

ff_sandbag_bent_over_row.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Hinge or lie as the variation requires and row the load toward the ribs or hip, then lower until the arm is long. Keep the torso stable and avoid yanking with the shoulder.

ff_double_dumbbell_bent_over_reverse_fly.description: Hinge onto one leg with the free leg reaching back and the weight hanging below the shoulder. Row toward the hip while keeping the hips square to the floor, then lower the weight without standing up. Use a light load if balance makes the torso twist.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_knee_supported_tr.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_prone_t.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Row the weights toward the ribs, pause, and lower until arms are long. Keep the chest glued to the bench and avoid shrugging at the top.

ff_single_arm_cable_tricep_kickback.description: Stand facing a high cable and hold one handle with palm up. Keep the elbow pinned near the side as you press the handle down, then return to about 90 degrees. Keep the wrist straight and avoid leaning over the handle.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_tricep_kickback.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_slider_dumbbell_goblet_lateral_lunge.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

Hold a kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the rack tight and avoid collapsing toward the kettlebell.
