ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_c.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_i.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the weight drift forward.

ff_slider_single_arm_dumbbell_low_hold_c.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_low_hold_alternating_.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_shrug.description: Stand tall holding the load at the sides or in the rack. Elevate the shoulders straight up toward the ears, pause briefly, then lower slowly. Keep the arms straight and avoid rolling the shoulders forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_seated_shrug.description: Stand tall holding the load at the sides or in the rack. Elevate the shoulders straight up toward the ears, pause briefly, then lower slowly. Keep the arms straight and avoid rolling the shoulders forward.

ff_slider_clubbell_order_lateral_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_slider_double_clubbell_order_lateral_.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_slider_single_arm_clubbell_order_cont.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_slider_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsi.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_slider_macebell_order_lateral_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_slider_single_arm_macebell_order_cont.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_slider_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsi.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_stability_ball_miniband_feet_elevated.description: Lie on your back with one heel on a stability ball and the other leg lifted. Press the heel into the ball to raise the hips, then lower without letting the ball roll away. Keep the pelvis square and the ribs down.

ff_stability_ball_frog_reverse_hyperexte.description: Lie face down over the ball or bench with hips supported and knees bent. Lift the thighs until they line up with the torso, then lower slowly. Squeeze the glutes and avoid kicking into a back arch.

ff_stability_ball_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Set the rear foot on a bench or ball with dumbbells at your sides. Bend the front knee and lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep hips square and avoid letting the front heel lift.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_bulgarian_sp.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_slider_suspension_reverse_lunge.description: Place the slider under the working foot or limb on a smooth surface. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_slider_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_latera.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_slider_sandbag_front_rack_lateral_lun.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_slider_sandbag_back_rack_lateral_lung.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_miniband_ankle_monster_walk.description: Place the band above the knees or ankles as listed and set a quarter-squat or hip-hinge stance. Step laterally or forward while keeping tension on the band through each step. Avoid letting the knees cave inward.

ff_slider_landmine_goblet_lateral_lunge.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_slider_landmine_low_hold_lateral_lung.description: Hold the landmine low at the chest or inside shoulder and stand tall. Step to one side, sit the hips back over that leg, then push the floor away to return. Keep the opposite leg long and avoid letting the working knee dive inward.

ff_slider_single_arm_kettlebell_front_ra.description: Hold a kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid rotating the torso.

Hold a kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the rack tight and avoid collapsing toward the kettlebell.

ff_slider_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the sliding foot. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid letting the bell tip.

ff_slider_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

Hold a kettlebell overhead on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the weight drift forward.

ff_slider_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_slider_single_arm_kettlebell_low_hold.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_slider_plate_overhead_lateral_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

ff_slider_plate_overhead_reverse_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep wrists stacked over shoulders and use a shorter slide if the ribs flare.

ff_barbell_reverse_grip_incline_push_up.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_shrug.description: Stand tall holding the load at the sides or in the rack. Elevate the shoulders straight up toward the ears, pause briefly, then lower slowly. Keep the arms straight and avoid rolling the shoulders forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_tall_kneeling_shr.description: Kneel tall with one dumbbell at the shoulder. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_seated_shrug.description: Sit tall with one elbow supported at shoulder height and bent to 90 degrees. Rotate the dumbbell upward and backward, then lower slowly to the start. Keep the elbow fixed and use a light weight instead of twisting the torso.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_shrug.description: Stand tall holding the load at the sides or in the rack. Elevate the shoulders straight up toward the ears, pause briefly, then lower slowly. Keep the arms straight and avoid rolling the shoulders forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_hang_snatch.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_hang_snatch.description: Set the dumbbell between or just in front of the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive through the legs and hips, then guide the dumbbell close to the body into an overhead lockout. Keep the arm path close and avoid swinging the load far away from you.

ff_alternating_single_arm_dumbbell_hang_.description: Set the dumbbell between or just in front of the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive through the legs and hips, then guide the dumbbell close to the body into an overhead lockout. Keep the arm path close and avoid swinging the load far away from you.

ff_miniband_kneeling_side_plank_clamshel.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Set up in a side plank with the top leg on a bench as listed, lift the hips, and hold or adduct the lower leg under control. Keep the body long and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_miniband_thigh_side_plank_hip_abducti.description: Loop a miniband around the ankles and lie on one side with legs straight. Lift the top leg slightly back and up, then lower with control. Keep toes facing forward and avoid rolling the hips open.

ff_stability_ball_feet_elevated_russian_.description: Sit with knees bent and heels hovering while holding the load at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the shoulders and ribs from side to side. Keep the motion controlled and avoid swinging the load with loose arms.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_single_leg_v_u.description: Lie on your back with both legs long and arms by your sides. Lift one leg toward the ceiling, lower it with control, then switch sides. Keep the low back heavy on the floor and avoid kicking quickly through the reps.

ff_battle_rope_alternating_lateral_lunge.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_tall_kneeling_s.description: Kneel tall with one kettlebell racked at the shoulder. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_seated_shrug.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Brace tall and press the load overhead until the arms are vertical, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_plate_hip_thrust.description: Set the upper back on a bench or floor and place the load across the hips or hold it at the chest. Drive through the heels to lift the hips, pause briefly, and lower under control. Avoid arching the low back at the top.

ff_double_dumbbell_incline_bench_prone_s.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand. Row the weights toward the ribs, pause, and lower until arms are long. Keep the chest glued to the bench and avoid shrugging at the top.

ff_suspension_single_leg_foot_elevated_g.description: Lie on your back with one heel on a bench and the other leg lifted. Press through the elevated heel to raise the hips, then lower until the hips lightly touch down. Keep the pelvis level and avoid pushing through the toes.

ff_tire_single_leg_foot_elevated_glute_b.description: Lie on your back with one heel on a bench and the other leg lifted. Press through the elevated heel to raise the hips, then lower until the hips lightly touch down. Keep the pelvis level and avoid pushing through the toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_incline_bench_prone.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_suspension_sissy_squat.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_incline_bench_pro.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_incline_bench_p.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_double_dumbbell_seesaw_bench_press.description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with palms facing you. Lower them beside the lower chest, then press up while keeping wrists stacked. Keep elbows close and avoid letting the weights drift toward the face.

ff_bulgarian_bag_dead_squat_clean.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_double_dumbbell_seesaw_incline_bench_.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_double_dumbbell_seesaw_decline_bench_.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_bodyweight_hand_assisted_pistol_squat.description: Stand in a split stance near a support you can hold lightly. Bend both knees to lower straight down, then press through the front foot to stand. Use the hand for balance only and keep the front knee tracking over toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_alternatin.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_alternatin.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_alte.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_altern.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_altern.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_alternatin.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_alternating_.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_alternating_.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_altern.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_alternat.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_alternat.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_alternating_curtsy_lu.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_curt.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_curtsy_.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_clubbell_order_alternating_curtsy_lun.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step back and across into a curtsy lunge, then return and alternate sides. Keep the hips square and the mace close to the body instead of drifting forward.

ff_double_clubbell_order_alternating_cur.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step back and across into a curtsy lunge, then return and alternate sides. Keep the hips square and the mace close to the body instead of drifting forward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_alternating.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step back and across into a curtsy lunge, then return and alternate sides. Keep the hips square and the mace close to the body instead of drifting forward.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_curtsy_lun.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step back and across into a curtsy lunge, then return and alternate sides. Keep the hips square and the mace close to the body instead of drifting forward.

ff_bodyweight_alternating_curtsy_lunge.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_alternating_cu.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_alternating_cu.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_plate_overhead_alternating_curtsy_lun.description: Press a light barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as you bend the standing knee, then pull back to stand tall. Keep the bar stacked over the ribs and avoid letting the chest fall forward.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_alternating_curtsy.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_alternating_curtsy_.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_plate_hollow_body_hold.description: Set the body in the plank or hold shape named by the exercise and brace the ribs down. Hold steady while breathing quietly or add the limb motion listed. Avoid letting the hips or shoulders sag.

ff_double_dumbbell_seesaw_floor_press.description: Lie on your back with dumbbells at chest height and feet planted. Lift into a glute bridge and hold it while pressing the dumbbells over the chest. Keep hips level and avoid flaring the ribs as you press.

ff_double_kettlebell_seesaw_bench_press.description: Lie on a flat bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_double_kettlebell_seesaw_incline_benc.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_double_kettlebell_tuck_l_sit.description: Press down through the hands on the floor, parallettes, or bells and lift the hips. Extend or tuck the legs in front of you to hold the L-sit shape. Keep the shoulders depressed and avoid rounding the upper back.

ff_double_kettlebell_seesaw_decline_benc.description: Lie on a decline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_bodyweight_hollow_body_flutter_kicks.description: Lie on your back and hold two kettlebells locked over the chest while the legs extend long. Lift the shoulders slightly, brace into a hollow position, and alternate small up-and-down kicks. Keep the low back heavy on the floor instead of letting the weight pull the ribs open.

ff_double_kettlebell_seesaw_floor_press.description: Lie on the floor with knees bent with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_double_dumbbell_seesaw_overhead_press.description: Kneel tall with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Press one weight overhead as the other stays racked, then lower and switch sides. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning away from the pressing arm.

ff_double_dumbbell_arnold_press.description: Lie with upper back on a stability ball and feet planted wide. Hold one dumbbell at chest height and press it over the shoulder, then lower until the elbow is near the ball. Keep hips lifted and avoid rolling toward the weighted side.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_arnold_pr.description: Stand with two dumbbells racked at the shoulders. Dip and drive to press one dumbbell overhead, lower it, then alternate sides. Keep the dip vertical and avoid leaning toward the pressing arm.

ff_double_dumbbell_seated_arnold_press.description: Lie with upper back on a stability ball and feet planted wide. Hold one dumbbell at chest height and press it over the shoulder, then lower until the elbow is near the ball. Keep hips lifted and avoid rolling toward the weighted side.

ff_alternating_double_dumbbell_seated_ar.description: Lie with upper back on a stability ball and feet planted wide. Hold one dumbbell at chest height and press it over the shoulder, then lower until the elbow is near the ball. Keep hips lifted and avoid rolling toward the weighted side.

ff_bodyweight_feet_elevated_frog_pump.description: Lie on your back with soles of the feet together, knees opened out, and feet elevated on the stability ball. Tuck the ribs down, press the edges of the feet into the ball, and lift the hips until the body forms a short bridge. Pause at the top and avoid arching the low back to gain height.

ff_miniband_feet_elevated_frog_pump.description: Lie on your back with soles of the feet together, knees opened out, and feet elevated on the stability ball. Tuck the ribs down, press the edges of the feet into the ball, and lift the hips until the body forms a short bridge. Pause at the top and avoid arching the low back to gain height.

ff_miniband_feet_elevated_glute_bridge.description: Lie on your back with one heel on a stability ball and the other leg lifted. Press the heel into the ball to raise the hips, then lower without letting the ball roll away. Keep the pelvis square and the ribs down.

ff_bodyweight_reverse_plank.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Brace the ribs down and hold the listed core shape while moving arms or legs as the variation requires. Keep the low back from arching off the floor and move slowly enough to stay in control.

ff_wall_ball_toss.description: Hold the wall ball at the chest and squat down with the chest tall. Drive up and throw the ball to the target on the wall, then catch and absorb into the next squat. Keep the throw smooth and avoid breaking at the low back.

ff_wall_ball_feet_elevated_russian_twist.description: Sit with knees bent and heels hovering while holding the load at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the shoulders and ribs from side to side. Keep the motion controlled and avoid swinging the load with loose arms.

ff_wall_ball_russian_twist_lateral_toss.description: Sit with knees bent and heels hovering while holding the load at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the shoulders and ribs from side to side. Keep the motion controlled and avoid swinging the load with loose arms.

ff_wall_ball_lateral_toss.description: Sit with knees bent and heels hovering while holding the load at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the shoulders and ribs from side to side. Keep the motion controlled and avoid swinging the load with loose arms.

ff_wall_ball_sit_up_toss.description: Press down through the hands on the floor, parallettes, or bells and lift the hips. Extend or tuck the legs in front of you to hold the L-sit shape. Keep the shoulders depressed and avoid rounding the upper back.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_bulgarian_.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load at the side. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the load at your side without leaning toward it.

ff_double_dumbbell_tall_kneeling_arnold_.description: Kneel tall with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_barbell_zercher_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load overhead. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_bulgarian_split_squ.description: Stand with heels elevated and hold a bottoms-up kettlebell by the horns at the chest. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the bell vertical and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_landmine_goblet_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated and hold a bottoms-up kettlebell by the horns at the chest. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the bell vertical and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_macebell_order_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_contralater.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load on the side opposite the front leg. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsilateral.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Hold the load on the same side as the front leg. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_clubbell_order_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_plate_overhead_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_tire_bulgarian_split_squat.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_tire_bulgarian_split_squat_jump.description: Set the rear foot on a bench or ball using bodyweight only. Bend the front knee and lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep hips square and avoid letting the front heel lift.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_knee_over_.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_foot_eleva.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_knee_over_to.description: Set a split stance with dumbbells at your sides. Let the front knee travel forward over the toes as you lower, then press through the front foot to rise. Keep the heel planted and move slowly instead of bouncing.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_foot_elevate.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_foot_elevate.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_ipsilate.description: Set a split stance and hold the load overhead. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_contra.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load in the front rack. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the elbows forward and the load close to the ribs.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_foot_e.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load in the front rack. ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_contralateral_.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_split_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated and hold a bottoms-up kettlebell by the horns at the chest. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the bell vertical and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_march.description: Hold the kettlebell by the horns at chest height. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_raise.description: Stand facing away from a low cable with one handle in hand. Raise the arm forward to shoulder height, then lower without letting the cable pull you back. Keep the shoulder down and avoid leaning.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_cross_body_hammer.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_suspension_hamstring_curl.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Set the anchor as listed and curl the heel toward the hips or control the lowering phase with the hamstrings. Keep the hips stable and avoid letting them rise as the knee bends.

ff_suspension_single_leg_hamstring_curl.description: Lie face-down with an ankle strap attached to a low cable. Bend one knee to curl the heel toward the glute, then straighten the leg slowly. Keep hips on the bench or floor and avoid lifting the pelvis.

ff_superband_prone_hamstring_curl.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Set the anchor as listed and curl the heel toward the hips or control the lowering phase with the hamstrings. Keep the hips stable and avoid letting them rise as the knee bends.

ff_cable_prone_hamstring_curl.description: Lie face-down with an ankle strap attached to a low cable. Bend one knee to curl the heel toward the glute, then straighten the leg slowly. Keep hips on the bench or floor and avoid lifting the pelvis.

ff_superband_prone_bench_hamstring_curl.description: Set up with heels on the ball, sliders, bench cable, or ankle cable as named. Bend the knees to draw the heels toward the glutes, then lengthen the legs again. Keep hips steady and avoid swinging for momentum.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_bicep_curl.description: Stand hinged slightly forward with one dumbbell hanging between the legs. Brace the free hand on the thigh and curl the weight toward the opposite shoulder. Keep the upper arm quiet and avoid twisting the torso for momentum.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_hammer_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_ring_dips.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_bodyweight_single_arm_push_up.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_bodyweight_aztec_push_up.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_slider_barbell_front_rack_curtsy_lung.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_slider_double_kettlebell_front_rack_c.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_slider_suspension_curtsy_lunge.description: Place the slider under the working foot or limb on a smooth surface. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_slider_landmine_goblet_curtsy_lunge.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_slider_barbell_overhead_curtsy_lunge.description: Press a light barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as you bend the standing knee, then pull back to stand tall. Keep the bar stacked over the ribs and avoid letting the chest fall forward.

ff_slider_barbell_back_rack_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_slider_double_kettlebell_suitcase_cur.description: Hold kettlebells at your sides and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep the front heel grounded and avoid leaning toward one weight.

ff_slider_double_kettlebell_overhead_cur.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

ff_slider_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_f.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. ff_slider_single_arm_kettlebell_front_ra.description: Hold a kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid rotating the torso.

ff_slider_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. ff_slider_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_suitcase_curts.description: Hold kettlebells at your sides and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep the front heel grounded and avoid leaning toward one weight.

ff_slider_double_dumbbell_overhead_curts.description: Hold two kettlebells overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep wrists stacked over shoulders and use a shorter slide if the ribs flare.
