ff_kettlebell_goblet_knee_over_toe_split.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_foot_elevated_knee_.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_walking_lunge.description: Hold one dumbbell or kettlebell vertically at the chest. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_ring_maltese.description: Hang or support on the bar or rings with straight arms. Lift or hold the body in the lever position named by the exercise while keeping shoulders engaged. Move slowly into position and avoid bending the elbows to cheat the hold.

ff_barbell_front_rack_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_curtsy_l.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_suspension_curtsy_lunge.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_barbell_overhead_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_barbell_back_rack_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_barbell_zercher_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_curtsy_lun.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_curtsy_lun.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Hold a kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid rotating the torso.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_contra.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_contra.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_curtsy_lun.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_contra.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_contrala.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_contrala.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_curtsy_lunge.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_curtsy_lunge.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_clubbell_order_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step back and across into a curtsy lunge, then return and alternate sides. Keep the hips square and the mace close to the body instead of drifting forward.

ff_double_clubbell_order_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step back and across into a curtsy lunge, then return and alternate sides. Keep the hips square and the mace close to the body instead of drifting forward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_contralater.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step back and across into a curtsy lunge, then return and alternate sides. Keep the hips square and the mace close to the body instead of drifting forward.

ff_macebell_order_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step back and across into a curtsy lunge, then return and alternate sides. Keep the hips square and the mace close to the body instead of drifting forward.

ff_bodyweight_curtsy_lunge.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_plate_overhead_curtsy_lunge.description: Press a light barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as you bend the standing knee, then pull back to stand tall. Keep the bar stacked over the ribs and avoid letting the chest fall forward.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_curtsy_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_ipsi.description: Hold a kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the rack tight and avoid collapsing toward the kettlebell.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_ipsila.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held on the same side as the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the front foot rooted and avoid tipping toward the weight.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_ipsila.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the weight drift forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the sliding foot. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid letting the bell tip.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_ipsila.description: Hold a kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the rack tight and avoid collapsing toward the kettlebell.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_ipsilate.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held on the same side as the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the front foot rooted and avoid tipping toward the weight.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_ipsilate.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the weight drift forward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsilateral.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step back and across into a curtsy lunge, then return and alternate sides. Keep the hips square and the mace close to the body instead of drifting forward.

ff_ring_archer_pull_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_barbell_single_leg_bent_knee_bicep_cu.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_bodyweight_freestanding_handstand.description: Place the hands shoulder width and kick or press into a stacked handstand against the wall or freestanding. Keep ribs tucked, arms straight, and weight centered over the hands. Avoid arching the low back or letting the shoulders collapse.

ff_bodyweight_wall_eccentric_straddle_pr.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Press the load away in the plane named by the exercise, then return under control. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid using momentum from the lower back.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_carry.description: Hold the load in the listed carry position and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_carry.description: Hold the load in the listed carry position and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_carry.description: Hold the load in the listed carry position and walk with tall posture and controlled steps. Keep the ribs down and shoulders level throughout the carry. Avoid leaning away from the weight or rushing the steps.

ff_single_arm_ring_feet_elevated_row.description: Place the feet on a bench or box and the forearms on a stability ball. Hold the plank while lightening or lifting one forearm from the ball. Keep the ribs tucked and avoid rotating the hips.

ff_double_kettlebell_dead_snatch.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_dead_half_snatc.description: Set up half kneeling with the bell in front of the working hip. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_dea.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sta.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_start_stop_snat.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_double_kettlebell_start_stop_snatch.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_start_stop_swin.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sta.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_double_kettlebell_start_stop_swing.description: Stand with the kettlebell just in front of the feet and hinge to grip it. Hike the bell back with both hands, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_ring_l_sit_pull_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_suspension_skater_jump.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_start_stop_clean.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_start_stop_clea.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sta.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_clubbell_staggered_stance_rotational_.description: Set a staggered stance with the working-side foot slightly back and the bell just in front of you. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

Set a staggered stance with the working-side foot slightly back and the bell just in front of you. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

Set a staggered stance with the working-side foot slightly back and the bell just in front of you. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_ring_bulgarian_pull_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_ring_bulgarian_l_sit_pull_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_single_arm_landmine_split_snatch.description: Stand at the landmine with one hand on the sleeve. Clean the sleeve to the shoulder, then dip and split the feet as you press overhead. Land with both knees soft and avoid pressing before the leg drive finishes.

ff_double_dumbbell_hang_power_clean.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_single_arm_barbell_bicep_curl.description: Set one cable high and stand side-on with the working arm out to the side. Curl the handle toward the ear, then extend the elbow without dropping the upper arm. Keep the shoulder down and avoid rotating your body toward the stack.

ff_ring_false_grip_pull_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_slider_kettlebell_goblet_lateral_lung.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

ff_ring_feet_elevated_pike_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_single_arm_landmine_split_clean.description: Stand at the landmine with one hand on the sleeve. Clean the sleeve to the shoulder, then dip and split the feet as you press overhead. Land with both knees soft and avoid pressing before the leg drive finishes.

ff_double_dumbbell_squat_clean.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_double_dumbbell_hang_squat_clean.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_single_arm_landmine_split_jerk.description: Stand at the landmine with one hand on the sleeve. Clean the sleeve to the shoulder, then dip and split the feet as you press overhead. Land with both knees soft and avoid pressing before the leg drive finishes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_ipsilateral_for.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Hold the load on the side opposite the working leg. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_clubbell_hammer_swing.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_hammer_swing.description: Stand tall with the kettlebell to one side and feet wide enough to let the hips hinge. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_double_clubbell_hammer_swing.description: Stand in an athletic stance and swing the clubbell or mace through the arcing path named by the exercise. Keep the grip secure and the core braced as the bell passes in front of or behind the body. Use a lighter bell until the path stays smooth.

ff_ring_tuck_back_lever_with_alternating.description: Hang or start inverted on the rings with a firm grip. Lower into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full back lever shape named. Push the rings down and avoid shrugging into the ears.

ff_ring_tuck_front_lever_with_alternatin.description: Hang or start inverted on the rings with a firm grip. Lower into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full back lever shape named. Push the rings down and avoid shrugging into the ears.

ff_barbell_split_clean.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_double_dumbbell_hang_split_clean.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_ring_archer_push_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_wall_ball_half_kneeling_lateral_toss.description: Sit with knees bent and heels hovering while holding the load at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the shoulders and ribs from side to side. Keep the motion controlled and avoid swinging the load with loose arms.

ff_wall_ball_isometric_split_squat_later.description: Sit with knees bent and heels hovering while holding the load at chest height. Lean back slightly, brace, and rotate the shoulders and ribs from side to side. Keep the motion controlled and avoid swinging the load with loose arms.

ff_superband_assisted_bar_muscle_up.description: Loop a superband over a pull-up bar and place one foot or knee in it. Hang with hands overhand, pull the chest toward the bar, then lower until arms are long. Keep the legs quiet and avoid bouncing on the band.

ff_parallette_push_up_to_l_sit.description: Start in an L-sit on parallettes with arms straight and shoulders pressed down. Pull the knees in, lean the shoulders forward, and shift into a tuck planche before returning with control. Keep the elbows locked and avoid dropping the feet between positions.

ff_single_arm_barbell_bent_press.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_sots.description: Lie on the floor with the kettlebell or kettlebells held bottoms-up over the elbows. Press to straight arms, then lower until the upper arms touch the floor. Keep wrists vertical and avoid letting the bells tip.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_sots_pre.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Brace tall and press the load overhead until the arms are vertical, then lower under control. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to finish the rep.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bottoms.description: Lie on the floor with the kettlebell or kettlebells held bottoms-up over the elbows. Press to straight arms, then lower until the upper arms touch the floor. Keep wrists vertical and avoid letting the bells tip.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_seesaw_s.description: Lie on a flat bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press one side up while the other waits near the chest, then switch sides, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_half_kneeling_h.description: Set up half kneeling with the bell in front of the working hip. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_ring_alternating_reach_push_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_outside_snatch.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_outside_half_sn.description: Set up half kneeling with the bell in front of the working hip. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_outside_snatch.description: Set up half kneeling with the bell in front of the working hip. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_ring_l_sit_dips.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_single_l.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_sing.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_single_ring_chin_up.description: Hang from the rings with palms facing you, arms long, and shoulders active. Pull the chest toward the rings by driving the elbows down, then lower until the arms are long again. Keep the body quiet and avoid kicking or shrugging through the top.

ff_single_arm_suspension_single_leg_stan.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_ring_l_sit_chin_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_single_ring_l_sit_chin_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_sing.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_ring_pelican_curl.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Curl the weight through the range named by the exercise, then lower with control. Keep the upper arm still and avoid swinging the load.

ff_ring_two_finger_chin_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_bodyweight_pseudo_planche_push_up.description: Place the hands on the floor and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_parallette_pseudo_planche_push_up.description: Grip the parallettes and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs together and body stiff. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_parallette_single_leg_close_grip_push.description: Grip parallettes close together under the chest and set a straight plank. Lower between the handles, then press back up. Keep elbows near the ribs and avoid letting the shoulders roll forward.

ff_single_arm_macebell_single_leg_standi.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_macebell_inside_circle_to_360.description: Hold the mace near the head with both hands. Swing it in a controlled 360 around the body, passing behind the back with a smooth hand change. Keep the ribs braced and use a lighter mace until the path stays even on both sides.

ff_single_arm_landmine_single_leg_standi.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_alternating_single_arm_landmine_singl.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_macebell_alternating_360_to_pivot_spl.description: Kneel with one knee down and hold the kettlebell near the outside hip of the down-knee side. Brace and guide the weight diagonally across the body toward the opposite shoulder. Let the torso rotate only as far as you can control, avoiding a loose lower-back twist.

ff_double_clubbell_single_leg_standing_b.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_macebell_alternating_360_to_front_fla.description: Hold two clubbells at the chest with heavy ends forward. Press one club out and back, then repeat on the other side. Keep the nonworking club stable and avoid rotating the torso.

ff_double_indian_club_crescent_swing.description: Stand tall holding one light Indian club at your side. Swing it outward in a heart-shaped path and return to the start with a smooth wrist turnover. Keep the shoulder relaxed and avoid forcing speed before the pattern is clear.

ff_single_arm_macebell_inside_circle_to_.description: Hold the mace near the head with both hands. Swing it in a controlled 360 around the body, passing behind the back with a smooth hand change. Keep the ribs braced and use a lighter mace until the path stays even on both sides.

ff_bulgarian_bag_spin.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_alternating_spin.description: Place the rear foot on a bench and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down. Bend both knees, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_feet_elevated_reneg.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

Set two dumbbells under the shoulders and hold a wide-foot plank. Row one dumbbell toward the hip, lower it quietly, then row the other side. Keep the hips level and avoid twisting the torso to lift the weight.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_snatch_to_overh.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_reverse_l.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_forward_l.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_barbell_zercher_walking_lunge.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_lateral_l.description: Press a light barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as you bend the standing knee, then pull back to stand tall. Keep the bar stacked over the ribs and avoid letting the chest fall forward.

ff_slider_barbell_zercher_lateral_lunge.description: Press a light barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as you bend the standing knee, then pull back to stand tall. Keep the bar stacked over the ribs and avoid letting the chest fall forward.

ff_slider_barbell_zercher_reverse_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep wrists stacked over shoulders and use a shorter slide if the ribs flare.

ff_suspension_pelican_curl.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Curl the weight through the range named by the exercise, then lower with control. Keep the upper arm still and avoid swinging the load.

ff_bar_archer_pull_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_bar_false_grip_pull_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_bar_l_sit_chin_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_bar_l_sit_pull_up.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Hang with arms long, pull the chest toward the hands by driving elbows down, then lower under control. Keep legs quiet and avoid craning the neck to finish.

ff_bar_tuck_back_lever_with_alternating_.description: Hang or start inverted on the rings with a firm grip. Lower into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full back lever shape named. Push the rings down and avoid shrugging into the ears.

ff_bar_tuck_front_lever_with_alternating.description: Hang or start inverted on the rings with a firm grip. Lower into the tuck, straddle, half lay, single-leg, or full back lever shape named. Push the rings down and avoid shrugging into the ears.

ff_barbell_squat_clean_to_push_press.description: Start with the bar over the midfoot and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the shoulders, stand tall, then press it overhead. Keep the bar close and reset your brace before the press.

ff_barbell_squat_clean_to_overhead_press.description: Start with the bar over the midfoot and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the shoulders, stand tall, then press it overhead. Keep the bar close and reset your brace before the press.

ff_barbell_power_clean_to_front_rack_squ.description: Hold the load in the front rack with elbows forward. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_barbell_hang_power_clean_to_front_rac.description: Hold the load in the front rack with elbows forward. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_barbell_hang_power_clean_to_split_jer.description: Start with the bar over midfoot and grip just outside the legs. Pull the bar close, receive it in a front squat, stand, then dip and drive into a split jerk overhead. Keep the bar close and recover the feet before lowering or resetting.

ff_barbell_hang_power_clean_to_push_jerk.description: Start with the bar over the midfoot and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the shoulders, stand tall, then press it overhead. Keep the bar close and reset your brace before the press.

ff_barbell_hang_power_clean_to_push_pres.description: Start with the bar over the midfoot and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the shoulders, stand tall, then press it overhead. Keep the bar close and reset your brace before the press.

ff_barbell_hang_power_clean_to_overhead_.description: Start with the bar over the midfoot and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the shoulders, stand tall, then press it overhead. Keep the bar close and reset your brace before the press.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean_to_split_jer.description: Start with the bar over midfoot and grip just outside the legs. Pull the bar close, receive it in a front squat, stand, then dip and drive into a split jerk overhead. Keep the bar close and recover the feet before lowering or resetting.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean_to_push_jerk.description: Start with the bar over midfoot and grip just outside the legs. Pull the bar close, receive it in a front squat, stand, then dip and drive into a split jerk overhead. Keep the bar close and recover the feet before lowering or resetting.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean_to_push_pres.description: Start with the bar over the midfoot and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the shoulders, stand tall, then press it overhead. Keep the bar close and reset your brace before the press.

ff_barbell_hang_squat_clean_to_overhead_.description: Start with the bar over the midfoot and hands just outside the legs. Power clean the bar to the shoulders, stand tall, then press it overhead. Keep the bar close and reset your brace before the press.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_front_rack.description: Hold the load in the front rack with elbows forward. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_push_press.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_double_kettlebell_clean_to_front_rack.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_double_kettlebell_glute_bridge_isomet.description: Lie back on an incline bench with the bottoms-up kettlebells at chest level and shoulder blades set. Press the weight straight over the chest, keeping the wrists stacked. Lower with control and avoid letting the elbows flare wide or the bell tip out of line.

ff_slider_archer_push_up.description: Place the slider under the working foot or limb on a smooth surface. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_suspension_archer_push_up.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_superband_chaos_push_up.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bodyweight_pivot_split_squat.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_thrust.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_push_p.description: Start with the kettlebell between the feet and hinge to grip it. Drive the hips, clean the bell to the front rack, then press it overhead after the body is still. Lower to the rack with control and avoid letting the clean crash onto the forearm.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_push_j.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_front_.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_landmine_pivot_split_squat.description: Anchor the bar in a landmine and hold the free end at chest or shoulder height. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_pivot_spli.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted. Hold the load overhead. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_snatch_to_overh.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Hold the load on the same side as the working leg. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_outside_snatch_.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Hold the load on the side opposite the working leg. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Hold the load on the same side as the working leg. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_out.description: Stand with the kettlebell just in front of the feet and hinge to grip it. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Switch hands only when the bell floats near chest height. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_kettlebell_half_kneeling_swing.description: Set up half kneeling with the bell in front of the working hip. Hike the bell back with one hand, then drive the hips forward to swing. Let the bell float from the hip snap rather than lifting it with the arm. Keep the back long and park the bell if the swing turns into a squat or shoulder raise.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_march.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_march.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_roll.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_alternating_hang_should.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_hang_shoulder_clean.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_horn_grip_al.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_kettlebell_dead_clean.description: Hold the kettlebell in the rack or at arm's length as listed. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_overhead_pre.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_clubbell_barbarian_alternating_forwar.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_clubbell_barbarian_forward_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_macebell_barbarian_alternating_forwar.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_macebell_barbarian_forward_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_contra.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the front rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_ipsila.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the front rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_contralater.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsilateral.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_macebell_order_step_up.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_macebell_order_alternating_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_barbell_zercher_step_up.description: Hold the bar in the crooks of the elbows with the chest tall. Sit the hips down between the elbows and stand by driving through the whole foot. Keep the elbows tight to the torso and avoid letting the bar slip down the arms.

ff_barbell_zercher_alternating_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_step_up.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the back rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_alternating_s.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the back rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_step_up.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the back rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_sandbag_back_rack_alternating_step_up.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the back rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_alternating_step_u.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_plate_overhead_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_plate_overhead_alternating_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_alternat.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the bottoms-up front rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_alternatin.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the overhead position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_suspension_alternating_hawaiian_squat.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Sit the hips down with feet rooted and chest tall, then stand by pushing the floor away. Keep the knees tracking with the toes instead of collapsing inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_alternatin.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the suitcase position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_alternating_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_alternating_step_up.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_tire_russian_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_tire_alternating_russian_step_up.description: Stand facing the box or tire with one foot fully on the platform. Drive through the top foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the back foot too much.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_seated_good_morni.description: Sit tall on a bench with dumbbells held at the shoulders or against the upper back. Brace, hinge forward from the hips, then return to tall sitting. Keep the spine long and avoid rounding the low back to get lower.

ff_trap_bar_bottoms_up_z_press.description: Kneel tall under or inside the trap bar with the handles at shoulder height. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_step_up.description: Stand with heels elevated and hold a bottoms-up kettlebell by the horns at the chest. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the bell vertical and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_step.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Circle the bell, catch it at chest height, then sit into the squat before standing. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back.

ff_superband_assisted_reverse_nordic.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Set the anchor as listed and curl the heel toward the hips or control the lowering phase with the hamstrings. Keep the hips stable and avoid letting them rise as the knee bends.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_mill_to_reverse_m.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_single_arm_macebell_mill_to_reverse_m.description: Hold the mace near the head and guide it through the mill or whip path behind the body with soft elbows. Switch hand timing smoothly as the mace circles. Keep the ribs braced and reduce weight if the bell pulls you off balance.

ff_alternating_single_arm_macebell_mill.description: Hold the mace near the head and guide it through the mill or whip path behind the body with soft elbows. Switch hand timing smoothly as the mace circles. Keep the ribs braced and reduce weight if the bell pulls you off balance.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_hang_power_clean.description: Set the dumbbell between or just in front of the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive through the legs and hips, then guide the dumbbell close to the body into an overhead lockout. Keep the arm path close and avoid swinging the load far away from you.

ff_alternating_single_arm_dumbbell_hang_.description: Set the dumbbell between or just in front of the feet and hinge with a flat back. Drive through the legs and hips, then guide the dumbbell close to the body into an overhead lockout. Keep the arm path close and avoid swinging the load far away from you.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_russian_step.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand tall, then step down with control. Keep the working knee over the toes and avoid pushing off the floor leg.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_alternatin.description: Hold two dumbbells at the shoulders and place one foot fully on a box. Drive through the box foot to stand, step down, then alternate sides. Keep elbows high and avoid pushing off the trail leg.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_russian_.description: Stand in front of a box with the weight in the front rack position. Place the whole working foot on the box, drive through that foot, and stand tall before stepping down. Keep the knee tracking over the toes and avoid pushing off the trailing leg.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_russian_step_up.description: Stand with heels elevated and hold a bottoms-up kettlebell by the horns at the chest. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the bell vertical and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_russ.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Circle the bell, catch it at chest height, then sit into the squat before standing. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back.

ff_miniband_hip_thrust.description: Place the band where the variation requires and keep tension through the rep. Set the upper back on a bench or floor, tuck the ribs, and drive through the heels to lift the hips. Pause at the top and avoid arching the low back for extra height.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_step_up.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_alternating_st.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_russian_step_u.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_alternating_ru.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_alternating_single_arm_macebell_rever.description: Hold the mace near the head and guide it through the mill or whip path behind the body with soft elbows. Switch hand timing smoothly as the mace circles. Keep the ribs braced and reduce weight if the bell pulls you off balance.

ff_ring_grip_straddle_planche.description: Set the rings low and lean forward until the shoulders are in front of the hands. Keep the legs straddled to manage balance. Bend the elbows to lower the body as one piece, then press back to the leaned support. Do not let the hips drop or the elbows flare wide.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_o.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_p.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_front_.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_p.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_double_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_push_.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_dead.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_bot.description: Hold the kettlebell upside down at the rack or overhead with the bell above the handle. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_f.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_dead_clean_to_t.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_dea.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side, alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked, alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.
