Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_macebell_bull_whip_to_mill.description: Hold the mace near the head and guide it through the mill or whip path behind the body with soft elbows. Switch hand timing smoothly as the mace circles. Keep the ribs braced and reduce weight if the bell pulls you off balance.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted. Hold the load overhead. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_kang_squat.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_feet_elevated_renegad.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

Set two dumbbells under the shoulders and hold a wide-foot plank. Row one dumbbell toward the hip, lower it quietly, then row the other side. Keep the hips level and avoid twisting the torso to lift the weight.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_forward_lung.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_barbell_front_rack_forward_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_barbell_overhead_forward_lunge.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_forward_lunge.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_contralateral_.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_barbell_back_rack_forward_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_forward_lu.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_forward_.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_forward_lu.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_forward_lung.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_contrala.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_contra.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_contrala.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_forward_lu.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_forward_lunge.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_clubbell_order_forward_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_clubbell_order_forward_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_contralater.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step into the lunge pattern named by the drill and push through the front foot to return. Keep the torso tall and the mace stacked over the shoulder.

ff_macebell_order_forward_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_contra.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_bodyweight_forward_lunge.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_forward_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_forward_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_plate_overhead_forward_lunge.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_contra.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_forward_lunge.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step into the lunge pattern named by the exercise, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return or continue. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_forward_lunge.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_barbell_zercher_forward_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_ipsilate.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_ipsila.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_ipsilate.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsilateral.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step into the lunge pattern named by the drill and push through the front foot to return. Keep the torso tall and the mace stacked over the shoulder.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_ipsila.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held on the same side as the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the front foot rooted and avoid tipping toward the weight.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_ipsi.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the sliding foot. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid letting the bell tip.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_ipsila.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the weight drift forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_alternating_single_arm_dumbbell_plank.description: Anchor the band low behind you and stand facing away with the band between the legs. Hinge back until the hips reach behind you, then drive the hips forward to stand. Keep the arms as hooks and avoid turning it into a squat.

ff_alternating_single_arm_dumbbell_feet_.description: Anchor the band low behind you and stand facing away with the band between the legs. Hinge back until the hips reach behind you, then drive the hips forward to stand. Keep the arms as hooks and avoid turning it into a squat.

ff_landmine_cyclist_thruster.description: Anchor the bar in a landmine and hold the free end at chest or shoulder height. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_single_arm_landmine_cyclist_thruster.description: Stand narrow with heels elevated and the load in the rack position. Lower into the squat or split squat, then drive out of the squat and press the landmine up along its angled path. Lower the load back to the rack before the next rep, keeping the elbows from dropping during the dip.

ff_clubbell_swing.description: Hinge slightly and swing the clubbell back between the legs, then drive the hips forward to swing it to chest or shoulder height. Keep the arms relaxed and the bell path close to the body. Avoid lifting with the shoulders alone.

ff_macebell_half_battle_lunge.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Step into a lunge, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_clubbell_order_pivot_split_squat.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_macebell_order_pivot_split_squat.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides with the rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, then press through the front foot to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid drifting onto the front toes.

ff_bodyweight_plate_assisted_hip_airplan.description: Stand on one leg with fingertips on a wall for balance and hinge slightly at the hip. Rotate the pelvis open and closed over the standing leg while the spine stays long. Move slowly and avoid letting the knee collapse inward.

ff_macebell_battle_lunge.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Step into a lunge, lower until both knees bend, then push through the front foot to return. Keep the torso tall and the front knee aligned over the middle toes.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_tactical_half_s.description: Start with one kettlebell between the feet and prepare to switch hands at the backswing. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_ipsilateral_fo.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_barbell_tall_kneeling_cuban_rotation.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Rotate through the thoracic spine or hips as the setup requires while keeping the base stable. Turn from the mid-back and hips instead of cranking the low back.

ff_barbell_cuban_rotation.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Rotate through the thoracic spine or hips as the setup requires while keeping the base stable. Turn from the mid-back and hips instead of cranking the low back.

ff_alternating_single_arm_landmine_tall_.description: Kneel tall facing the landmine with glutes squeezed. Brace and press the bar forward and up until the arm is nearly straight, then lower back to the shoulder. Press one side for the planned reps before switching. Keep the torso from rotating or leaning away from the bar.

ff_barbell_feet_elevated_glute_bridge.description: Lie on your back with both heels in the suspension straps and knees bent. Press the heels down to lift the hips, then lower slowly while the straps stay quiet. Keep ribs tucked and avoid letting the knees drift apart or collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_tactical_snatch.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_bodyweight_push_up_to_alternating_lat.description: Start in a strong plank with hands under or slightly wider than the shoulders. Do one push-up, then rotate and thread one leg under the body before returning to plank. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_double_dumbbell_single_leg_romanian_d.description: Stand on one leg holding the kettlebells with a soft knee. Hinge by sending the free leg back and lowering the load toward the floor. Keep the hips square and avoid opening the pelvis to chase more depth.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_contralateral_sin.description: Stand on one leg holding the kettlebell in the hand opposite the stance leg with a soft knee. Hinge by sending the free leg back and lowering the load toward the floor. Keep the hips square and avoid opening the pelvis to chase more depth.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_single_leg_romani.description: Pinch the weight plates together at your sides or in front of the thighs. Hinge at the hips with a soft knee bend and lower the load along the legs until hamstrings load, then stand by driving the hips forward. Keep the back long and avoid rounding to chase extra range.

ff_alternating_double_clubbell_pullover.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Guide the load in a controlled arc around or over the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare.

ff_barbell_tall_kneeling_bicep_curl.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_dumbbell_tall_kneeling_overhead_trice.description: Sit tall holding an EZ bar overhead with elbows pointing forward. Bend the elbows to lower the bar behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_clubbell_inside_circle_to_shield_cast.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_alternating_single_arm_clubbell_insid.description: Stand in an athletic stance and grip the clubbell for the named cast or mill. Guide the bell through the arcing path with soft elbows and a braced core. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the bell travels behind you.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sna.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked, alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_cable_prone_bench_single_leg_hamstrin.description: Lie face-down with an ankle strap attached to a low cable. Bend one knee to curl the heel toward the glute, then straighten the leg slowly. Keep hips on the bench or floor and avoid lifting the pelvis.

ff_double_dumbbell_tall_kneeling_cuban_r.description: Sit tall holding light dumbbells with elbows lifted out to the sides and bent. Rotate the forearms upward until the weights are above the elbows, then return slowly. Keep the ribs down and avoid shrugging to move heavier weights.

ff_macebell_single_leg_standing_bent_kne.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_single_arm_macebell_single_leg_standi.description: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable and stand on the opposite leg. Keep the working knee bent as you rotate the thigh outward, then return slowly. Keep the pelvis facing forward and avoid swinging the foot.

ff_alternating_single_arm_macebell_360_t.description: Hold the mace with both hands near the head of the tool. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Curl the weight through the range named by the exercise, then lower with control. Keep the upper arm still and avoid swinging the load.

ff_double_dumbbell_suitcase_reverse_lung.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step one foot back into a reverse lunge. Drive through the front foot to stand, then alternate legs. Keep shoulders level and avoid swinging the weights.

ff_barbell_front_rack_reverse_lunge.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_barbell_overhead_reverse_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep wrists stacked over shoulders and use a shorter slide if the ribs flare.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_reverse_lunge.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_contralateral_.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_barbell_back_rack_reverse_lunge.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_reverse_lu.description: Hold kettlebells at your sides and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep the front heel grounded and avoid leaning toward one weight.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_reverse_.description: Hold two kettlebells in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot straight back into a reverse lunge, then pull it forward to stand. Keep elbows tucked and the front knee aligned over the toes.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_reverse_lu.description: Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_ipsilateral_re.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_walking_lun.description: Hold the kettlebell in the front rack. Step forward into a lunge, lower both knees, then drive through the front foot into the next step. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_alternating.description: Hold the mace in the order grip at one shoulder. Step into the lunge pattern named by the drill and push through the front foot to return. Keep the torso tall and the mace stacked over the shoulder.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_contralater.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsilateral.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_contralater.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsilateral.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_cable_incline_bench_preach.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_double_kettlebell_seesaw_bent_over_ro.description: Hold a kettlebell in one hand and hinge with the other hand braced if needed. Row the bell toward the hip, then lower until the arm is long. Keep the torso square and avoid yanking with the shoulder.

ff_double_dumbbell_seesaw_bent_over_row.description: Hinge onto one leg with the free leg reaching back and the weight hanging below the shoulder. Row toward the hip while keeping the hips square to the floor, then lower the weight without standing up. Use a light load if balance makes the torso twist.

ff_barbell_spider_curl.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_slider_kettlebell_horn_grip_reverse_l.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_superband_good_morning.description: Place the band or bar across the upper back and set the feet hip width. Hinge at the hips with a soft knee bend until the hamstrings load, then stand by driving the hips forward. Keep the back long and avoid rounding at the bottom.

ff_trap_bar_feet_elevated_calf_raise.description: Stand with the balls of the feet on an edge and kettlebells held suitcase style. Lower the heels below the edge, then rise onto the toes with control. Keep the knees mostly straight and avoid bouncing out of the bottom.

ff_superband_prone_single_leg_hamstring_.description: Lie face-down with an ankle strap attached to a low cable. Bend one knee to curl the heel toward the glute, then straighten the leg slowly. Keep hips on the bench or floor and avoid lifting the pelvis.

ff_superband_prone_bench_single_leg_hams.description: Lie face-down with an ankle strap attached to a low cable. Bend one knee to curl the heel toward the glute, then straighten the leg slowly. Keep hips on the bench or floor and avoid lifting the pelvis.

ff_barbell_seated_cuban_rotation.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Rotate through the thoracic spine or hips as the setup requires while keeping the base stable. Turn from the mid-back and hips instead of cranking the low back.

ff_cable_rope_pullover.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Guide the load in a controlled arc around or over the head and return to the start. Keep the elbow path smooth and avoid letting the ribs flare.

ff_kettlebell_close_grip_push_up.description: Start in a high plank with hands under or just inside the shoulders. Lower with elbows tracking close to the ribs, then press back up. Keep the body straight and avoid flaring the elbows wide as reps get hard.

ff_kettlebell_kneeling_close_grip_push_u.description: Grip parallettes close together under the chest and set a straight plank. Lower between the handles, then press back up. Keep elbows near the ribs and avoid letting the shoulders roll forward.

ff_kettlebell_feet_elevated_close_grip_p.description: Grip the parallettes under the shoulders in a plank. Keep the hands close and bend the elbows back along the ribs as you lower. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_barbell_close_grip_incline_push_up.description: Grip the barbell with a stable setup for the lift. Set the hands under or just outside the shoulders, lower the chest toward the floor, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_march.description: Hold one dumbbell or kettlebell vertically at the chest. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_march.description: Hold one dumbbell or kettlebell vertically at the chest. Hold the load overhead and march in place or forward, lifting knees without losing the stacked arm position. Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back as the knees rise.

ff_dumbbell_crush_grip_overhead_march.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_cable_pallof_press.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Press the load away in the plane named by the exercise, then return under control. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid using momentum from the lower back.

ff_single_ring_pallof_press.description: Set the rings at the height needed and keep the straps vertical. Press the load away in the plane named by the exercise, then return under control. Keep the ribs stacked and avoid using momentum from the lower back.

ff_kettlebell_low_hold_foot_elevated_cos.description: Stand in a wide stance holding the landmine sleeve. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_barbell_seated_overhead_tricep_extens.description: Sit tall holding an EZ bar overhead with elbows pointing forward. Bend the elbows to lower the bar behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_barbell_standing_overhead_tricep_exte.description: Sit tall holding an EZ bar overhead with elbows pointing forward. Bend the elbows to lower the bar behind the head, then extend back overhead. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the elbows flare wide.

ff_barbell_tall_kneeling_overhead_tricep.description: Set up on the bench or kneeling holding the EZ bar. Bend the elbows to lower the load behind the head or toward the forehead, then extend the arms. Keep upper arms steady and avoid flaring the elbows.

ff_miniband_thigh_kneeling_side_plank_hi.description: Loop a miniband around the ankles and lie on one side with legs straight. Lift the top leg slightly back and up, then lower with control. Keep toes facing forward and avoid rolling the hips open.

ff_bodyweight_crunch.description: Lie on your back and brace the core before curling the shoulders or legs through the range named by the exercise. Exhale on the effort and lower slowly. Avoid pulling on the neck or using momentum from the arms.

ff_bodyweight_reverse_crunch.description: Lie on your back and brace the core before curling the shoulders or legs through the range named by the exercise. Exhale on the effort and lower slowly. Avoid pulling on the neck or using momentum from the arms.

ff_bodyweight_dips.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Dip athletically, drive through the floor, and land softly in the position named by the drill. Stick the landing with knees aligned over toes before the next rep.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_incline_bench_pow.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_cable_rope_half_kneeling_rotation.description: Set a cable around shoulder height and kneel with the working arm farthest from the stack. Hold the elbow at shoulder height and rotate the hand backward against the cable. Keep the ribs stacked over the hips and avoid letting the elbow drift down.

ff_cable_rope_isometric_split_squat_rota.description: Anchor the battle ropes and stand with a slight athletic bend in the knees. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Place the rear foot on a bench or floor in a split stance and lower mostly straight down, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_double_dumbbell_tall_kneeling_cuban_p.description: Kneel tall with two dumbbells at the shoulders. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_good_morning.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_clean_to_overhead_press.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_bulgarian_split_.description: Stand with heels elevated and hold a bottoms-up kettlebell by the horns at the chest. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the bell vertical and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_split_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated and hold a bottoms-up kettlebell by the horns at the chest. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the bell vertical and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_knee_over_toe_sp.description: Set a split stance and hold the load in the front rack. Lower mostly straight down until the front knee bends deeply, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking with the toes and avoid tipping the torso forward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_foot_elevated_kn.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_cossack_squat.description: Stand in a wide stance holding a dumbbell at the chest. Shift into one side by bending that knee while the other leg stays long, then move back through center. Keep the working heel down and avoid collapsing the chest.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_alternating_coss.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Circle the bell, catch it at chest height, then sit into the squat before standing. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_foot_elevated_co.description: Set a split stance with the working foot forward and the back heel lifted, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_low_switch_cossa.description: Take a wide stance with toes slightly turned out and Hold the kettlebells in the front rack. Sit into one hip while the other leg lengthens, then press back across to the other side. Stay low as you shift from side to side if you can keep the heel down. Keep the loaded position stacked and avoid letting the bent knee cave inward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_cossack_squat.description: Stand with feet about shoulder width and knees soft. Circle the bell, catch it at chest height, then sit into the squat before standing. Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and move the bell around a quiet torso instead of twisting the low back.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_squat.description: Stand with heels elevated and feet close holding a kettlebell by the horns. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_contra.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_ipsila.description: Place the rear foot on a bench behind you and set the front foot far enough forward to lower straight down, then hold the load overhead. Hold the load on the side opposite the working front leg. Bend both knees, stand by pushing through the front foot, and keep the front knee tracking over the toes. Keep the arm or bar stacked over the shoulder as you move.

ff_double_dumbbell_low_hold_lateral_lung.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_barbell_front_rack_lateral_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_barbell_overhead_lateral_lunge.description: Press a light barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as you bend the standing knee, then pull back to stand tall. Keep the bar stacked over the ribs and avoid letting the chest fall forward.

ff_heavy_sandbag_bear_hug_lateral_lunge.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_contralateral_.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_barbell_back_rack_lateral_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_suitcase_lateral_lu.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_front_rack_lateral_.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_front_ra.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_front_rack_lateral_lu.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_double_dumbbell_overhead_lateral_lung.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_suitcase_contrala.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_contra.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_contrala.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

ff_double_kettlebell_overhead_lateral_lu.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

ff_dumbbell_goblet_lateral_lunge.description: Hold dumbbells at the sides and step forward into a lunge. Push through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate legs. Keep the torso tall and avoid the front knee collapsing inward.

ff_clubbell_order_lateral_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_clubbell_order_lateral_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_contralater.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_macebell_order_lateral_lunge.description: Grip the clubbell with a secure handle position for the arc. Alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_double_kettlebell_bottoms_up_overhead.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up overhead and stand with one foot on a slider. Slide that foot out into a lateral lunge while the standing knee bends, then pull back to center. Keep wrists stacked, ribs down, and make the slide shorter if the bells wobble.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_suitcase_contra.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_bodyweight_lateral_lunge.description: Use bodyweight only with a stable start position. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_bulgarian_bag_back_rack_lateral_lunge.description: Hold two kettlebells bottoms-up in the front rack and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as the standing leg bends into a lateral lunge, then return to standing. Keep elbows under the bells and avoid letting the knee cave inward.

ff_sandbag_front_rack_lateral_lunge.description: Hold the bar in the front rack and stand with feet under the hips. Step one leg back and across into a curtsy lunge, lower under control, then push through the front foot to return and alternate. Keep the hips mostly square and avoid letting the front knee dive inside the toes.

ff_plate_overhead_lateral_lunge.description: Press a light barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as you bend the standing knee, then pull back to stand tall. Keep the bar stacked over the ribs and avoid letting the chest fall forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_cont.description: Hold a kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid rotating the torso.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_contra.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the bell stacked over the shoulder and avoid leaning away from it.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_kettlebell_goblet_lateral_lunge.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_lateral_lunge.description: Stand tall with the working foot on a slider. Step or slide to one side, bend that knee, and keep the other leg long before pushing back to center. Keep the planted foot flat and avoid twisting toward the load.

ff_barbell_zercher_lateral_lunge.description: Press a light barbell overhead and place one foot on a slider. Slide that foot sideways as you bend the standing knee, then pull back to stand tall. Keep the bar stacked over the ribs and avoid letting the chest fall forward.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_front_rack_ipsila.description: Hold a kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the rack tight and avoid collapsing toward the kettlebell.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_overhead_ipsilate.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the weight drift forward.

ff_single_arm_clubbell_order_ipsilateral.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_ipsi.description: Hold a kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the rack tight and avoid collapsing toward the kettlebell.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_fron.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack on the same side as the sliding foot. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull forward to stand. Keep the elbow close and avoid letting the bell tip.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_overhead_ipsila.description: Hold a kettlebell overhead on the same side as the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to stand. Keep ribs down and avoid letting the weight drift forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_over.description: Start tall with the working foot on a slider. Slide or step one foot back into a reverse lunge, then drive through the front foot to return. Keep the front knee over the middle toes and avoid leaning away from the load.

ff_heavy_sandbag_shoulder_ipsilateral_la.description: Bear-hug or shoulder the sandbag as the variation requires. Walk with the load held in the listed position while keeping the torso tall and steps controlled. Keep the shoulders level and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_contralater.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_single_arm_macebell_order_ipsilateral.description: Stand with one foot on a slider and a kettlebell held at the side opposite the sliding leg. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then pull through the front foot to stand. Keep the front knee tracking over the toes and do not let the weight pull you sideways.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_cle.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked, alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

ff_single_arm_landmine_half_kneeling_ips.description: Set up half kneeling with the working-side hand on the end of the bar. Brace and press the bar forward and up until the arm is nearly straight, then lower back to the shoulder. Press one side for the planned reps before switching. Keep the torso from rotating or leaning away from the bar.

ff_stability_ball_copenhagen_plank.description: Position the stability ball under the listed contact point. Set up in a side plank with the top leg on a bench as listed, lift the hips, and hold or adduct the lower leg under control. Keep the body long and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_double_kettlebell_gorilla_clean.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

ff_stability_ball_side_plank.description: Position the stability ball under the listed contact point. Set up in a side plank with the top leg on a bench as listed, lift the hips, and hold or adduct the lower leg under control. Keep the body long and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_stability_ball_mountain_climber.description: Start in a high plank with shoulders over wrists. Drive one knee toward the chest, switch legs, and keep a steady plank as the feet move. Brace the ribs down and avoid bouncing the hips high.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_seated_bicep_cur.description: Hold a kettlebell by the horns with elbows near the ribs. Curl the bell toward the chest, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep shoulders down and avoid swinging the bell forward.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_cle.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side, alternate sides each rep with the same form on both sides. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_clean_to_bent_p.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_copenhagen_plan.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_suspension_copenhagen_plank.description: Adjust the straps so the handles hang at the right height. Set up in a side plank with the top leg on a bench as listed, lift the hips, and hold or adduct the lower leg under control. Keep the body long and avoid rolling the chest toward the floor.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bent_knee_copen.description: Stand on one leg with a soft knee and hold the kettlebell in one hand. Pass or circle the bell around the body while the stance knee stays bent and the torso stays tall. Move slowly and avoid letting the hip drop or the foot wobble wildly.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bottoms_up_bent.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_suspension_kneeling_rollout.description: Kneel or stand with the rollout tool under the shoulders and brace the core. Roll out as far as you can while keeping the hips from sagging, then pull back with the abs and lats. Shorten the range if the low back arches.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_tall_kneeling_bi.description: Hold a kettlebell by the horns with elbows near the ribs. Curl the bell toward the chest, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep shoulders down and avoid swinging the bell forward.

ff_kettlebell_horn_grip_half_kneeling_bi.description: Hold a kettlebell by the horns with elbows near the ribs. Curl the bell toward the chest, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep shoulders down and avoid swinging the bell forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_bicep_curl.description: Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Curl the weight toward the shoulders without moving the upper arms, then lower until the elbows are straight. Keep the shoulders down and avoid leaning back to finish.

ff_double_kettlebell_seated_bicep_curl.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_bicep_c.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_seated_.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the arms hanging straight and the weight in your hands. Curl by bending only at the elbows, then lower until the arms are long. Keep the chest on the pad and avoid swinging the shoulders forward.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_bicep_curl.description: Set one cable high and stand side-on with the working arm out to the side. Curl the handle toward the ear, then extend the elbow without dropping the upper arm. Keep the shoulder down and avoid rotating your body toward the stack.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_seated_bicep_cu.description: Set one cable high and stand side-on with the working arm out to the side. Curl the handle toward the ear, then extend the elbow without dropping the upper arm. Keep the shoulder down and avoid rotating your body toward the stack.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_incline_bench_bic.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_incline.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_double_kettlebell_incline_bench_bicep.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull one weight at a time toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_incline_bench_b.description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with the weights hanging below the shoulders. Pull the weight toward the ribs while keeping the chest supported. Lower fully and avoid lifting the chest or shrugging at the top.

ff_alternating_double_kettlebell_tall_kn.description: Kneel tall with two kettlebells racked at the shoulders. Press one bell overhead, lower it, then press the other side while staying upright. Squeeze the glutes and avoid shifting the hips side to side.

ff_double_kettlebell_tall_kneeling_bicep.description: Kneel tall with two kettlebells racked at the shoulders. Press one bell overhead, lower it, then press the other side while staying upright. Squeeze the glutes and avoid shifting the hips side to side.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_tall_kneeling_b.description: Kneel tall with one kettlebell racked at the shoulder. Brace the glutes and press the weight overhead until the arm or arms are straight, then lower to the shoulders. Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid leaning back.

ff_single_arm_dumbbell_half_kneeling_con.description: Kneel with one foot forward and hold a dumbbell in the hand opposite the front leg. Brace the glutes and press the dumbbell overhead, then lower to shoulder height. Keep ribs down and avoid leaning away from the weight.

ff_sled_push.description: Load the sled and grip the handles with the body angled forward. Drive through the balls of the feet with short, powerful steps for the listed distance or time. Stay low through the start and avoid upright jogging when acceleration is the goal.

ff_sled_rope_pull.description: Load the sled and grip the handles with the body angled forward. Drive through the balls of the feet with short, powerful steps for the listed distance or time. Stay low through the start and avoid upright jogging when acceleration is the goal.

ff_sled_rope_seated_pull.description: Load the sled and grip the handles with the body angled forward. Drive through the balls of the feet with short, powerful steps for the listed distance or time. Stay low through the start and avoid upright jogging when acceleration is the goal.

ff_sled_forward_drag.description: Load the sled and grip the handles with the body angled forward. Drive through the balls of the feet with short, powerful steps for the listed distance or time. Stay low through the start and avoid upright jogging when acceleration is the goal.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_front_rack_marc.description: Hold a bottoms-up kettlebell in the front rack opposite the foot on the slider. Slide that foot back into a reverse lunge, then return to standing. Keep the wrist vertical and avoid rushing the balance challenge.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_tactical_clean_.description: Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

Stand at the landmine with one hand on the sleeve. Clean the sleeve to the shoulder, then dip and split the feet as you press overhead. Land with both knees soft and avoid pressing before the leg drive finishes.

Start with the kettlebell between the feet and hinge to grip it. Drive the hips, clean the bell to the front rack, then press it overhead after the body is still. Lower to the rack with control and avoid letting the clean crash onto the forearm.

Start with the kettlebell between the feet and hinge to grip it. Drive the hips, clean the bell to the front rack, then press it overhead after the body is still. Lower to the rack with control and avoid letting the clean crash onto the forearm.

Stand with heels elevated and feet close with the kettlebell bottoms-up at the shoulder. Squat straight down, then drive through the feet to stand. Keep the torso tall and avoid letting the knees collapse inward.

Hold the load in one hand at the rack or by the side. Work one arm at a time while the other arm supports or waits at the side. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

ff_cable_straight_bar_incline_bench_tric.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Set the elbows in a fixed position and extend the arms to straighten, then bend back under control. Keep the upper arms still and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.

ff_barbell_tall_kneeling_reverse_grip_bi.description: Stand holding an EZ bar with palms facing down. Curl the bar while keeping elbows close to the ribs, then lower to straight arms. Use a lighter load and avoid bending the wrists back.

ff_cable_straight_bar_tall_kneeling_tric.description: Kneel tall facing away from a high cable with the rope behind your head. Extend the elbows until the arms are long, then bend them to return. Keep glutes squeezed and avoid flaring the ribs.

ff_single_arm_cable_bayesian_curl.description: Set one cable high and stand side-on with the working arm out to the side. Curl the handle toward the ear, then extend the elbow without dropping the upper arm. Keep the shoulder down and avoid rotating your body toward the stack.

ff_cable_rope_incline_bench_tricep_pushd.description: Set the cable to the height listed and stand in a stable base. Set the elbows in a fixed position and extend the arms to straighten, then bend back under control. Keep the upper arms still and avoid letting the elbows drift forward.

ff_double_kettlebell_start_stop_half_sna.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sta.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_single_arm_kettlebell_start_stop_half.description: Start with the kettlebell on the floor or in a hike position in front of the feet. Hinge, drive the hips, and pull the bell close as it rises. Punch the hand through so the bell finishes quietly over the shoulder. Keep the wrist straight and avoid casting the bell away from the body.

ff_double_kettlebell_kneeling_push_up.description: Place the feet on a bench or box and set the hands under the shoulders. Lower the chest toward the hands, then press back to straight arms. Keep the body in one line and avoid reaching the head forward to shorten the rep.

ff_bodyweight_oblique_crunch.description: Lie on your back and brace the core before curling the shoulders or legs through the range named by the exercise. Exhale on the effort and lower slowly. Avoid pulling on the neck or using momentum from the arms.

ff_double_kettlebell_start_stop_clean_to.description: Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

Start with the kettlebell slightly in front of you and hinge to grip the handle. Hike or pull the bell close to the body and use the legs and hips to bring it up. Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

Hold the load locked out overhead with arms stacked. Squat down with the load in the rack, then stand hard and continue into an overhead press in one smooth sequence. Keep the elbows up in the squat and avoid pausing before the press unless you need to reset.

Hold a weight in each hand at the rack or by the sides. Pull the weight close with the legs and hips, catch it as the name describes, then stand or finish overhead. Keep the weight close to the body and avoid pulling with the arms alone.

Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

Drive the hips, clean the bell to the front rack, then press it overhead after the body is still. Lower to the rack with control and avoid letting the clean crash onto the forearm.

Guide the bell into the front rack with wrist straight and elbow close. Avoid letting the bell slap the forearm or pulling it in a wide arc.

ff_alternating_single_arm_kettlebell_sta.description: Stand at the landmine with one hand on the sleeve. Clean the sleeve to the shoulder, then dip and split the feet as you press overhead. Land with both knees soft and avoid pressing before the leg drive finishes.

Start with the kettlebell between the feet and hinge to grip it. Drive the hips, clean the bell to the front rack, then press it overhead after the body is still. Lower to the rack with control and avoid letting the clean crash onto the forearm.
